user_comment: I ordered an 18 Short (28 inseam by online description) the sticker says 18S W34 L30 How do I get a short inseam jean? I am 5’2”. The jeans are great otherwise It seemed that every Jean I grabbed to try on said L30. Now I’m wondering if the specs sent overseas were not followed correctly. I’ll try regular Levi’s next.

I ordered an 18 short, but I probably could have gotten away with just a 16. They flares are rather large, but not quite "bell bottoms". I have a 29 inch inseam, and these were SO LONG when I tried them on before washing them. After I washed them, they fit me exactly like every other pair or women's "short" or "petite" pants fit. They're high waisted, (but not in a trendy way, like, in a police officer's pants way. But, I did get them for work, so that's fine. Warning: the color bleeds badly on the first wash. You'll want to wash them alone or with a color catcher. At least for the first wash.

I ordered an 8 short, the in waist tag read 29x30. The inseam actually measures 33+ inches. I’m 5’ 2”. These are mislabeled, way too long! Hoping the next pair will be the right length. Waist and hips fit perfect

i ordered an 8S and these pants cover my whole foot. i showed my right foot with the pants cuffed to show how much i would need taken off in order to not trip and die lol. for reference, i am 5’2 and roughly 145 lbs. the reason i bought these pants is because im a waitress and i would say other than the fact of it being too long on me they’re perfect. they’re stretchy, comfortable, and have a decent thick amount of material on them that they should not rip once so ever. they’re also super flattering! but just be warned that even though you’re buying a petite size it might not fit!

I ordered an S (smallest option) in light khaki. The color is spot on. The fit, unsurprisingly, is not suitable for my stature (5’1”).The legs are baggy throughout. Length, regardless how high or low I wear these trousers, are longer than I’d like. I may get these hemmed or not since I did not find them flattering especially the back view. Unless you aim for this look, go for it.Anyhow, the fabric is a medium weight. It’s versatile for year-round. The style also allows for a dressed-up or casual look. At 30 bucks, these pants are a lovely choice.

I ordered an XS Medium Indigo style Levi’s shortalls. I’m 5 feet and weigh 115lbs. I saw most reviews here that this shortalls look good on other women. I decided to order one for myself. I never owned a pair shortalls before. I tried them on. They’re comfortable. It has 5 pockets (2 back pockets, 2 on the sides and by chest area). The top part (the adjustable straps and the waistline) are a good fit for me. However, the shorts itself (both leg openings) were baggy for my taste. They make my butt look flat. Too bad, I can’t ask for a refund. They only have a return or exchange only policy. Since I cannot ask for a refund, I had to salvage this shortalls by folding the hemline twice and secure them with safety pins. So I can sew the folds in place. To make the leg openings somewhat cute and tolerable for me to wear. Hope this helps.

I ordered an XS. I am curvy (curvy not fat) 27 waist 37-38.5 ‘hips’ (arse) and these pants are perfect! The only pants I have right now that fit without alterations are leggings and Allegra line stretch waist pants. The waist holds but doesn’t squeeze and I actually do have maybe half inch gap when sitting. The only issue is the pockets work themselves inside out if empty so I might make a small stitch on the front to keep them in place I wish they had them in different materials but these will be great for winter or indoors in AC

I ordered an XXL because they were out of the XL in black, so I thought they would need to be taken in, but they don’t! They definitely do run a little small considering an XXL fit me (usually a large or XL does well for me). They are very long and will need to be hemmed, but that was to be expected since I’m 4’11. Will be wearing these to see Harry Styles in a couple of weeks! They are very comfy and silk legging-like material :) Very pleased with my order!!

I ordered beige and these were much warmer than the cool beige advertised. Also the outline of the pockets was very visble from the front of the pants. Might be less so in a darker color. The medium was too big for me, sat lower on my hips rather than waist. I have bigger hips & thighs, smaller waist, 5' 4" and I'm returning for a small in a different color.

I ordered black and got blue. However, it is so warm and cosy i can work wearing that pants all day. The weather now is 40 F 15 wind and this pants keeps my legs warm. Im in love with this pants, i would be happier if it was black tho
size_display: largestyle: ultra soft fleece lined denim leggings

user_comment: I ordered black in these overalls because I fully intend to work in my garden wearing them and thought black would hide stains much better than the lighter colors. What I didn’t expect was to love these overalls as much as I do. They are so cute I can work in them or wear them out. The jean material is thick and they feel and look well made. I am 5’7 and weigh 147 pounds for reference, and I ordered the medium. In my opinion, they are true to size. The legs have a little bit tighter of a fit at the ankle then regular work overalls. The Black in particular, is a darker black and not so much a faded black. I’m super happy with these overalls and can’t recommend them enough. I’d like to have the orange color as well.

I ordered black, I got blue. They’re still pretty cute but I hate the thin band inside the waistband. It just ruins the entire waistband. They also ride up with movement, shorter inseam than stated, until they get a bit damp from sweat. Speaking of sweat, these show it big time. There’s no hiding from it. These have potential but there’s too much no for me. I’m 29 waist, 38 hips and I ordered a medium.

I ordered both a large and an extra large through Prime Wardrobe because I wasn't certain about the fit. I'm glad I did. The only difference between the two sizes was that the large was a little more snug across the chest, and tighter in the armpits. I opted to keep the extra large because if I wear a top with any kind of bulk to it (like a sweater) the large isn't going to fit and it was already a bit more snug than I would like. Just for reference, I'm 5'6", wear a 36/38C (depending on the bra), and usually wear a large in most tops. The first photo is of the large jacket and the second is of the extra large.

I ordered both medium and large as I wanted a loose fit. I’ll be returning the large. Not much difference except longer crotch/inseam. I hoped these would be cropped like the pic and the FAQ said even for the short influencer they referenced. They are not on me. But I’m still liking them a lot. Would purchase again. I have a 27 inch inseam as reference.

I ordered both the dark and light. The darker ones I have on in the pic.....PERFECT! I’m 5’8 and 188 lbs. I have the dreaded cellulite and these shorts cover it all perfectly but aren’t so long that it makes you look “older” if that makes sense lol. Love them. The light pair was more snug and I didn’t care for the color. It’s lighter than pictured so I’m sending those back.

i ordered Cody color and it is much more blue than the advertised faded/washed out denim i was hoping for. even though the color is different than i was hoping for— still looks great with white or black. very cute look, will be keeping. i’m 5 foot 108 lbs and size small is a little too big but i guess still does the oversized overall look. very stretchy— we shall see how it does in the wash. really hoping this blue doesn’t fade horribly but i will be leaving a review after a few washes.

I ordered five dresses in three different colors and two different sizes. For a reference I am 5’ 3”, 125 pounds on the fluffier side since I’m in my 40s. First I bought two white dresses one small, one medium. The medium is a super soft material and runs big with NO pockets. So I ordered a small which is it different rougher material with almost no stretch and no give and has pockets. The small fit me find everywhere but around the arms it was a little tight when reaching or grabbing for something. The denim blue dress which I ordered in a small and medium were almost identical in size length and material. The medium was about a quarter of an inch wider than the small and that was the only difference that I found. So both ran super big on me. Lastly, I ordered the black denim dress. And it’s a small. It fit me perfectly! So I am sending both denim dresses back and trying to decide what to do about the white dress as I love the softness of one but it is too big.

I ordered it oversized, large, I’m 5’7 and 145lbs and loved the way it fit, soft denim and very stylish . I am 59 years old and I guess this jacket made me look young and hip because I had to show my DL on a recent girls trip. lol

I ordered Large because size chart says it's for 28-inch waists. The waistband of Large stretches from 13-inches to 19-inches (14 to 20 once worn). Large will thus fit anyone with a 28-inch waist (relaxed) all the way up to a 40-inch waist (fully stretched). Technically, you want to be on the relaxed side, and I would say 28 to 34-inches is fine for Large. I have 40-inch hips and Large fit fine despite size chart saying it's for 33-inch hips. The material is very stretchy. I posted some pictures showing that white is very see-through, and if you stretch these enough, you will even see camel toe silhouette as the material is very light and thing. and I recommend wearing a white slip-short underneath. I'm 5'6 and the pants are too long, and I'd have to put on some really high heels so they don't touch the ground. A 31.5-inch inseam, for high-waisted pants, should fit fine on my low-waisted 29-inch inseam, but these are too long.

I ordered multiple brands of overalls to try on because it's hard to find the right fit! These were by far my favorite. The quality is great and I felt comfortable wearing them all night. The quality and fit held up after being in the washer/dryer as well. I am 5'2 and found the length to be perfect. I tightened the straps quite a bit so I think the fit would be great for taller women as well with the straps a bit looser.

I ordered my regular size 4. I have a 27" waist and am only 5 feet tall. Order your regular size. I like these jeans and they will get a lot of use. These are long on me but length is likely good for others. See photos, I purposely showed the additional length. There is a small amount of fashionable "wear" markings along the pockets and hem. These jeans are soft, have good stretch and are comfortable to wear. Waist is good, without bad puckering in the small of my back. 98% cotton, 2% spandex. Made in Pakistan. Recommend.

I ordered my regular size and shockingly these fit! At first I thought they looked too small to fit me, but they actually fit well and we're not tight due to the moderate stretch in the fabric. The weight of the denim is much thicker and heavier than I like, but the jeans are actually pretty comfortable nonetheless. The length was too long at first, but after one wash in cold water and only partially tumble drying them, they did shrink to a perfect length! The waist still fit perfectly which is often my trouble spot of not being generous enough. The online size guide for my "regular" size even listed the waist measurement as several inches less than what is typical, but I ordered my "regular" size anyway and this pair of jeans fit very comfortably because of the generous stretch. I do not like tight clothing. I would go so far as to say those gals who are more petite or slimmer, should consider sizing down! I have a thick waist (5' 4" & 154 lbs) and my regular size (12) fit me very comfortably. This style is kind of cute for an occasional mix-up/change. If you are on the fence, I'd say give them a try. They seem very well made! Mine were sewn perfectly and no flaws in the denim. They washed, partially tumbled, and hung to finish drying great! I only experienced shrinkage in length.

I ordered my usual size an XL (5’4 190lbs) I have a small waist and larger butt/thighs, often times with pants I have a gap in the back of my waist… these pants DID NOT DISAPPOINT! Like others have said they are a little noisy like that swishy sound but besides that these are super comfortable, stretchy, they hug my waist perfectly! I could have maybe used another half inch or so on the bottom but I was honestly surprised they were as nice as they are! I’ll def be wearing these again and again!

I ordered my usual size. I should have gone down a size but I do like the cut. These jeans have quite a bit of stretch. I wore them around the house for an hour and they did loosen up with wear unfortunately. What I like best though, is how the waist band is cut. It cuves in the back so it sits flat on the waist, no gap. The sides curve up a bit so there is no muffin top (can see in the pic) and the front sits about 1 inch below the belly button. I am 5'3 and ordered the short length which is listed as a 31in. inseam. They are how I like but some might still find the short too long for them. To wear with tennis shoes I will have to cuff up the back or they will drag but should be perfect with a platform. Update: I decided to return as they were just too baggy. I tried the Elyse in the same cut in a size 20, too big, then a size 18, too big. I switched to the infinity cut and those fit how I wanted.

I ordered petite and they are WAY too long. I'm 5'2 ordered 29' inseam and they are longer than long would be. The tag on the pants doesn't even say petite. I have to hem them now cause it's not worth the hastle of sending them back. For the price, these are so not worth it. Lousy quality, worst than Walmart. Not sure how these have such good reviews. They are poorly made and should be half the price charged. Total rip off.

I ordered size 10 because I own a pair of shorts by Lee that are size 10 that for really well, even though I'm usually a size 12. I'm 5'3" and around 140 lbs. I literally just tried them on and until I saw the pictures I really loved them 😂 that being said, they stay in place and aren't too baggy or tight for my taste, so I like them.

I ordered size 12 for reference. I normally wear size 30 waist jeans. These are so so cute! I can’t wait to wear them!

I ordered size 4 but received size 10 so I can’t really say how they actually fit because they super huge I’m 95lbs size 10 is not a fit for me. But from a quality perspective they seem ok. I’m a bit disappointed because I think the cut should be nice. I can’t really tell if they’re high waisted or not from the wrong size.

I ordered size 4 regular in black. They're more like a 10 than a 4. I usually wear a 7 in Juniors jeans for reference. I weigh 129lbs, my hips are 40", waist 25" and they were pretty loose and baggy. Which could be great if that's the look you're going for. The material is nice and thick and they have back pockets.
I ordered size X/XL. I am 5'2 tall, weight 130 lbs. It fit me loosely perfect. Very soft and comfortable pants. I wear it everyday at home. Hiking, jogging, running.

I ordered size XL in Navy blue. I am 5'10" and the length is decent. The material is very light weight and see-through, was hoping to wear them to work but with them see-through I am not so sure unless I wear a long enough shirt to cover the butt area, so I feel buying pants locally would be better as it's hit-n-miss with ordering clothes online that you can't see in person until they arrive, hence the 2-star rating on these.
I ordered small/medium for my 28" waist. It's perfect. It holds you in and can stretch to accommodate a larger waistline. Well made! Does not look or feel cheap. I got the black and white combo pack.

I ordered Style4 in Blue, size Large I am 5’5” and approximately 170#. My inseam is approximately 32”. I have a rectangular figure with a thicker waist and not a lot of hips. I usually wear a US women’s size 10 or medium to large. I ordered these in size large after reading reviews and deciding not to size up. They fit comfortably. The waistband is a little snug for my liking, but not problematically tight. They fit right above the navel and just below the ankle on me. The crotch gusset hangs down just about 2.5” when pulled up to my waist. The quality seems very nice: good pockets, sturdy, and well made. I live in a hot climate and wear a lot of light linen clothing in the summer. The fabric is a bit heavier than I was expecting. That’s not a complaint; I still like the pants, but it may be something to take into consideration if you order them for summer wear. I have not laundered them yet, so I can’t say whether they’ll bleed or shrink. I have uploaded a close-up photo of the fabric for your reference.

I ordered the “Blue” color version of these jeans in size 12 as recommended. I’m 5’2 & as you can see in my photos, the crotch area is baggy/big, as well as it’s a bit too tall for me. The photo in the advertisement is a much softer blue toned than what I received. The blue denim I received is not only darker but the acid wash effect is also harsh. I think the seller may have sent me the “N Blue” pants because the blue denim advertised, doesn’t have the acid wash design in the back. The placement & harshness of the acid design looks terrible as if someone diy’d it. The only positive thing I can say is that the denim material itself is soft which is comfortable for wear. Overall I’m not sure if this was false advertising or a clerical error of being sent the wrong item but I’ll be returning these pants. I’ve lost faith in opting for an exchange because who’s to say any of the jeans are the correct style/color of what’s advertised here. Not worth $40 gamble.

I ordered the “Capri-Dark Green” but pretty sure this matches the “Capri-Green” selection. I am 5’9” and 190. The XL fits fine. I don’t understand the too short comments. These rest about mid calf for me and that is what I expected. Just wish they were more forest green than army green.

I ordered the 10 short, I am 5’4” and 157 lbs. These jeans do not have the stretch or elasticity that most pull on jeans have. Nor are they the same quality material of the R&R denim that I purchased from Kohl’s over a year ago. The waistband doesn’t stay put like the original pair I bought either. They are thin and I don’t expect that they will last long. I’m keeping them because it’s difficult to find short length jeans. But won’t purchase again for this price!!

I ordered the 16 Plus, they came in and were a little on the snug side, but still fine. However, I am 5'8 and they were about 2 inches too long for me. In a 3 inch heel they were just above the ground. Normally my boot cuts with a little bit of clearance with shoes that are not that high. I am returning them. Took a pic of the tag I am guessing that the say the inseam is a 33? Well I would guess 34 min. (no information on the length in the description) The material was a bit thinner than I was expecting for Levi's but had a nice slight stretch, If it wasn't for the length I probably would of kept these.

I ordered the 31w x 29L what I received is 12/31A! How is a 31 length considered ankle? It is also not 29 inches in length as advertised. These pants happen to fit me around the waist but they are very long. I'm 5'1 I specifically buy my jeans short. I'm not sure if a size 12 is equal to a 31w. Each brand seems to vary. I don't know that these are real Lucky brand Jeans they are soft and comfortable Jeans and if you are ok with the length and size then I would say get them. Especially if they are on sale for $29.00 you can always return them if they don't work out.

I ordered the 32” inseam. I’m 5’8” and these are stupid short on me. I ordered both M and L, they are both only 30” I ordered these solely for the longer inseam, very disappointed and will be returning. Not for tall ladies despite advertising a 32” inseam.

I ordered the 447-medium Denim Blue in size 4. I am only 5 feet tall and have a 27" waist. Pants are long on me and need hemming but that is to be expected and normal. The jeans are a loose fit, especially at waist. As described a baggy high waisted jean with wide straight legs. They are very comfortable. Nothing is tight or pulls in a bad way. Material is a softer jean with distressed marks along back and front pockets. I noticed on my figure the back waist rides up higher than slopes down on the front. I like the jeans and believe they will get lots of use. Price is good for the quality.

I ordered the 5x. I’m 5”8, 250lbs, waist 47.5, thighs 27, glutes 50, inseam 30. I say this because the pants fit me like a comfortable glove, l was able to tighten the draw string some with a small amount of wiggle room. I hope this is for everyone choosing their respective sizes. I love them!😄

I ordered the 711 12L W31/L32 and they are perfect! Since they are not available in blue, in my size, I ordered the 311 Shaping Skinny in blue. Same size as the black, 12 L W31/L32. The 311s are nearly 3 inches shorter than the 711! How is this possible? 32 L is 32 L, right? What am I missing? Maybe they were mismarked? I am 5'11 so length is important. UPDATE I reordered the jeans in my size again and they are perfect! The original pair I received were definitely mismarked. The new ones fit so well and are so nice that I ordered a second pair. Fingers crossed they are marked/sized correctly. 🤞

I ordered the 9, or “9 regular” like it says on the tag. These jeans would be great but they are too short. I am 5’6 with long legs and a shorter torso and they barely reach my ankle bone. If these came in long they would be perfect. They’re also not tight enough around the ankle. I like my skinny jeans SKINNY.

I ordered the black 6M. I’m 5’ 5” and 145-150lbs and 6 months post-second-baby. I’m currently wearing a size 8 or 10 jean. But for some crazy reason I ordered a 6 in these. *face palm* These were way too small for me. Had I ordered my real size I probably would be wearing them right now instead of returning them. So they are made to fit tight and suck you in - almost like compression leggings around the waist. So if you order the right size, these are probably a dream. I didn’t get photos of the ankles, but they are slim, but not TIGHT around the ankles. The material is stretchy but thick. They don’t have the feel or look of leggings. I thought they felt and looked more like black khakis instead of denim. I would say more about the rest of the fit, but it was all pretty bad because of my poor ordering. If I reorder in my size, I’ll update the review.

I ordered the Black D pants, straight legs cut, in black, size M. I'm 5'7 and 134 lbs and it's a perfect fit! 🤩👍 After trying the same kind of pants that were more like constrictive (second-skin tight) jeggings rather than real pants, it's a great feel! These are more flattering. I like the jean style, the pockets and the zipper (tried other faux leather pants with only button fermeture and didn't like it) I read in other comments that the material is noisy and I have to agree with that, BUT nothing that a little mineral oil rub cannot fix. Besides, I am sure it won't be so obvious at a party. I also appreciate the material looks, not latex shine looking. I chose the straight legs cut for it's versatility; it can be worn with high heels as well as tucked in boots without the bulk. I didn't notice any smell. A little pricey in my opinion for faux leather (even with a discount) but I had been wanting those pants for a long while so... 🤷🏽‍♀️ I finally gave in, and I don't regret it! 😁 Also, sorry the photos are not the best quality, I need a better phone!

I ordered the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL. To start, these are not high waisted pants. They are hip huggers, meaning they sit low on your waist and will not restrict your tummy if you have a few extra pounds. Furthermore, they state they jeans are stretch. They have a slight stretch to them, but they are not what is normally considered a stretch pant. Finally, these are not baggy on the average girl. They are boot cut/flare leg, but not baggy.With all of that said, I am 5 foot, 5 inches tall, 275 pounds, wear an 18/20 USA size and a 2x/3x top. My waist measurement at belly button level is 46 inches. My upper thighs and calves are thicker than most. I was concerned these pants would not fit, however, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL fit comfortably. I will say there is a bit of tightness in the knee area, but I tend to have this issue due to my thick legs. I am not use to a hip hugger pant, so was genuinely surprised that these pants fit tight, but comfortably, as they sit under my "jelly roll", so there is none of that constrictive feeling on your stomach. I also like my pants longer and I found these to fit perfectly, touching the ground, giving them room to shrink a bit.Overall, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown in Size XXL are well made, the denim is thicker than most and I absolutely love the color. The fit is surprisingly good and the hip hugger style makes them extremely comfortable. So if you are a curvy girl like me, check these out. Five stars.

I ordered the S/M and the L since I'm between a M/L in leggings and don't like a waistband that's too binding. Oddly, when I opened the packaging, they looked exactly the same! I placed one on top of the other, and the rise, inseam, waist, hip, and ankle are identical (see photos of them stacked one on top of the other). I'm pretty sure these are one-size leggings that they've slapped two sizing tags onto because "one-size leggings" sound too good to be true. That said, the L fits me (31" waist, 40" hip) and I had my XXS daughter try on the S/M and they fit her 22" waist without gapping, so there's some magical stretchiness happening here. Length-wise, we're both 5'2" so there's a bit of extra length at the ankle but we can hike them up so it doesn't pool. Pros: - Sturdy exterior that looks like it will hold up to washing, soft fleecy interior (this must be the "velvet" they're referring to as they're NOT velvet on the outside, a bit like a teddy bear on the inside though), very comfy - Not so thick that it makes my legs look bulky, though there's a bit of puffy folding behind my knees and under the bum - Wore outside for an hour at 36F and I can feel the breeze through them but stayed warm - The waist band stays up; no muffin top Cons: - The front and back are identical, so there's not much room for a booty—i.e. the back side takes some coaxing to come up to mid-rise on me, though the front is easily high rise. No issue riding down throughout the day though. - They're oddly jogger-like at the ankle. More pronounced on my daughter since she has skinnier ankles. I tucked the extra fabric into my boot socks, but it's a little odd-looking with exposed ankles. Great for the $13 sale price and we're keeping both pairs.

I ordered the short 26" inseam in small and they measure 25" in reality making these too short unless I wear them lower, or below the high waist. This makes them fit oddly on my body. The right leg inseam also veers toward the front too much, more than the left leg. I am only 5' and a size 4. The material is a soft 4 way stretch jean, making these good to move and work in. 61% Cotton, 34% Polyester, 3% Rayon, 2% Elastane. The pockets are deep. I am able to fit my entire hand in to the wrist. Because the length is off an inch it makes them look like I'm waiting for a flood and for the crooked inseam on right leg I deducted stars.

I ordered the short size 12 and they are good but not the ideal jegging for me but still worth keeping. I have taken two photos with two different shirts and you can see that with the loose shirt, it camouflages my waist area. With the tighter shirt, it created an unflattering fit to see my fat bulging from the tight waistband. Ideally a looser band would be preferred, but I have a difficult body to fit jeans and pants since I am 5’3” and I have more of a “gut” than butt. So to get pants to fit in the butt, I have to order a size down but then it creates a tight waist and sometimes a muffin top and this is exactly what these jeggings did for me. But other than that, the length is great, the material nice and stretchy while still looking like jeans, they are comfy, and the waist band is still comfortable, despite the muffin top effect. I think I’m going to keep and just wear with looser shirts.

I ordered the size small. I am 5”3 and about 150. They are a little snug around my stomach but everywhere else is perfect. These are super cute! I got them on sale for 31$. However, it was not prime shipping so my jeans took a week to arrive. Still worth it. I am waiting for them to go on sale again to buy more pairs!

I ordered the size specified in the manufacturer's images. This was the same size Amazon recommended based on my purchasing history. They fit very comfortably. They are not at all as "barrel shaped" as would seem from the image with the model. The crotch is lower than jeans would be but not awkward, not ill-fitting. I am 5' 7", about 125-130 lbs., and this is the size M. They are a bit shorter than I prefer my pants to be, but I think they will be fine with booties, and I think they look great rolled up. I have no "caboose" and the pants are comfortable on me; if you are *very* curvy in your derriere, these might not suit you, but they'd be worth a try. They are so comfortable and soft. If I was told they were cotton, I would believe it. They're not see-through or flimsy-feeling. I like that the elastic back waist can be hidden with a belt. For pleated corduroys (with which one runs the risk of looking larger than one is) I think the fit is actually flattering. I think the price is slightly high for fabric that is not composed of natural materials (hence four stars instead of five), and the model photo is misleading, but I'm very, very pleased with these and they will get a lot of use this fall. They are great going-out-for-coffee or to-the-pub trousers. Looking forward to pairing them with a vintage, close-fitting sweater, a tweed blazer, and some Born booties. I hope this review was to you. :)

I ordered the The Drop Women's Standard Nancy Pleat Front Wide Leg Trouser to wear to work. I love the navy color with the white pinstripes. These are a nice trouser to wear to the office. The material is heavyweight and great for cold weather. The zipper and closures all work well without any defects. The front pockets are a good size, while the back pockets are just for the look. The legs are really wide and the length was good for my 5'7" height as they did not drag on the ground. The only issue I had with these pants is the waist seems to run a bit small. I normally wear an XL and these were slightly uncomfortable to wear to work. There is some elastic at the back, but I intend to move the clasp a bit to make them more comfortable. If you are between sizes, I recommend sizing up. They are pricey when compared to similar products as I am not familiar with this brand.

I ordered the Womens Sexy High Waisted Flare Pants Boho Printed Stretchy Bell Bottom Leggings. I wasn't sure what to expect when I ordered these, but they are not really as expected. I ordered the size small, but technically I needed an extra small which they did not have. So, going into it I felt they were going to be a little bit big which they absolutely were and almost so much where I won't be able to wear them out as planned with a cute top. However, that is my own fault for ordering a size larger and taking a gamble. They are very soft, but they are incredibly thin. They are not as thin as leggings but definitely not thick at all like they appear in the pictures. Because they are so big on me, I can't really get an idea of what they would look like if they had a slim fit. I'm not even sure if an extra small would give the slim fit, actually, like the photos. I am 5'4, and yes they are long which I could tell, and if I just pull them up really high they are fine. I can also flip the waistband over, but that changes the entire look to be even worse than they were before so I won't do that. I noticed the stripes are not the same as the pictures. Right down the middle and towards the Lady area, there are white stripes, and it actually gives the illusion that there is a triangle shape down there, and it's actually pretty funny and not appealing. If they were dark like the pictures, then that would be something different. Even though I wouldn't wear these out because of all these situations, they are incredibly comfortable and feel good to wear around the house which is what I will use them for so they are not getting wasted, and they are more comfortable than most house pants that I have, so that is a plus, thank you.

I ordered the XXL and was sent 4XL. But I'm glad, because after trying them on, I don't think the XXL would have fit. The 4XL clings but isn't tight which is what I wanted. Upon going back to the product images, I'm kind of wondering how those size models fit into these? I'm 5'3'' 195lbs and I have a pear shape. Measurements hips: 48.9 inches. Waist: 40.9 inches. While taking a picture for review, I noticed a rip on a seam near the hip.. It seems the fabric is doubled so it's not actually a hole. I think I'll just fabric glue it shut and call it a day. UPDATE: I've had these leggings for several months now and they're my go to because of the pocket, high waist and comfortable fit. I just wore them to my trip to NYC and it was wild. While trying to navigate to my seat on the bus as it's moving and being tossed around; the stuff in my pocket got seriously caught on the seat handles not once, not twice, but THREE times and it yanked SO hard I swore my pants were done for! To my surprise I look down and they look absolutely untouched! Still completely in tact! That day I swore I had to update my review and express how sturdy and good quality these are! For that price, what a steal. I'm so happy with these! Get them!! I just ordered 2 more pairs. :D

I ordered these & LOVE THEM! My first pair of pants like this! I ordered 2 more colors as well! They are dressy casual which is what I’m looking for for the warmer months. I’m 5’7 & I ordered a medium & that fit great- not too tight or too big!

I ordered these ANRABESS Women Wide Leg Baggy Cargo Sweatpants in a size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she loves the way they fit! For reference, she's 5' tall and 105lbs-ish with a 26" waist. Though these pants are a little long on her, she insists that is fashion... and I'm here for it! Here's what we like about these sweatpants: •QUALITY GARMENT: These sweats are well made. The stitching is straight and secure. They feel more like a higher end pair of sweatpants than fast fashion. •SOFT: These sweatpants are made of very soft, cozy material. While they are warm, they aren't extremely thick or heavily lined with fleece. •FASHION FORWARD: The actual cut of these pants is really nice! They are very baggy in the legs, specifically at the knees. But they taper up at the waist, giving them a really good shape. My daughter loves the cargo pockets on the sides. •WASH & WEAR! These pants can be washed, dried, and worn dozens of times without compromising the quality. Mom win! Overall, my daughter is just obsessed with these! She likes to wear them with tank tops and a bunch of accessories (not shown in the picture, but she was just lounging). She also wears them with graphic tees and athletic shoes. I just like that they are affordable, comfortable ad made well. We highly recommend them!

I ordered these as a loose-fitting pair of jeans so I could wear to work comfortably. They fit the bill. They’re nice and loose in the thighs. The waist is a bit too big, but I usually wear a 0-2 so I expected this, I just need to wear a belt. Otherwise these are very comfy and I like the fit. They’re definitely a denim material, not a jegging- which I like. The rise comes to a little under my navel. They hold their fit throughout the day and don’t loosen up. I’m only 5’3 and they’re still a little long, even with the short length but not too bad. Overall, great for the price.

I ordered these as I needed more warm clothing for the weather change. I’m 5’6 and my measurements are 37-31-44. The large fit. I love how warm they are. The fleece material inside is soft and not itchy. The pockets are perfect and deep enough to hold a cell phone and car keys. The product is advertised as high waisted, but they did not fit high waisted on me. The material itself is thick and of good quality, so they will keep you warm. My only complaint is the price as this is kind of pricey for basic sweatpants. When I say basic, I mean appearance and design. My other issue is that the pants shed a lot on the inside. Hopefully after washing them a few times, the lint will stop coming off on the inside. Overall, I’m happy with the item.
I ordered these as part of an alien Halloween costume and I was super impressed with the quality and look! Fit is as expected but a little short. I am 5'5" and 120 lbs and ordered a small.

I ordered these as work pants because I wanted something not as form-fitting as my leggings, but still stretchy and soft. I always get a little nervous with different pants because I have sensitive skin (might be a sensory thing idk) and some material feels like little pinches on my legs all day and is unbearable. Anyway, while I do get the little pinchy sensation here and there, it's very tolerable and I love these pants! They're stretchy, soft, easy to walk in, and I love how they're fitted from the top and down my legs but loose at the ankle! I'm between a 14/16 and ordered XL and they're totally perfect. I will say though, I'm 5'6" and they stop slightly before my ankle bone, which I like, but thought it was worth mentioning. I'm ordering another pair but in navy! I HIGHLY recommend, even for the girlies with sensory issues😉

I ordered these because I needed a black work pant and I am so glad I did! I am between a 31 and a 30 size wise, and ordered the 31. They fit PERFECTLY but there is no waistband stretch so if you are comfortably a size 31, then order a size 32. I loved the fit, the cut, and the pant length. Also, it goes up right to my belly button, so this is ideal for a business appropriate pant and can accommodate tucking in a blouse. See (unflattering) pictures for front and side perspectives. These hit right at my ankles (for reference, I am 5'8) so I didn't feel like they were high waters like the other pair of dockers I've owned. Material is soft and smooth. 98% cotton, 2% elastane based on the tag. Value on these? Awesome! They retail for $58 and the price was $39 on Amazon.

I ordered these because I’ve got about 10 pairs of Libin’s cargo joggers, which I love, and these palazzos looked fun. (For reference, I’m 5’2”, 36 inches at the waist, 41” bum). After trying them on here’s my impression: PROS: 1. 100% polyester, so they won’t shrink. The outside is a textured waffle weave that is 100% breathable, but the inside is smooth and soft. 2. SIzing is Spot on. I went by both my usual Libin size (XL) and their sizing chart (36 in waist), and super happy I did. These don’t pinch at the waist, but the top part of the pants aren’t excessively baggy. 3. Versatile and cute. I was just going to try them on this morning, thinking that they’d be great for church, but then I ended up wearing them on today’s errands. Considerations: 1. These are Palazzo pants, meaning there’s a lot of material in the leg. If you’re like me (a bit chunky, significant bum) you’ll want to wear these with a fitted top, to maintain a feminine silhouette. 2. The 27 inch inseam just works on my 5”2” frame (but I have a short waist and long legs for my height). Shoes keep them just off the ground. Anyway, I’m a fan.

I ordered these because of the great reviews but honestly planned to have to return them. I am 50 years old (5'5" and 150 lbs) and figured they'd only look great on a younger woman. I love the style, fit, comfort, and material. They are not see- through. Giving four stars because I feel they could have better tummy control. They are a perfect rise, but provide no control.

I ordered these because they are listed as longer length. Even the reviews said they were. I measured them after I tried the high-waters on. See my photos. I am 5'9" 140lb. Size 7 fit perfect for my waist and thighs. However, it is terribly misleading to call these long length. They were a waste of time. :-(

Size 7 fit perfect for my waist and thighs. However, it is terribly misleading to call these long length. They were a waste of time. :-(

I ordered these because they were the only burnt orange color I could find to match my fall sweatshirt, but it matches perfectly! They are warm and very soft and you can't see through them. The only downsides are the price, they are a little long (I'm 5'3") and looser around the ankles then I like and the piling of fabric after only one wear.

I ordered these because they're my favorite color, purpley-pink-orchid. They're fantastic. Lightweight. The wrinkles steamed out easily. Big pockets ftw! I'll probably order another color too, I like them so much! I ordered XL according to the size chart. For reference I'm about size 10/12, 5'8", 175lb, 38B.

I ordered these black stretchy work pants in 14short since I'm 5'4" so I tend to need pants on the shorter side. Even though I ordered the short version, I still find I have to roll the cuff of the pants under once to give me a clean look, otherwise the pants look baggy on me- I included a photo with one pant leg cuffed and one pant leg as is. My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

I ordered these capris in white and in “Sedona,” and they fit differently. First, I currently weigh 160 lbs and waist and hip dimensions are 34” and 40” respectively. I am wearing a size 14 in all photos. The “Sedona” denim had bizarre extra fabric in the rise as well as the hip areas. See how the rise puckers up into extra layers beneath my waist in the photos showing me sitting down? SO not flattering! The extra hip fabric is visible in the photos of me standing. (BTW, same shade of capris, but sitting down pics had a lot of natural light and standing photos had barely any natural light.) Anyway, maybe I just have funky measurements, but my “Sedona” capris puckered out on both sides of the hip region. I now seem to recall having the same issue with Lee’s Easy Rider jeans. For whatever reason, I don’t see the same extra fabric in the white capris; those are keepers. Hope this review and photos are to someone!

I ordered these Dokotoo Women’s Casual Mid Waist cargo jeans for my daughter and she loved it. Cargo jeans are sewn with high quality and neatly. The seams are even throughout and the threads do not stick out anywhere. The inner seams are carefully finished. The fabric from which the jeans are sewn stretches well and is pleasant to the touch. Cargo jeans look good and complement the look nicely. Cargo jeans have a beautiful gray color. I ordered a size 12 for my daughter and it fit well. My daughter was deciding between 10 and 12 and chose 12 because she doesn't like jeans that are too tight. The daughter is 5.3" tall and weighs 140 lbs. Her hips are 41.5 inches and her waist is 32 inches. There is a size chart in the description, which is very convenient. The cargo jeans are a bit long for her height, but this can be easily fixed with iron-on adhesive tape.

I ordered these dressy looking trousers for my granddaughter who I figured would appreciate a nice pair of trousers or pants designed for a taller frame such as she has. It looks like Tall MobPlace did a nice job with these. She notes "The polyester fabric should be durable and easy to care for, unfortunately, the white pants are rather see-through and you can very clearly see where the pockets were put in. The material looks classy and the fit is good. They are very comfortable for fit and easy to get on. They hug the waist and hips and they’re definitely very long and probably will be worn with heels - otherwise they will need to be hemmed up shorter." We considered that since she is an excellent seamstress and could quite easily do that, however the following day she was able to wear them with some 3" heels which she has. I hadn't anticipated that at 5'11" she would have those in her closet but I was obviously out of the loop. It looks like the 34" inseam will be about right with 3” heels. If we had ordered the 32” inseam they would be worn with short heels or flats to fit her correctly. As her grandfather I suggested: "I guess you can just wear the heels and look for tall guys that match up to your higher views and expectations in life."When ordering: Just realize that when they list as a 34" length, that is what they will be. The trousers in all other respects fit and feel real well.

I ordered these for my 19 yr old who’s slim and tall - approx 5’8.5.” I was initially hesitant because the fiber content for these pants is 95% polyester. However, like a lot of other teens, she also wears oversized hoodies and sweatpants in our 80 plus degree weather. Her waist is about 28,” but per the brand’s size guide, I ordered these in XL - the largest size available. Not only is the material surprisingly light, they also fit her well. They’re a smidge loose in the waist, which is what she prefers and also makes for a comfier fit. We’re both super happy the length is perfect for her long legs. (That’s her modeling in the attached image) The quality seems great - she’s worn these several times without any issues. Per other reviews, apparently these aren’t for everyone. (I know I’m most comfortable in natural fibers). However, my daughter’s very happy and although I received these through Amazon Vine, at under $20, I’m definitely purchasing a backup pair for her to take back to college. The brand, Yoawdats, has a store here on Amazon with some other cute items she might like. Overall I do recommend these pants, but strongly suggest following the brand’s size guide.

I ordered these for my daughter who is 5'1 and they are perfect. She wears them to run around in, as they don't drag the ground, but they are also comfortable enough to just lounge. She is a size 10 and I ordered a L, which is her normal size. They fit her perfectly. Overall, great find.

I ordered these for my daughter who looked them over (before ordering) and she approved. Which was a shocker to me. Teens and pre-teens are so hard to buy for. It's like a game, that I seldom win. We ordered them, and they came pretty quick. These are cotton jeans, which means they are going to shrink unless you hang them to dry. Which turns out is a good thing for us. I ordered a size 2 and should have gone with a 0. She is 5' and weighs about 94#. So heres to shrinking! The material is soft and feels as though they have been worn and washed several times. The color is good too. There won't be any of the breaking in, as the designer worked that into these jeans. She is happy with them, (gasp). She did say if the size would have been correct, she would have worn them straight out of the package, which is big for her. Without her throwing a fit, I am taking these jeans as a win, or as a recommendation.

I ordered these for my niece. She is 5 months pregnant. She said she loves these pants because they’re soft and comfortable. She has room for her belly to grow. She is 5’7” so I ordered the large/ tall. They fit perfectly!

I ordered these for my niece. She is 5 months pregnant. She said she loves these pants because they’re soft and comfortable. She has room for her belly to grow. She is 5’7” so I ordered the large/ tall. They fit perfectly!

I ordered these in 26 because I wanted a looser fit, they sent me the 25 and I'm glad because they run a bit big and the waist has a gap. I normally wear 24 or 25 in Levi's so I'm hoping a few good runs through the dryer will shrink them a little bit. Other than that, these jeans are freaking awesome!! The waist hits right at my belly button. They are a 26" inseam and are a "cropped" style, but I'm 4'11 and they fit me like regular pants which I expected. The bleached out spots are really subtle and give the jeans a nice worn in look. They have that classic 501 fit and make your butt look great!

I ordered these in 33x33. I'm 5'7.5" and weigh 150-155. They fit perfectly! They are just a touch long but I prefer my jeans to fit that way. I typically wear a size XL in Juniors or a size L in Women's. I hope this review helps. I plan to order another pair of these as they will definitely be a regular wear for me.

I typically wear a size XL in Juniors or a size L in Women's. I hope this review helps. I plan to order another pair of these as they will definitely be a regular wear for me.

I ordered these in a 27, I normally wear a 28 but they have a lot of stretch so I wanted them a little tighter. Sizing down was perfect for me, but does leave a little love handle action on the sides (I have a soft mom midsection) that would be noticeable with a cropped top. I ordered regular even though I’m 5’3 - so they are a little long but work perfect with a platform sneaker or heal. Love them!

I ordered these in a medium (Amazon suggested a small). I'm 5'4" and weigh around 140 lbs. They are fitted in the butt and slightly loose everywhere else. I could probably size down but I mainly ordered these to wear to work so didn't want them too tight. They have a ton of stretch and are super comfortable. They definitely can pass for casual slacks when paired with a nice top. I will definitely but more in other colors. Love these pants!

I ordered these in a size L/XL, and normally wear a size 10-12. It fit fine with no muffin top. However, the fabric is a silky feel, shiny, lightweight material on which the denim markings are printed. I knew about the printing from other reviews but did not expect the fabric to be so shiny in a denim print. The whiskering, fading and fly/pocket/contrast stitch printing all look so incredibly fake hah. It was also pretty short on my ankles (I normally wear an inseam 30 in skinny jeans and 31.5 in straight leg). Overall it looked kind of ridiculous on me. I still gave it 3 stars though because my expectations were different, but others may be looking for something exactly like this. I've uploaded a couple of pictures including closeups of the print.

I ordered these in a size large. I am 5'6" and about 180lbs. Normally I order my clothes XL and a little loose. This fit right and were not as baggy as my other pants.When I think of sweatpants, I think of the kind that are lined with fleece. I took a close-up picture of the fabric. It is light and net like not soft. Not uncomfortable, just different. It did wrinkle and I don't want to iron this material.They also don't go high waist, they hit just around the belly button. I kept fidgeting with the waistline

I ordered these in a size M and they are generally true to size. I'm 5'6", 28" waist, and a typical size 6/8 for reference. These looked like a very cute wide leg jean from the description pictures but the pocket inner lining can be seen from the front and it adds bulkiness around the top of the thigh where the pocket ends. Wish they would have made the pocket lining shorter and the pockets not so deep (you can see this in the picture). To be able to use these, I pushed the pocket liner up towards my waist and it isn't as apparent, but not perfect. These are a lightweight denim material, with a functioning drawstring waist and a wide leg opening. Overall, the jeans are truly high waisted, a good length, and a cute style!

I ordered these in my usual size 4 (27 waist) and while the high rise is really cute, the wide leg is too revealing for my 39 year old self. I feel like my cheeks are completely exposed. Normally a 3 inch inseam works well for my height and comfort level, but the leg holes flare out in a way that I can feel the air on my butt, and it's just not cute. Maybe for someone younger who wants everyone to see their goods, these would be a good option. I may wear them as a swim suit cover.

I ordered these in size small, which according to the tag is US size 0. I’m usually a size 2. These have an elastic waist band on the back so it fits me fine. My issue is the length. I’m 5’4” and these are short when worn with heels. It looks a little awkward. It looks better with flats or sneakers (90s style). Problem is I like wearing pants with heels instead of sneakers. They are cute, but not the best. I like the color I got. This taupe color can be be paired with many colors. I wish they were a little longer, they would have gotten 5 stars. I think they are a little pricey for the quality.

I ordered these in the “Blue” color in size Medium. I typically wear a size 8 in pants, but the size chart could have had me in a Large instead. However, the listing says the model is “wearing size Medium, Hips 40 / Height 5’3” / Weight 136. I’m about an inch and half taller, weigh about the same, and hips are maybe an inch smaller. The Medium fits me very well and is comfortable. The inseam is at least an inch shorter than the stated 26.38” (I measured it at about 25”). The fabric has a nice weight and is soft and stretchy. The waistband is beautiful and also comfortable. It seems like it would retain its size and shape, however, I have not worn them long enough to test this theory. There are no belt loops to hold them up should they stretch out, or if they would be too loose on you. The buttons seem a little loose, but don’t show any indication of coming off.Now, I know these are advertised as wide leg, but they have W-I-D-E legs! Since they are denim which has structure, If I stand with my feet about hip width apart (a natural stance), the legs of these jeans cross over each other, which looks rather ridiculous. The seams go crooked and it looks like I'm not standing straight even though I am. I have to stand with my legs spread strangely apart for them to look as they do on the model. If only I could slim down the legs, these would be attractive, but as they are, I just can’t see myself ever wearing them, not even just around the house, as the legs crash together with every step. I’ve included photos of how they look on me (5’4.5”, 30 waist, 39 hip, about 29 inseam), and also some with a measuring tape to show the actual measurements of the size Medium. I hope this helps in your decision.

I ordered these in the "blue" color, size large. I am 5'3, 145 lbs. These fit great! I love the stretchiness of the material, it is very soft, and almost lightweight enough for summer. These will look cute with either sandals or wedge heels. Very cute!

I ordered these in the color "bluing" in size 10. I am 5'3", 145 lbs. and they fit great! They are soft and have the right amount of stretch. I like the raw hem as well. Can definitely wear these with sandals, boots, or even wedges/heels.

I ordered these in the medium (unisex) size––I’m 5’5”, 140lbs and I found the medium to be the perfect amount of baggy without swimming in them so much that I feel like I have no shape. The elastic on both the ankle and waist is nice and not at all tight; the drawstring is just a simple cotton shoe-lace style rope; and there are no grommets to keep the string from disappearing into the elastic waistband. For me, the backside is a little saggy, but that’ll be different for everyone. Plus it will shrink over time because it’s cotton. The fabric is medium to lightweight, it’s slightly fleeced on the inside and a mostly soft outside with some texture to it, which is similar to a fine, corduroy stripe. Pepper is a great, dusty dark grey that goes with everything. I’m happy enough with them that I’m thinking of also buying them in blue.

I ordered these in two pairs (one blue and one black). For reference, I am 5’6” and 170 ish lbs. I ordered the 31 Short and they fit me perfectly and were the exact length I wanted them. If I hadn’t read the details, I probably would’ve ordered the regular and those would’ve been too long on me. I love the fit and stretch of these and was worried based on the other reviews. My waist is smaller than my hips so there’s a little gap in the back but not too bad with a belt. Very excited to wear these more!

I ordered these IVIR Women's Wide Leg High Waisted Cargo Pants in size Medium and color Khaki. For reference, I normally wear about a size 8 in jeans, and these pants fit me fine with some roominess to allow for freedom of motion and for shrinkage. The fabric is medium lightweight. Its cotton-soft hand-feel and slight stretchiness make for a very comfortable wearing experience. I like the wide-legged cut of these pants and all of the totally useable, actually deep-enough pockets! These pants are very high waisted (above my belly button prior to laundering). I am 5'4", long-waisted and short inseamed. Before laundering these trousers, I wore them with cowboy boots to absorb the excess leg length. They are extremely comfortable to move in and have an edgy-stylish vibe. I even received unsolicited compliments on how these trousers look. I recommend them!

I ordered these IWOLLENCE Women's Casual Loose Checkered Wide Leg Pants High Waist Straight Lounge Y2K Gingham Trousers Fall in a Size Small for my 10 year old daughter and she LOVES them! For reference, she is 5', 105lbs-ish, and wears a size 14 Girls from a Big Box store. These pants fit her great! The material is very soft and breathable. The overall garment quality is solid, with secure stitching and straight seams. The elastic waistband makes them very comfortable. Plus, the checkerboard print and wide leg make them very fashion forward. In this picture, my daughter just came in from playing outside all day when it is very windy... so she looks a little worse for wear in her sweatshirt and crazy hair. But when we have gone out in the past she has worn them with a white tshirt tucked in and her favorite tennis shoes-- and she got lots of compliments on them! While I do think they could be priced a little more competitively, we do love these pants!!!

I ordered these jean leggings for my wife, and I think they complement her figure beautifully. Here’s what she said about the fit: -The dimensions provided on the size chart are accurate. -A medium is great for someone with approximately a 28-inch waist, 40-inch hips, and 5'6" in height. A small size could fit because the pants are stretchy, but they would be very, very tight. -The fabric is good quality and breathable. -Keep in mind that the bell-bottom is super wide, and the waist is very high. If you have a belly ring, it doesn't cause discomfort due to the open design at the front.

I ordered these jeans as I like the style of them with the side pockets. Jeans can always be tricky to buy online but I've had some luck as of late so thought I would give these a try. The jeans are heavier than I was expecting. They are advertised as 'light' but I could tell just by the weight of the package that they were not as light as expected. I'm 5'4" and typically wear a size 12 so I ordered that same size. They fit fine in the waist and the hips but they are a bit long for me. I wish that the listing indicated the inseam vs. the total length because that's harder to judge on a high-waisted jean pair of jeans. I could still pull these off with the right platform boot or shoe so will wear them in those cases; especially since some jeans will shrink a bit in length with repeated washings. Otherwise, there is a little bit of stretch to them and they are reasonably comfortable.

I ordered these jeans in a size 34 (pictured) and also a size 32. For reference, I am 5'5", and I have a 31" waist and 45" hips, and am muscular. Neither pair fit me at all, which sucks since I love the colour and distressing. The larger pair fit me comfortably throughout the hips and thighs, but was massive in the waist. Almost comically so. I ordered a pair two sizes down, which is tight through the thighs and hips but is STILL far top large through the waist. To make matters worse, the smaller size (32, not pictured) are too short for me to wear, even with flip flops or another super-flat shoe... and I'm only 5'5"! Overall, I would not recommend these for women. They have a fit for a child: very straight up and down body with hips and waist that are the exact same size, and they are incredibly short as well. Based on my experience with these and other pairs of Levi's jeans, I think they should stick to making products for men. They clearly do not understand the shape of a woman's body.

I ordered these jeans in a size 9 in both Amal and Amal Pure colors. I already ordered the jeans in a size 9 in the color Lake and loved how soft the material is. I wanted a lighter color and chose the two Amal colors to compare which one I like better. Both of them have too much fading for my liking so I returned both. Also, the stitching is a bit off and is more noticeable on these jeans than on the Lake pair. The Amal color has the wallflower logo on the back and stitched within while the Amal Pure does not. The Amal Pure has a plain black tag within and less decorative back pockets. Also, the Amal Pure has tiny front pockets while the Amal has more normal sized front pockets that you can actually fit your hands deeper inside. The Amal color jeans are slightly lighter than the Amal Pure. In my pictures with them side by side, Amal is on the left and Amal Pure is on the right. In terms of fit, I am 5'4", 140ish lbs, 38" hips, 29" waist. I usually wear a size 7 in juniors and a 4 or 6 in women's and the size 7 in these jeans was a tad too tight so I kept the 9 in Lake. I love how soft these are but they do slide down when walking around extensively or when getting up from sitting. They are more like leggings than jeans. UPDATE: I wrote my original review in Fall 2018. It is now July 2020 and Wallflower instasoft jeans are the only jeans I wear since they are soft and stretchy and don't bother my sensitive skin. I still have my original Lake and they are a little less dark but still in great shape. I also have and wear regularly Shannon and Hayden all in a size 9. The Lake seems a little tighter than the other two but all are softer than normal jeans. I always wear these with a belt since they are super stretchy. But overall, they have held up to constant wear and look great which is surprising since the material is thin and my thighs rub.

I ordered these jeans in a size L according to the size chart as provided and they fit me exactly as expected. I am a short 5'3" long and always expect that jeans are going to be longer than what they appear on the model and though these were no exception, they are not as much longer as I expected! They fit perfect when I wear a boot with a little height , but also work well with my tennis shoes and sandals without dragging. The jeans have a crossover look at the button which give a different look and have a little button on the inside to keep the crossover waist from bunching or sliding. They have a little stretch to the denim which makes them super comfortable.

I ordered these jeans in color Blue, size 8. They fit me just the same as most name brand jeans of that size fit me. The waist is high yet well-proportioned in that it doesn't rise above my belly button like some other high-waisted pants I've purchased. I like where the hemline hits my legs. For reference, I'm 5'4". The fabric is substantial in weight and has the feel of a very comfortable, stretchy cotton denim. I've put these jeans through 5 laundry cycles using cold water and one brief drying cycle on medium heat and although they did shrink a bit, it didn't affect the fit much because the fabric is stretchy. I think the cut of these jeans is both stylish and flattering, and the wide pant legs allow lots of freedom of motion in the thigh area. There was one hiccup, however. The jeans arrived carrying a strong odor in the fabric, which to me smells like petroleum. This is the reason I took off one star. I've now put them through 5 wash cycles, one brief dryer cycle and hung them outside in the sunshine. I'd researched that baking soda, vinegar and/or lemon juice might help, so I used those in the last wash cycle and it did help to a degree.

I ordered these jeans in my usual size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. There’s a lot of stretch to them so hopefully they will keep their shape and not get baggy or loose on me after some time. I’ll update if that’s the case. I really like this vintage color as it goes with so many tops. I’m 5’4” for reference on length. I weigh 132 lbs. These jeans feel thick and substantial, like a good quality jeans that will last a long time. I like that they’re more of a mid rise since I have short torso. They’re more flattering on me than super high waisted pants. These are cropped and will look cute with sandals or boots. The rise is 10” and the inseam is 27”. If you are petite, these will probably work too. They’ll just be regular length on you. I definitely recommend these flattering jeans. I think they are nice on the booty as well.

I ordered these jeans in size large, light color. I'd give these jeans a 4/5. The material is very comfortable and has some stretch to it which is very nice. The pockets are a good size and they fit my phone in there with no issues. The only reason that I took it down 1 star is because they are kind of short. I am only 5'-4" and the jeans do not even cover my ankles. I would prefer if they were a little longer instead of feeling like high waters. They are listed as high waisted but they definitely aren't.

I ordered these jeans in size medium in the black, grey color. I'm 5'8" 125lbs with a fairly straight figure, and they fit very comfortably. The material has a nice stretch to it and fits snugly but not too tight like some high-rise jeans do. I measured the rise at 10inches. The inseam was 29inches which is a little short for my height, so I'll probably wear them mostly with boots in the future. The cut is wide leg but has kind of a slight flare and the leg opening is 10inches. A few small issues are that the jean material doesn't go down into the pocket at all so when I stand up after sitting, I have to re-tuck the pockets. these are regular sized pockets, so that's good. Also, two of the three buttonholes are a little small and I have to kind of force the buttons in. Still, overall, these are good jeans and a cute and comfortable style.

I ordered these jeans in size Medium in the color Medium Blue. They have a recycled look to them. They are cut roomy in the thighs and legs, which I find comfortable. They are made of 76% Cotton/26% Polyester/2% Elastane so they have some give to them. After laundering them three times, they haven't noticeably shrunk. For reference, I'm a long-waisted/short-inseamed 5' 4", 124 lbs, 29" waist and 38" hips. The length of these on me is at the longest side of OK. I think I could have ordered these in a size small, as they are a little baggy and the waist slips down a bit if I don't wear a belt. They have a strange smell that hasn't completely washed out after 3 washings, but I suspect it will eventually. Overall, I like these comfy jeans and I think they look kind of boho stylish.

I ordered these jeans with the intent on having a comfortable, loose fitting basic denim in my closet. These do have a lot going for them. The fabric is stretchy and comfortable. I love the high waist and the wider hemmed bottoms. The sizing seems 'close' but maybe just slightly off. I ordered my regular size of 12 with the expectation that the waist might be a bit too tight based on the measurements given. They actually fit perfectly in the waist so I would suggest they might run slightly large to their own measurement chart. On the downside, I knew that these were wider in the leg but there's almost too much fabric without the kind of shaping to help make it work. At 5'4", they are also a bit too long. I'm not so worried about the length because I can make it work with a chunky loafer or boot but overall, these are not particularly flattering on me and I don't think I'll wear them out much. I'll still give them a chance...maybe after a few more washes/dries, they'll work better for me but for now, I would just call them 'okay'.

I ordered these overalls for my wife and she absolutely Loves them. She said they are super comfortable. She is 5’ 5” and 112 lbs and I got her a size medium in the color “blue tides.” They fit her a little baggy but run pretty much true to size. She says that she loves the fabric and color. She also said they washed well. I think they look very cute on her and I highly recommend them.

I ordered these overalls for my wife and she absolutely Loves them. She said they are super comfortable. I ordered these pants because I'm all about comfort these days. I wanted something stretchy I could dress up to wear to work or workout in. These pants do check those boxes. They are high waisted and comfortable. The only negatives for me is the legs are a little too wide for my taste and the length is too long. Great for taller girls. For reference I'm 5'4" 139lbs.

I ordered these pants for a bachelorette event. It seems we will have custom T-shirts that we have to wear with hot pink or "Barbie" pink pants/bottoms. I wasn't sure what look I wanted to go for so I ordered these when I saw them because they were the right color & something a bit more dressy than jeans. I ordered size Large, which is my usual size. I might have been able to get away with a Medium as they are fairly loose & the waist has elastic. I'm tall (5'9") & was worried that they'd be way too short but when I look at them in the full-length mirror, I think the length really isn't bad. The material is light enough for summer. I'm not sure if I'll be wearing these for the event but I do see myself wearing them for something else. They are cute & well made & kind of different from what I normally wear, so It'll be fun to mix things up.

I ordered these pants for a holiday party — one in red rose in an XL and one in green in XXL —because I wanted to make sure they wouldn’t be too tight on my legs. The green didn’t arrive in time, but the red in XL was perfect! I am 180 lbs, 5’5” with short legs, usually a size 12. They hit the top of my foot. I wish they were a little shorter more like ankle pants to show the ankle strap on my shoes. But maybe I’ll get them hemmed before next year. Because they are such a great price and quality and I love them so much, I just ordered them in 5 more colors!! As I’ve gotten older I’ve become a lazy shopper — once I find something I love/like, I buy multiples!!

I ordered these pants for school because of our uniform policy. Unfortunately the color, instead of tan and khaki, is dark olive green. I saw the title of it and thought that was a mistake because the picture was Tan-ish brown. Green is not what I ordered and I'm very unhappy with this fact. Although the color was ENTIRELY wrong, the fit was actually really good. That's why I give them 3 stars. The size couldn't have been better. I'm 96 lbs and 5"1 and these were the perfect size. They were EXTREMELY long though. Very flared so I just cut about 2 inches off the bottom and they were good. But in honesty, listen to the negative reviews and find a better pair of pants. NOT worth the 40$

I ordered these pants in "Green" and the pants I received are far less a true green than the appear on screen - more a muddy color, like a khaki green. That's fine - even preferred - for me, but not what I would've anticipated based on the image. They're attractive and fit well (I ordered based on the size chart). I'm 5'5" and they hit me at the ankle as they do on the model. I would probably not recommend these pants if you are taller than 5'6". I want to rate these well - these pants are listed at less than $20, so I take quality with a grain of salt. But the zipper on the pair I received is truly awful quality. I'll be surprised if it lasts a month before becoming unusable. No one else so far has mentioned the zipper quality, so if you want to take a chance, go for it. But unfortunately, I can't recommend these.

I ordered these pants in a size medium. I would normally get a large, but because I am working on losing some weight and wanted them to last longer for me I ordered smaller. They definitely will look a lot better once I lose another 10 pounds, but they are still comfortable in the meantime. They have a little bit of stretch to them, so they work. I am 5’6” 182 pounds and I’m definitely rocking a mom of 4/perimenopause body. My butt usually needs a lot of help from my pants, and these don’t necessarily do that, but a long sweater paired with it is helping me. I’m really happy with the quality for the price, and hopefully these keep me a little bit motivated to keep working on my weight loss goals.

I ordered these pants in Black and the color is true to the picture. I am 5'6". The pants were ordered in XL and they fit me well - the pants end as expected. I love the elastic waistband that is secured with a drawstring too. This gives the pants a fun look. The pants have front pockets. The fabric has an interesting feel to it. The fabric is a little thin, but not is not see through.

I ordered these pants in size medium with a 30” inseam as I have longer legs. I am 5’6” and weigh 135lbs. The pants fit great and I love the added length for the ability to wear both flats and heels. I ordered the color beige and it is not see through though I’d recommend lighter colored undergarments. These pants can be both dressed up or down for a more business casual look.

I ordered these pants in size Medium, in the color Sail Blue, which is a beautiful shade that neither looks washed out nor so dark that you can't see enough of the soft blue tones...it's just right! The Lyocell fabric is soft to the touch and thin but not see-through, so these trousers will feel great on warm days. The fabric is very flowy in the wide legs. Wearing these pants is a comfortable experience. The nice wide, fully elastic waistband adds to the comfort, and the functional, deep pockets just make like easier all day long! For reference, I usually wear about a size 8 in jeans. My waist is about 29", my hips are about 38", I'm 5'4" tall and long-waisted (hence, I have a shorter inseam than some other people of my height). I may have been OK with a size Small, but I like how the elastic waistband of the size Medium feels. It's comfortable and not restrictive. The length is just a tad long on me, but it still works. The photos I've attached were taken after the pants were washed and dried on Medium heat one time. I've read that Lyocell could shrink about 3% during the first laundering and then not much afterwards. These are wonderful, smart-looking, comfy trousers that are a joy to wear!

I ordered these pants in the XL. They are cute and fit great. They are pretty long. They come past the ankle and I'm 5ft6in. My waist measures 40inches and I they were not tight at all.

I ordered these pants looking for a cooler option in the Texas heat. These are perfect for dressing up or down. I got tons of compliments, I need them in every color! I’m 5’2 and 120lbs, these are a small and fit perfectly.

I ordered these pants postpartum because of the elastic waistband. They're a great lightweight pant for summer. I only have two complaints: the elastic waistband is a bit wide for my liking and the pants are shorter than I expected. I am 5'4" for reference and they come just to the floor if I wear flats. You can see the length in the picture with me barefoot.

I ordered these pants with the hopes of wearing them on an upcoming Europe trip. Initially they arrived very wrinkled and long so I steamed them a bit to see what they would truly look like on and this did help shorten them a bit as well. I loved how they truly look like they're fully linen. The waist and proportions of size Small comfortably fit my 5'3" frame just right. The biggest issue though was that one pant leg was longer than the other! In trying them on with shoes, it was visibility apparent that one side reached the ground but the other wasn't. I tried adjusting but the difference was still obvious. Laying the pants down, you can see in the photos how the uneven hemline is up to an inch different. Returned as defective and hopefully the replacement will not have this issue!

I ordered these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans for a healthy purpose. I have osteoarthritis in my knees and plan to wear my new knee braces when my boyfriend and I start walking trails regularly during the cool fall weather. I needed jeans that would allow room for my braces. These jeans are definitely 12" wide leg baggy.I ordered the largest size 14 (size chart measurements: waist 36.6", hip 46.5") because my waist is 38" and hips are 42". Lucky for me, the waist and hips fit well. Other reviewers have said they run small. The waist band is also adjustable with additional buttonholes. I stand 5'4" and already knew these would be long on me, as are most jeans I have ordered. Sometimes, I can roll up pantlegs, but these wide legs won't accommodate. The fabric is not overly heavy, and the stitching appears good. Because they are wide-legged, I was able to slide these on even while wearing my sneakers, and no one will know when I wear knee braces underneath. I did notice a bit of a buckling with the zipper though, which was even more apparent after I washed them.I checked to make sure these jeans were colorfast before I washed them in cold water on a delicate cycle. Then I tumble-dried on medium heat with other jeans but removed and hung them 15 minutes before the end of the cycle. I wore my white shirt that's more form-fitting. I didn't notice any shrinkage in the jeans, other than maybe a half inch in the length. However, the zipper’s buckling was definitely more uneven. I figured out it's better to zip up, first, and then button the waist.Overall, this wide leg style doesn't really look attractive on me because of my apple shape and short height, but these PLNOTME Women’s Boyfriend Wide Leg Jeans will serve a purpose for me to start walking again for exercise so I can start losing this excessive weight.
