I ordered these specifically because I need a 31" inseam, which is what the description states. When they finally arrived (2 full weeks after I ordered them), they were shipped from Costco (note the Costco package in the photo) and were marked as having a 30" inseam. I tried on one pair, and not only were they too short, the fabric was thin and not of the same quality as my previous NYDJ Alina jeans. I checked the Costco website, and sure enough, they sell these jeans for $24.99 (less if 5 or 10 pairs purchased) with a 30" inseam, and many reviews thin/lesser quality fabric. I returned these for a refund, and was charged a $9 restocking fee. Perhaps that was somewhere in the fine print, but I did not see it. All in all I am thoroughly dissatisfied.

There are two buckle straps, three side button closures, two side pockets, and a bib pocket. I had to roll up a small cuff on the medium. Machine wash on cold. They look very nice on and I think they will last a long time and maybe look better with age.

I ordered three different types of Levi’s trucker jackets all in size Large (recommended size) for a side by side comparison. Please note that the 3 trucker jackets DO NOT COME WITH A HOODIE. I was simply making sure I could layer a hoodie underneath the denim jackets as this is how I like to wear them. All 3 Large sized jackets passed my hoodie test and Large felt like an accurate size recommendation to the style of each jacket. The sleeve length was long and perfect for me. If you’re looking for a more “form fitting” jacket, order the original trucker in your normal size (it does have stretch if you’re between sizes), order the Ex-Boyfriend a size smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length though), and skip the Baggy trucker altogether or try ordering it two sizes smaller (no stretch.. watch out for arm length changes ordering small sizes). My measurements: Height 5’8” - Weight 155lbs - Shoulder to Shoulder 17" - Bust 38" - Waist 28” - Hips 40". All of the Levi’s denim trucker jackets have inside “pockets” which is a feature I have always loved from Levi’s denim jackets. If you notice from the photos, each jacket is about 2” longer than the next. The Original trucker jacket being the shortest length and darkest wash; pictured in the first photo, all the way to the left (top & bottom) of the second photo and all the way to the right in the third photo. It is meant to be roomy but more form fitting, while the other two styles are meant to be baggy. I was still able to layer the Original trucker over my hoodie comfortably in my recommended size. While the Original trucker fit nicely and probably would look best over dresses out of the 3, I chose the Ex-Boyfriend because I already own a form fitting denim jacket and wanted something different. I liked the Original trucker the least because it was so dark, had all black buttons, and looked too new and clean. This sounds weird, but all my black jeans are all faded, so I preferred the faded washes on the other two black trucker jackets. The Baggy Trucker and Ex-Boyfriend trucker also have no stretch and have contrasting brownish brass buttons which felt more vintage. Notice the Ex-Boyfriend jacket has buttons on the right “men’s side” while both the Original Trucker and Baggy Trucker have buttons on the left “women’s side”. I hope my review helps you decide!

I ordered three pair in prints and was not disappointed. The colors are vibrant and the patterns are beautiful.(5'7" / 160 /curvy). Ordered one size up, so XL would have been too small. The 2XL feels good. 4 stars not 5 because they did shrink and also Ithe fit on the waist, the elastic sits low on the hips if I want a full length. I tried them on before washing and they fit perfectly but I washed them and hung to dry and then 10 min on Low in dryer. They came out about 2" short on the legs and the overall fit more snug and is similar to sweat pants. The fabric is comfortable and not thin, but light on the body and translucent in the light. I'm happy and considering buying more.

I ordered two pairs of the Plus Size: one in white and one in navy. I placed the order on Monday evening, and they arrived today, which was good not having to wait long to get them. I tried both on. They are very soft and stretchy, but fairly clingy on the thighs, behind, and mid-section (and I could see my cellulite with the white pair). I am 5'8" tall, weigh 180 pounds (yes, I'm overweight), and wear a size 14 or XL in bottoms. I don't see how these would fit as comfortably in sizes much larger than my size 14/XL if you're curvy and/or overweight. I liked that they were long enough to reach ankles even after getting them around my hips, behind, and tummy. My ankles are small and lower legs are thin, and these were not way too loose at the bottom. I included a picture my thigh in the white pair with a red paper underneath to show that the white is somewhat sheer, and I/you can see through them some. But that's okay with me, since I'll only be wearing these with long, loose tops, so they won't be visible much above the thighs. The navy pair was not sheer to me. Overall, I like them, would recommend buying them, and will probably reorder in at least one other color. Hopefully, this long review was .

I own several pairs of Amazon Essentials Hi-Rise Skinny Jeans jeans in sizes 26 - 27, and these seem to be sized and cut differently than my other AE Skinnies. For reference, I am 5'3", 117 lbs, and size 26 is typically the best fit. I believe that translates to size 2 - 4 in vanity sizing, and the pair I received is a size 6, so they feel quite roomy with a very relaxed fit throughout the legs. The color is beautiful, but they look oversized and baggy throughout the legs and especially the seat. The fabric is made with 3% Elastane, and they feel very stretchy. I prefer a second skin type of fit, and these would have been perfect in size 2, or possibly even 0 (pushing my luck). These are good to have on hand for the holiday feasting season in case my weight takes a dramatic upward turn in the aftermath of all those yummy desserts. These jeans are generously sized and I would recommend sizing down if you prefer a skin-tight fit. If you like having a bit of leeway, go ahead and order your usual size. ** If you found this review to be at all, please take a moment and click the button below to let Amazon know. Thank you so much 😊 **

I own these cargo pants in just about every color, and I was shocked by the quality of the material. The fabric is smooth and gives the pants a tailored look, with an elastic band in the back that prevents gapping. There isn’t much stretch, but they are true to size. I typically wear a size 16 or 17 depending on the brand, and I purchased these in a size 18—they fit perfectly. The pants have functioning pockets and a real zipper, unlike some that just have decorative details. In my opinion, they are tall-friendly. At 5’6”, they look great with heels. These can easily be dressed up for an elevated casual look or paired with sneakers or flats and a tank top for a neat, casual style.

I picked these up for my daughter in a size small in the fresh bright blue color option. My daughter is 5’4" and 120lbs. These are quite big, especially in the length. She is happy with the high cut waist and the wide leg. These hit her at the belly button, but she needs a belt to keep them up. We washed them in hot water and machine dried them in hopes of shrinking them, but there was not much shrinkage. Made in China from 71% Cotton, 27% Polyester, 2% Elastane they have a metal zipper and button closure and nice deep pockets. Overall they are a cute, and very current style of jean. There is whiskering to the dye at the leg creases and the color stayed fresh. I did wash them inside out to protect the color and prevent fading.

I purchased 3 *different* pairs of these jeans. One pair of "Immaculate, One pair of "Janice" and one pair of "Mascara." All in the same size, 16L. All described to be the same cut, "Gold Label" etc. etc.. All you have to do is chose the 'color.' But the price varies wildly. So I assumed something other than the color of the jeans was the basis for this reasoning. Wrong. Apparently the price is soulely based on popularity. Seriously? Please read on, because I do love these jeans & I did get a more than satisfactory response from Amazon. I'm 5'9" and thick in the middle and prefer a little extra length to my jeans. I probably could have gone a size smaller, but I wanted to allow for at least ten percent possible shrinkage due to washing. And I don't want to look ridiculous. The jeans I ordered have slightly different shades of dark blue with gently faded patches. But there really is no other way to tell which pair is which. Remove *all* the tags, and you have 3 pairs of the same jeans on in different shades. When I showed my husband, he didn't notice much of a difference between the pairs either. Just that the 3rd was slightly darker. However all three pairs cost different prices. The most expensive pair being titled "Immaculate." Once the jeans came in the mail and I had tried them all on, surprisingly *they all fit exactly the same.* I have no problem admitting that I'm very happy with all three pairs. But laid out side by side, all 3 pairs of the jeans look *very* similar. Was I sent the same model *twice*, or what? Why is one pair so expensive? No where on the jeans' tags do I see any difference between them other than their scan numbers. They have no 'names' or clear numbers, like my beloved "501's." If I had gone to the mall and picked out the same jeans myself, they would have all been from the same shelf at the same price. They are all the same length, cut, rise, style, and fit exactly the same. The inside and outside labels, text/print and logos are nearly the same as well. And only one pair states "Gold Label" on the outer back leather tag. It's like the one pair of jeans came out a year earlier under a different name - but is otherwise the same in every other way to the other pairs. So I don't get why one pair cost me twenty one dollars, the next pair costing me twenty four dollars, and the last cost a whopping thirty eight dollars? I'm seriously considering returning the most expensive pair/s (which ones though?) and reordering the cheapest pair/s again. Like I said, I *really* like these jeans. They all *fit* the same and fit well and are flattering. But I feel like someone is trying to pull the wool over my eyes here, and I'm really annoyed by the difference in cost because that is the only thing that really differentiates one pair from another, aside from the color. Because if it's color/dye that costs more, that's absurd, and one or more pairs has to go back. In the mean time this whole thing has kept me from actually wearing any of the jeans. So. Which pair or pairs of jeans do I return?!

I purchased a Medium because the last belt I bought on Amazon was a medium and it fit perfectly . My waist is a 36 , and this belt is waaay to small . I measured the belt with a tape measure . It measured 38 inches from buckle to end but the measurement from the buckle to the first hole is 30 and to the last hole is 34, therefore this belt should be for no larger than a 34 waist . So make sure you go a size up

I purchased a medium but probably could have done a small. I’m 5’2”, 120 lbs. The back has elastic for a little give. They were a little long. I rolled them once and did a quick hand stitch. Glad I did. I wore my Doc M with them and super cute!

I purchased a pair of these 725 Levi's in a size too small (recently lost a lot of weight)so I'm confused on what size I wear. I first ordered a size 6 which I was not able to pull above my thighs. I purchased the 2xl in Blue. I want to love this jacket, since it fits perfectly and is exactly what I am looking for but the smell is so BAD! It arrived with tags on and in a plastic ziplocked bag. Once I took it out it had a horrid odor, like your face being smushed up to a new stack of tires or like it was dipped in chemicals! I washed it with a brand name quality detergent most of us use and lightly dried it with my favorite smelling dryer sheets. Still it smells bad! I then sprayed it with a wrinkle eraser spray that smells like clean cotton and hung it. After letting it hang to air out for two days it STILL SMELLS! For anyone that can overlook the smelliness, the collar is not frayed and the 2xl is the perfect size for anyone that is a size DD, 18 or 20, and 5'10''+. It has a bit of a stretchiness to it, which is nice. However, it is much lighter denim than the ones you get at some other big brand names and stores.

I purchased the A-line style described as 94 centimeters long and it is in reality only 80 centimeters. I am tall, which I knew already it was never going to look all the way down to my ankles as I wished, but at 6ft tall, those 5 centimeters would have made a difference. The skirt looks Midi on me and, because of my frame (plus size), it doesn't look good on me. Fabric is nice, very stretchy. I am size 1X or 18 and the 18 fit perfectly and it wasn't restrictive at all like normal denim is. I have contacted the seller to ask which model is the longest since I really liked the product, I just need something longer.

I purchased the Size: 6 | Inseam Length: 28 Inches | Color: Cape Town-waterless. They fit true-to-size, I am 5' 3" ~133 lbs and curvy. The fit was snug but flattering with a generous amount of stretch. The 28" length was perfect - right at my ankle. For anyone wondering, these fit very very similar to the pull-on version (which I also purchased) and the sizing is consistent between the two. In my opinion, they are equally as comfortable and you get real functioning front pockets with this version. I absolutely would have kept them if the color was as depicted in the photos. However, the color of the whiskering and fading is much more yellow and dingy in person and not at all white like the pictures. Overall, these are a really great value for a twenty-ish dollar price point especially if you find a color you like. I'm trying to lose the quarantine 15 and not looking to invest in expensive jeans until I do.

I purchased the X-large in light blue. I'm 5'5" and 190lbs. I typically wear a size 16 jeans and these were tight in the hips and waist. I don't think these have much stretch to them as there was only a little give in the material. These are definitely a light weight material, which would be good for spring and summer weather. I feel these "would" be comfortable if the size had fit better, as is,they don't have much comfort because they are tight to sit in. The quality is good, well stitched, solid button. As for flattering...once again, size. I would say these fit a solid size 14 in women's jeans. These are a decent valueif you get the right size.

I purchased these and was a little skeptical because of the look of them on display vs on a physical person. Baby when I opened the package I was in love 😍! I am 5’10 180lbs and size 8/10 and I ordered a medium. Love these pants

I purchased these as part of a hippie costume for my 16 year old daughter. She is 4’10” and 88lbs, so they’re a bit big on her but really cute. They aren’t the best made.. thin ( but silky) material and poorly sewn waist band, but suitable for the cause. She looked really cute. I wouldn’t but these to wear around all year, but they made a great costume!
I purchased these for a rave and they were incredibly comfy and stylish! For reference, I ordered a medium and im 5’2 and I weigh 128.

I purchased these for Sparkle Day and Rock Your School day! I love that they do not itch as the inside does not have a seam that allows the sequins to scratch your skin. The inside is a smooth material! They were a bit warm when I wore then outside in 80 degree weather! I am 5’ 6” and they were very long; I had to wear heels! The flare is excellent! I ordered a small and they are high waisted and fit great!

I purchased these for work. I’m 150 pounds and bottom heavy but these pants fit me well. They’re very soft and stretchy. I love the deep pockets. I wish the legs were longer but I don’t think that’s worth losing a star.

I purchased these in a size 9. I am 5ft 6in, 160lbs and wear a ladies size 8-10. I used to buy these at Kohls but they stopped carrying them. I wear these fulltime (40 hours/week) and they last about 6-8 months. I bend up and down with my job so I am pleased that they last 6-8 months. Each time I have replaced these khakis it has been because they rip in the inner thigh area. Before shopping for them here I searched for bootcut khakis EVERYWHERE! I couldn’t find anything close to these. Everything I tried was either a skinny leg or cropped leg and the few that were bootcut were horribly shaped. I love these khakis and I’m so please I found them on here! So pleased that I purchased an additional 5 pairs!

I purchased these in both black and khaki. Love that these pants are both dressy AND casual. They are a tad looser than I was expecting, but luckily not too long as I'm only 5'2 and they don't need to be hemmed. The material is nice and light and feels nice too. Oh, and there are pockets!

I purchased these in Ellis, Black, and Seattle, all size 6 Medium. First off, they're not very high rise- more like a mid-rise. My Levi's all have a much higher rise, so if you're looking for a true high rise, look elsewhere. Secondly, you're going to want to order a few different sizes bc they're pretty inconsistent. The Black pair were so tight I could barely get them over my hips! The Seattle were slightly looser, but still tight, and Ellis was just right! All were a tad long (I'm 5'6 145lbs). Going to return for Ellis size 6S and Seattle Size 8 Short. The Black pair had a cheaper fabric quality, more like a jegging but the Seattle and Ellis washes are ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ quality. Will definitely try those two washes in a Short!

I purchased these in men’s first, due to the fact that people said the men’s fit better. The men’s were lower waited and fit every where except for my waist there was a big gap in the back. When i ordered these women’s i struggled so much with the sizing. i’m usually a 6-8-10 depending on the jean, i new these didn’t have much stretch so i went with a 13-32. im 5’5 for reference and these are a little long but with boots will be fine. These fit like a glove, i’m a little wider on the hips and smaller on the waist. Highly recommend getting a size or 2 bigger than you’re normal size. Overall LOVE

I purchased these in my normal size small, black, they fit TTS, very stretchy, forgiving and soooooo comfy. I’m 5’3”, 130#, short torso with long legs. Loved these pants BUT I personally didn’t feel they were a keeper for me bc of my body type. I needed something to elongate my torso and this high waisted pants just doesn’t do it for me. Will be returning. Would be great for work wear.

I purchased these in the color B-BLACK in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say they run a tad small if you have bigger hips/butt. I sized up because I was concerned about them being too tight, and I'm glad I did! I could do a large but I really like the looseness of the XL, as it's comfy but still flattering, and with a bodysuit, the fit is perfect in the waist (this one is from Pumiey, btw; I have a review of it as well). They are loose in the tummy area without being too baggy. I'm glad the pleats are super subtle. This style does not have the elastic back, as I don't love that, but I do wish they had some little fake slit pockets in the back, like other slacks do. I'm a little on the shorter side and had some extra length at the bottom, even though my legs are longer in proportion to my body, so I could wear a pair of heels and still be fine, but I don't often wear anything but flats, so I'll probably end up getting them hemmed by an inch or two. The only small complaint I can make is that I wish the button was darker, as I feel the tan one cheapens the look a bit. The material is a bit hard to describe, but I'd say it's pretty standard Amazon loose slack material, if you've tried others. It's thin and a little slick, but not very stretchy, if that makes sense. They feel kind of cool on the inside and they move nicely. I couldn't tell you how well these will hold up in the long run, but I'll try to update after I've worn and washed a handful of times. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these, but maybe size up if you have bigger hips/butt. I've been looking for some cheap slacks to try out because I haven't tried to dress nicely for the office in so long, not since I gained weight years ago, and these are a nice intro back into it. I actually purchased this exact style in B-BEIGE in the same XL size, so I'll post pics of those. I may grab the C style next, since they have tonal buttons and slit pockets in the back, even if they have elastic in the waistband too.

I purchased these jeans because I really like the embroidery and it will match most of my wardrobe. The material is standard stretch, super cheap. They are mid rise jeans, and they will drag a little bit. I am 5'6 and they are long on me. I wish these were high rise, if they were I would wear them more. But overall, these are alright definitely wear a belt with them.

I purchased these jeans in 3 colors: Dark Blue (8/13/21), Quinn (2/15/22), and Samantha (4/24/22). All 3 were in size 5 and fit great (I'm about 5'5" 120 pounds). Dark Blue has a smoother fabric that’s not quite denim and while the leg cut is the same style, the back pockets are different, they’re more trouser looking. Quinn and Samantha are the same style, both have the “W” logo stitching in the back pockets. Quinn was made of thicker/sturdier denim and cost $50 but Samantha cost $69 and is in a thinner cheaper denim. Samantha being the newest one barely lasted 3 months because there's a rip along the back pocket. Thankfully I noticed the tear while folding laundry and was not out in public. Each pair isn’t cheap but for Samantha to cost the most at $69.00, I would expect it to last way more than 3 months!

I purchased these jeans in the color Cetacean blue, or dark blue. I bought both the size 4 and 6 bc I didn’t know which size I would be. For reference, I’m 5’4”, ~138-142 lbs. These are very stretchy! They have a lot of give, so I would probably size down from your usual non-stretch size jeans. I ended up keeping the size 4 (pictured). Material is good quality! (not amazing), but for the price, it’s good quality. The length hits just above my ankle. I have a lot of room in these jeans because of the stretch and wide leg style. Will be cute with boots and a belt or with sneakers for fall.

I purchased these jeans not really knowing what to expect, but I’m glad I did! I’m currently around 255, 5’4, and wear size 16 jeans and after reading the comments I decided to order a size up (size 18), size 16, and a size down (size 14). I was able to fit the size 18 pair, but the size 16 fit me perfectly and was slimming to my stomach area! The jeans offer a slight stretch and were very comfortable to wear all night. If you are plus size and on the fence of getting these jeans I encourage you to give them a try! I love them and will be ordering more as I continue to lose weight!

I purchased these leggings to make an outfit with the new fad of Kimono tops. I selected the pair in white. First, let me say that when I opened the package of these and touched them for the first time, I totally fell in love. They were so amazingly soft. However, my love instantly turned to disdain after I discovered all of the issues with the leggings. First, the entire seam of the back of the leggings (at the buttocks) was half-ripped and looked as if there was an issue when sewing them. (See photos) I immediately knew that I had to return them but I wanted to get a new pair, so I did an exchange. The other issue with my first order was that it had strange stains in multiple places. I notified Amazon and got my exchange started. The new pair arrived quickly and they were just as soft and luxurious as the first leggings! Thankfully, this time the leggings were free of any defects. They fit perfectly (I am 5'8, 185 pounds and all hips and thighs- I purchased a Plus Size). I probably could have ordered the next size down, too.The white leggings are slightly see-through and be fair warned, if your legs aren't toned, it will look like you got hit in the legs by a bag of nickels. Regardless, I still liked them a ton and will likely buy them in other colors!

I purchased these pants back in May and didn't wear them until recently Sept. This was the only time I've worn them and the 2nd pair I've purchased. The same tear happened in both pair in the exact same place. The inner thigh(right). The pants didn't fit tight at all. However, there is no stretch in the fabric. I wear a size 16-18 (5'6";230lbs), and I purchased the XXXL which were a comfortable fit all around, especially on my thighs and belly.

I purchased these pants for a beach vacation! Perfect for those sunset nights! These pants are light weight, flowy, and perfect for short girls! I’m 5ft 1 and these pants hit to the ground with flat shoes, which is how I like my pants. They are very wide leg and the color is vibrant! The only con is the waistband is a little snug than I prefer.

I purchased these pants for a Safari trip. The quality is decent for the price and they fit great. They are comfortable but the waist band is a little tight even if it’s elastic. I’m 5’4, 150 lb and I purchased a Medium.

I purchased these pants for a tropical vacation. I loved the fit so much I wore them almost everyday. I wore them as a swimsuit cover up and even for dinner out. I purchased the black, blue, white and green. The white are lined. And I purchased two pair for my 19 year old daughter. We are both 5’3” and around 160 pounds. We purchased the large.

I purchased these pants for camping. They are really comfortable and have good stretch. I am 5’5 and 135 lbs. they fit perfect and I love the look of them. Definitely going to get some good use out of them. For outdoors and fall winter outfits!

I purchased these pants in camel and white. I am 5'10" 222lbs and I bought them in xxl long. I love the length. I think the colors are true to pictured. The quality seems good. As you can see, I carry a lot of my weight around my middle. These pants have a wide waistband that helps suck me in a little. My only complaint is that, even with my big middle, the waist could be a little with belt loops added. They stretch out a little after wearing. I've tried to order them in black but twice I received a different pant, one being a Levi's brand, neither did I like. I'd really like to have the same pant in black. They're comfortable pants. I like them.

I purchased these pants in dark blue previously in 6L. I am 5.8 and 140 lbs. 6L fits perfectly. Then, I came back for these light blue ones, but these are cut differently. Not as curvy as the dark blue and tighter on the legs. They still fit, but are not comfortable and too tight in the tighs causing these to ride down when walking. I have attached photos to show the difference. The color is nice, though I could live without the light distress on these...the blue is a nice light jeans blue. Update: I have purchased 2 more pairs of the same, thinking maybe it was just that one pair cut differently, but now looking at all 3 light blue pairs here, all look the same and all are different than the dark blue pair. They will go back as they are not comfy.

I purchased these pants in the green color, which is true to the picture. The fit and feel are nice and at 5’ tall ,it is nice that they are short girl friendly. The issues is ,like others reviews, is that the button popped right off as soon as I put them on. This is obviously an ongoing issue and the seller should make improvements and quality control to make sure these buttons are sewed correctly. Now I have to purchase a sewing kit and try to add the button back. I can’t see so, hopefully this will go okay.

I purchased these pants to pair with a champagne colored crystal top I got for NYE last minute, and they ended up being perfect! Was worried that they may not be long enough, or would be too large around the waist, but a small fit me very well. I’m 5’-8” tall, 128lbs, and normally bounce between a small and medium due to my height but more slender figure. There was enough room in the waist for me to sit comfortably, but they didn’t fall below my belly button when standing. I wore heels with these, and they fell just below my ankles… JUST long enough for someone my height to wear heels with in a size small. They’re very comfortable (feels like silk pajamas!) but they’re lined enough to not be sheer. Was able to use a standard iron on these without damaging the fabric (which is important, because they do wrinkle easily.) however, would suggest you iron them inside out. Love!

I purchased these pants to wear to concert and I absolutely loved them. They are really comfortable & they look great. Only thing I would suggest, is making the back pockets deeper (the back pockets are a little shallow and I could not fit my cell phone in the pocket) but other than that... the waist was comfortable with the elastic band, the pants length was perfect and they looked great... not to mention they were affordable. I plan to get another pair. I am 5'8" and weigh approximately 198 lbs and the XXL was a perfect fit for me.

I purchased these to wear to a concert: Tori Kelly's "Purple Skies" tour. The album screamed Y2K vibes and... hello, PURPLE!! The fit: The pants fit a little bit snug around my waist. I ordered a Medium, as I'm typically a medium in leggings and sweatpants. Even though they fit snug, I don't know that I would have sized up because everywhere else, the fit was just right.. any baggier I wouldn't have loved the look as much. They come just above my bellybutton, so they'd look nice with a cropped top as well. I'm 5'7" and they hit right above my ankle; however, depending on the shoe you wear, it won't look like you're about to wade through a flash flood. You can also loosen or tighten the draw string at the ankles. The color: These pants are VERY purple. Less of a royal purple color, more like a deep lilac; which I quite like (see the second pic for the true color). So, keep that in mind :) The quality: Without a doubt, these are some pretty well made pants for an amazon find. It's always hit or miss for me when shopping for clothing.. sometimes things are so thin and very see through; but, the fabric used for these pants are on par with any decently priced cargo's from a traditional retailer. I've washed and dried them with no shrinkage or color bleeding and very minimal wrinkles. All of the pockets are functional!

I purchased this jacket in black, size M (I’m 5’3 for reference). I absolutely love this jacket! The length was perfect, and the material was surprisingly soft! It was slightly oversized, but not too big where it can swallow a small frame. I will definitely purchase the other washes!!

I purchased two colors to see if either would work for a 70s party. Although the measurements and fabric on both pair are listed as being identical, the measurements and fabric were actually different. I’m 5’10 barefoot, weigh 185 lbs., and ordered a Large. Black Pair: The black pair were 2-3 inches shorter than the printed black-and-blue ones. They were highwaters on me without wearing shoes. The fabric was extremely thin, which is great for summer. They were also comfortable and stretchy, but they’re made like a super-thin, cheap t-shirt…or the cheapest black leggings you can find. A medium would’ve been fine, but I ordered Larges in hopes of having extra length. The black pair will pick up lint (etc.) fairly easy, they wrinkle fast and came out of the package SUPER wrinkled, and they’ll surely fade or shrink after a wash or two. Great purchase for the price if you only want a few wears out of them. Black-and-Blue Pair: The quality was much better. The black-and-blue pair had a little more “suction” and elasticity, they were just as comfortable, and they were much longer. I can wear them with heels and they’ll be long enough. The picture shows how they looked right out of the package, which is very different from the black pair. Hope this helps!

I purchased two pairs. First time I wore them they were great. I washed in cold water (no dryer) and the first pair shrank 5 inches! I actually got our a ruler to measure. I'm 5'3" and they're so short now that I could only wear them with flats (and they'd still be a little too short IMO). Probably great for very petite ladies. Anyone over 5'3" should avoid unless you want a crop palazzo. Otherwise, they were cute and comfy.

I put above that these run a little small, and I'm not sure if that is correct. They fit and are comfortable, but they don't lay as flat as in the photo. The waistband makes the stomach area puff out a little, so the fit isn't as flattering as I'd like. However, the color is beautiful. A little brighter than shown on the Amazon page. Those look more like Burgundy. I don't mind- I love both colors. I am almost 5' 8", which fits like in the image on Amazon- a few inches above the ankle.

I put these pants on and thought, I’m washing these up and wearing them tomorrow. And I did. Then I got home next day and thought, I need more colors. They are the best pants for anything. I’m 5’4” and weigh 115. I wore a small in the short. It fits perfect. Nice material. They hang nice and while you certainly can wear them for yoga, I’m wearing them for anything, everyday, or going out. No iron. Wash, dry, and wear.

I RARELY do reviews BUT these are my favorite shorts EVER! I am so pleasantly surprised how these fit. They are SO comfortable, cute, flattering and have great stretch! I’m a mom of 3 and these are the perfect length and fit for my body! I got the black ones too and I love them (they aren’t a high rise but still fit amazing)! The high waist is perfect and is not TOO high. I WOULD BUY THESE OVER AND OVER. I am 150lbs , 5’7” and I ordered a medium. Perfect fit!

I rarely ever buy clothing online, especially from Amazon. But I have to say these jeans more than me my expectations. It was worth the wait. I’m 5’10 & 72 pounds. I ordered the size 14 because that was the largest size they made and kept my fingers crossed. I absolutely was not disappointed. I might even buy another pair. They look so great.

I rarely give 5 stars but I love these pants. These are fantastic pants for warm weather! The material looks more expensive than it is and they feel great. I was nervous about the cream but they don't show through at all as long as you're not wearing dark undergarments. They are quite versatile and look great with a jacket for work or a tighter, shorter top for weekends. They feel like pjs on, and I work a lot from home/on the road so that's an added bonus. I wash them in cold and hang them to dry, and they don't require anything else. I also packed them for a trip (I travel a lot) and the wrinkles came out easily while I showered (I hung them in the bathroom and let the steam work on them). They are perfect for travel because they're so versatile and easy to care for. Also...pockets!!😃 I did order up based on the size chart, and I'm glad I did because I have some junk-in-the-trunk and I didn't want these to be tight and show off my flaws. I typically wear a US large (12-14)/Asian XL in pants and I ordered the XXL. They fit great. I could've worn the xl but I like the loose look of this size-- it is exactly what I wanted. For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape. They're long enough for me, which means a petite lady may need to hem them.

I rarely write reviews but had to for these. Since COVID-19, I've put on some weight and none of my pants fit anymore. I don’t want to spend a fortune on new pants, and decided to give these a try. These are definite dupes for Lululmeon On the Fly pant, at less than a third of the price! I read one review that the fabric is too shiny and was a little worried, but when I got them, they were not bad. They are very comfortable and flattering and I love the Melanite color (dark gray). I'm 5'0" 115lbs and the S fits me perfectly. I don't like the drawstring so I cut them off, and the waistband still functions. The only reason I didn’t give 5 stars is because when I first received these, they have a chemical-ly smell, but after airing out overnight the smell is almost gone. And I did notice a line running down the right inner thigh area that I know I didn’t make since I’d only tried these on, see picture. The line is not that noticeable since I got a dark color, so I’ll still keep these. Also, the pockets do gape open a little and stick out a little bit. Otherwise these are perfect!

I rarely write reviews but this one is worth writing about- I can’t say enough how perfect this jacket jacket. I am 5’4 , 130 lbs and the small fits great. I don’t plan to wear sweaters under it but if you do, size up because the arms are very fitted.

I rarely write reviews, but these are so bad they deserve the effort. I was really looking forward to receiving these as the design looks comfortable and flattering. Not. At. All. These are made of a stiff, cheap cottony material. It doesn't drape well and just looks cheap. Cheap, I'm saying. Cheap. The sizing is HUGE (I'm 5'2", weigh 130lbs, ordered a M and these are enormous on me, they literally reach the floor). But worst is the sewing/waistband as the photo shows -- puckered, bunchy, weird, ill-made and just ugly. Do not waste your money by ordering these or your time in having to return them -- unless your goal is to look like you just fell off the pickle boat.

i read a few comments about these overalls and i was still skeptic about them like one other customer stated she had been looking for overalls for 15years ive also been looking for about the same years if not more ive always had trouble finding clothes n my size womens pants never worked for me im 5ft 7in currently weighing in at 181lbs so i looked read looked read and looked and on this product i ordered xl hoping they werent too big considering im not an xl my usual pants size are either too big in the waist and just right in the length or vise versa but these are WOW i slipped these on and didnt want to take them off they’re so comfortable and roomy they stretch im glad i got the xl cause a large wouldve been too short in the length now if you’re my size more or less i suggest you get xl for comfort and length the crotch area is a bit low but i just might buy more n other colors truly one of the BEST things ive bought from amazon and ive had a lot of disappointments from things i’ve purchased but not these i urge anyone who wants these to try them out you wont be disappointed unless you get the wrong size again thank you

I read a lot of other reviews before purchasing, mostly about fit. I have an hourglass shaped build at 5'2 and 190-200 lbs. The fabric is soft and not super stiff like denim can sometimes be. The cut of the skirt is super flattering, even if you have a belly. I could see this looking especially nice on a top heavy or straight framed body type due to how it flares from the waist. The belt is of a nice quality and the "metal" (feels like a really hard plastic) on the belt and buttons are a nice antiquated brassy color. The buttons are purely decorative and not at all functional. I got an XL based on my measurements and it was actually a little roomy, but tightening the belt fixed the issue. The belt loop has elastic in the back so it does have some stretch to it. I would definitely recommend knowing your waist/hip/leg measurements to make sure you get the right waist size, but also because this is a fairly long skirt, especially if you're short. The color is accurate to the listing (but looks lighter in my pictures). I bought this for a specific event but like it enough to keep it in my regular routine. I would say it's worth the price and would probably recommend to others.

I read a lot of reviews saying how bad these are and sizing is off. While I agree, I followed the sizing guide and ordered a size smaller than I usually wear, and they fit perfect. I'm in love with these. So, do not hesitate to buy them. They are extremely flattering and FINALLY a pant for my tall girls!!! When I sit down and then stand back up, I don't have to tug and pull on my pants!! For size reference, I am 5'7, 120 pounds, short torso, long legs, 28in waist, and about a 30 hip. Ordered a 1.

I read a lot of the reviews so that I’d have a better understanding on what size pants I should order. I bought them in a size large and they fit me so nicely. I don’t typically wear jeans, I never liked having to button my pants up so I usually just wear leggings. I primarily dress for comfort. I absolutely loved the way they looked on me. My favorite part about these pants is that you don’t have to button or zip them up because the waist is stretchy and you can tie them however tight you’d want the waist area to be. I don’t feel restricted in these pants, they’re not too long and they’re not too short either. I’m 5’6 and weigh approximately 170lbs so I’m very glad I went with a size large. I definitely recommend getting these jeans and order them in your actual size. I don’t see a reason why you’d have to size up with these jeans. I want to get them in other colors! EDIT: To add an update: So I’ve bought these pants in most of the colors they offer and I still love them. They are easily on my list of favorite jeans to wear. They are just so comfortable, I can squat down and the jeans move with me because they’re so stretchy! Absolutely fantastic jeans :)

I read all of the description before buying, and was so shocked that these are pull-on pants (no fly, buttons are for looks) that I almost sent them back immediately. However, they were so flattering (husband approved) that I decided to keep them. They really should specify in the description. But give them a chance - these are real jeans, not jeggings, and not your grandmother's elastic pants! This 56yo size 12 has to wriggle into them (but jeans stretch as the day goes on, you know), but they're comfortable and look great. (Sorry, photo is at the end of a long day.)

I read all the reviews and went into this knowing that these are not high quality. What gave me hope was the curvy girls I saw that loved them. For reference I'm 5'5", 36/27/40 measurements, 138lb. The good: I don't have a gap in the back. (this never happens!) The length is perfect. (also never happens) They are a little stiff but mostly comfortable. My thighs and butt have room to breathe! The bad: The distressed look on these is so tacky. I'll try to mute it by using dye. The seams are awful and could need reinforcement in stress areas. Look at the belt loop placement. Couldn't be bothered to make them symmetrical. The hardware is cheap looking and you'll need to rub a wax candle on the zipper to prevent sticking. Overall if you're athletic build/curvy these are so worth it for the cut alone. I very rarely find pants that fit me. I want to cry. When these inevitably fall apart, I'm going to take them apart and sew a higher quality copy of them with fabric that has stretch. This is such a relief after years of skinny jeans and manufacturers that pretended we didn't have bones, muscle, and fat on our bodies. With those very human things, you can also have a small waist! I'm mostly happy and glad I bought them. Took two stars off because they won't last long and I shouldn't have to dye them and resew the belt loops right out of the box.

I love that I can put my wallet and phone in both pockets and I don't feel like the pants are being dragged down. The color is a good medium without being too bright but not faded. I really like the look of these jeans. They have a nice relaxed feel but are perfectly fitted in all the right areas. I really like how they fit along the waistline - fitted but not tight and the high-waisted design and straight leg fit are just perfect. For reference, I'm 5'6", 145 lbs, with a 28" waist and 38" hips, and the Medium fit perfectly. The fabric is made of a nicer denim material than I expected, and I love that they have a bit of stretch to them. It makes them so comfortable. I really like the material and the fit. I’m not a fan of the wide leg bottoms (hence the 4 stars) but at least the top fits and they are long enough for my 5’10” height.

I really like the pants! The only reason I gave it a 4 is because the buttons seem a little cheap and will likely fall off in a few wears. Faux back pockets. But I love the length. I’m 5’2 and they fell exactly where I wanted them to at the ankle. Good material. Definitely keeping.

I would’ve kept it, and saved it for running errands and gotten a larger set for traveling but if you see in the picture the pocket on my right was flared out, and no matter what I did it wouldn’t flatten out. The other was fine and I’m assuming it’s just a defect so I returned it with the intention of ordering the same size and a size up. Again, totally a preference thing - I am really picky with sleep clothes, and fly long haul overnight flights. This is a PSA for anyone who feels the same. For reference, I’m 5’8 and a 28” waist. The length was great, I ordered the 31” inseam :)

I also don’t dislike them, still trying to figure out if I’ll keep or return these! If you are worried about looking wider/bigger because of the style I recommend pairing it with a tight fitting top. I really like these jeans! I'm 5'2", 120lbs, 28" waist/34" hip and ordered a size 27 in Blue-4. The lay-flat measurements are as follow: 13.5" waist, 10.5" rise, 25" inseam (so pretty close to what the size chart says). They're made of a soft, stretchy material - I usually prefer non-stretch denim, but I like these because they don't feel thin or cheap, and they're sturdy enough to hold their shape. The online photos make them look like they're made of all cotton and have a button fly, though neither are true in real life. However, I still found them to be very comfortable and flattering, and they're not too distressed, which I like. They're more of a skinny/straight leg fit than I expected, but the waist fits perfectly so I don't think they're too small. All in all, I think they're a very versatile style of jeans, and I'll enjoy wearing them. Please note: be careful when washing these. I threw them in the dryer in hopes that they'd shrink a little bit and get a little tighter, but they shrank vertically instead! I lost at least half an inch off the length of the legs. Not a big deal when you're short like me, but someone taller might have an issue with it.

(I’m usually an 8/10 in pants) I originally bought a large, but since I’m pear-shaped (W: 27.5”, H: 42.5”) they were tighter in the legs than I was wanting, although the waist fit perfectly. I thought maybe I just got a bad batch, so returned them and reordered. Unfortunately, they were the same 30.25 inch length. I contacted the seller and they said to return them and try again in a month or so to reorder. I will do that, and certainly hope that they corrected their quality control issues, and produce the pants in the stated length. It would end my endless search for a comfortable pair of nice looking black slacks.

These pants shouldn't be worn tight because the seams don't look very strong. They are otherwise very breathable and comfy. Some folks might find them to be a little short. I really like these pants! They are warm, which is just what i was looking for. I really like these pants! Ordered a Large to begin with, but DANG! BEWARE! They run SMALL! For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & apx. 140lbs with 30" waist & 40" hips & the large was SNUG in the waist! For better comfort I went with the XL & I'm happier! LOVE the length! That was one of the BIGGEST reasons I went with these! The reviews said they WERE longer, and they ARE! LOVE that I can wear heels! YEAH! Only issue I have is the material! It's more a thinner cheap polyester so I took off a star for that! Would have expected, for the price, for them to have been a higher grade material! The pics made me think they were too! But, all and all, I like them & will keep them! LOVE the pockets! Just be aware that if you want that look they show in the pictures, I'd go up TWO sizes! You can tell from the pics, even with XL they are a wee on the tighter side showing ALL! So, I think I'll be adding some shape wear!! But, for the price and the LENGTH I recommend them!! Hope all this help!! Thanks much! ENJOY!

I really like this jacket….mostly. I love the color, and it does look and feel like real denim. I like the length of it as it fully covers the booty. Now what I don't like is that it has a drop shoulder, and it feels really big and loose. I read a lot of reviews saying to size up if you want a boyfriend-jacket type feel and size down if you want it to be a little more snug or form-fitting. I just went with my regular size (2XL) as I didn’t want either of those things. I just wanted it to fit like a regular jacket would. But it still seems to be really huge on me and the with the drop shoulder, it can look like I’m swimming in it. I had to roll up the sleeves by a lot so that the sleeve isn’t hanging off my hands. BUT - it really is a cute jacket and goes with just about everything, so I’m keeping it. For reference I am 5’4”, 220lbs and regularly wear a size 14/16, depending on the brand. I can wear an XL or 2XL but generally wear a 2XL.

I really liked these jeans until I started wearing them all day. They stretch out SO MUCH, and the waistband is much lower than I expected for a high-rise jean. I’m short so I knew the split hem would be too long, but rolled up they look great. Since it’s a raw hem, I might just cut them off. I’m 5’0”, 148lbs, and normally wear an 8 short in GAP or Abercrombie jeans. I ordered an 8 and they’re actually too big! They’re best for curvy figures; roomy in the waist, hips, crotch and butt. Plenty of stretch and wiggle room. I’m afraid if I sized down, they’d be too snug in the thighs. The fabric is really soft and feels similar to Abercrombie or American Eagle denim, but unfortunately the waistband feels a little scratchy and uncomfortable. Another issue is the back pocket placement. Instead of lifting my bigger booty up, they tend to pull it down and flatten it out. The back pockets are super low! Size down if you’re between sizes, or size up for a looser fit. Hope this helps! 🙂

I really liked these pants. They fit well and were very comfortable. It is difficult to find clothes that are made of 100% natural fibers, so I was willing to pay to get what I wanted. These pants arrived at my house on October 18th 2020 and on May 4th 2021 the khaki pair ripped on the inner thigh. As you can see from the photographs above, the fabric is threadbare! For $30 a pair, I expected a little more longevity. Extremely disappointed and feeling cheated. Update: Unfortunately I tried one pair and liked them so I ordered a number of others in different colors. On June 13, 2021 the Navy pair ripped.

I really love these Levi stretch jeans. I have other Signature by Levi Straus jeans that I have purchased on Amazon. They all fit amazing. I am always a 10 short so that’s the size I ordered. These are definitely comfortable but are a bit longer in length and not tight around the ankle as shown in picture. I think maybe I could wear an 8 short in this style. I am considering buying another pair in the smaller size because I like this style and I am really curious to know if they run slightly big. Overall, It is a great stretch jean.Update on 11/25/19...I ordered the 8 short and am wearing them today. Not truly happy. I thought sizing down from a 10 to an 8 would reduce the bagginess and that the ankles would have the skinny fit as shown on the model. And no there is no “skinny” to these jeans. I attached a picture of the 8 short to let you see where the are baggy in the thighs and ankle. Don’t get me wrong, these are very comfortable but I wanted the look portrayed on the model. ☹️ And I do love the Levi Strauss& Co. brand.

I really love these pants so much. They are super comfortable and can be dressed up or casually. Differently buy them in different colors. I have the black one at the moment. I got a size large. I’m 5’5 and 175lbs for reference.

I really love these pants! They fit great and are very comfortable and flattering. The pockets on the back are a nice touch because I love wearing leggings but sometimes wish they looked more like real pants. The back pockets do just that by making them more versatile pants that can be dressed up or down in a lot of styles! I like the shiny black leather-y kind of look, because it just adds a little touch that differs them from other black denim jeans or jeggings I have. The quality seems great so far. I'll update over time if they wear out quicker than expected or something of the sort. The only downside for me personally is that I ordered the wrong size. I normally wear Large for bottoms (5'4" 185lbs). But, I've gained a few pounds recently and wanted a bigger pair for my closet because even some of my large pants are feeling tight lately. I thought I'd go up a size and get an XL in these just to have a pair that fits me good now. It turns out they're a bit bigger than I expected though, because I feel the looseness of them and constantly have to hike them up, whereas I think they would stay up fine if I'd gotten my normal size. So my suggestion is stick to your normal size because you don't want leggings/stretch based pants to be loose!

I really love this burgundy wine belt. I ordered it in the 38-40" size to fit my 40" waist and it fits both at the natural waist and with jeans (womens plus size 18w), so I would say it runs very true to size. The leather is top-grain, genuine leather, which is often difficult to find, and the style is pretty unisex. This isn't a super wide belt, which means it will fit into the belt loops of women's pants, making it very wearable by both men and women. The hardware is heavy-duty, and the antique brass color is very attractive. The belt comes with the tools necessary to adjust the length, which is a nice bonus. My daughters admired it as soon as they saw it, and we all love the tasteful but somewhat out-of-the-ordinary wine color. Good quality + great appearance = a winner!

They fit like leggings all the way from waist to knees, then flair out unnaturally. I expected the fabric to drape, but it juts out like a bell. After washing and gentle drying, the bottom hem flipped up most of the way around, making the pants a little bit shorter. I have long legs and the pants look too short on me. Oh, and there is no tummy control. I don't know what they are talking about.These are a medium and I am 5'6" and normally wear a size 6 (no hips)

I really wanted this to fit. It’s just a bit tight around my chest. I bought a large. I’m 5’1”, weigh 175lbs, and my bra size is 36D. It was only a little tight so I think the XLarge may be too big. I’m not sure if I’ll try the next size. I’m returning this one. Other than that, everything else about the dress would’ve been perfect. Just what I’d like, if it was a tad bigger in the top section. Oh, no pockets.

I really wanted to like these but they’re just not comfortable due to the skinny band at the top being a bit tight. The material overall is thin but heavy and feels soft and comfortable. I like the ribbed aspect and the pockets. They are short girl friendly (I’m 5’2” and the length is perfect). In addition I also feel like the material is slouchy under the bump which I don’t like. I probably won’t wear them to be honest.

I really wanted to like these pants! I mean, they look SO cute on the skinny model!! 😋 But, on me -- they just look frumpy and make my body look bigger than it actually is. 😝 Unfortunately, these slacks just didn't hug me in the right places. For one, I'm almost always bloated and this waistband was WAY too tight, as in -- tight enough where I cut a slit on each side of the waistband so I didn't feel like a stuffed potato. 😂 For reference, I'm 5'2, weigh <125 lbs., wear a DDD bra size, and have defined, slender legs. These pants accentuated my belly, made my booty look flatter somehow (still not quite sure why....?), and didn't do anything to help my legs stand out...at least in a positive way! 😝 Although very comfy and flowy, I feel like these pants made my legs look bigger than they are. In addition, they were just a tad little too long while wearing flats, and they didn't seem to look right with the two different types of heels I tried them on with. While I will say the material is quite soft and comfortable, I wouldn't rely on these pants to keep me super warm when I'm feeling really cold. They'd be best suited to wear on days when there's a light breeze. Again, while I really wanted to like these pants, unfortunately, they're not a home run for me. But, if you're rather slender with a small tummy, you might love them. ✌️

I really wanted to like this skirt! I ordered the medium (5’5, 160lbs) and while it fit comfortably at the waist, they were not kidding when they said this was a MINI skirt. It barely reaches my bum and any walking/movement makes it hike up. Aside from the length, the quality just isn’t there. For $31, I would want at least a thicker material (especially since they market this as “cargo”). Some stitches are already unraveling and I haven’t even washed it! In my opinion, save your money for another skirt.

I really wanted to love these linen/cotton blend pants. However, the size medium pants I received hit me just at the ankle, and I am worried washing them is going to make them very short. These are also a little tighter fitting in the butt and thighs than I would ideally like. For reference, I am 5'3" and 145 lbs. I debated exchanging for a size up but instead decided to return; based on reviews these pants are all over the place in terms of fit. Color wise they are acceptable, although a bit brighter orange (like creamy tomato soup) than the stock photo. I was hoping the more muted color on the model was true to real life.

I really wanted to love these pants because I like their style and fabric but unfortunately they look unflattering on me. I’m 5’7” 150 lbs. I have relatively slim hips and thighs. My waistline is my trouble area where I carry most of my extra weight. I usually wear pull-on style pants in size large. These pants in size large are little too big on me. The waistband is too loose even with the adjustable drawstrings. When I walk it keeps loosening and I have to tighten the strings frequently. There’s too much space in the hips. I know these are not supposed to be tight fitting but on the model they fit in a relaxed yet flattering way and me they look frumpy and they make me look thicker as well as shorter than I am. I forgot to take a picture from the back but that’s where they look the worse on me. Also, when I’m sitting there’s too much fabric in the hips level that bunches up unflatteringly in the front. These are not for me but I’m still giving them 4 stars because I think that if you can find a size that fits you correctly, they could actually be nice, casual, breathable, lightweight pants.

I really wanted to love these pants but was a bit worried they wouldn't work out. For me there were several things working against them that ultimately led me to return them 1. They make you look much thicker. I think it's a combination of the lining and outer layer, but they do add some thickness which was not the look I wanted going into the new year 2. One the black pants, the white lining shows significantly at the top of the waist (which I would have covered with a belt), but also at the bottom of each leg (which I had no option to cover so this is the primary reason for returning them). 3. They're short... I'm 5'8" with a high waist. I was so excited that these actually did hit my waist (which is uncommon) but disappointed when I looked down and noticed how short the legs were on me... which means when I put shoes on they would be even shorter.... Whole these didn't work great for me (I opted to return them), I think they could be a hood option for someone shorter. They are comfortable and not itchy

I really wanted to love these pants. The fabric is lightweight and flows so perfectly & the color was such a great vibrant red. Would be perfect for work or a night out. Unfortunately they are definitely more for a taller person or someone with a longer torso. When pulled up to where they looked good on me the butt was fitting very oddly & unflattering. For reference I am 5’, 140 lbs with hips and an butt & I purchased a medium.

I really wanted to love these. I'm really into this style of pants this summer, so I had high hopes. I ordered them in Orange. They looked more rust toned in the picture, but they are bright pumpkin orange. Also, in the picture, they fall just above the ankle, which I loved. In real life, they run quite large and pool a bit at my ankles (I'm 5'5"). I ordered Large, which is my usual size, but I wish I had ordered them in Medium. They might have looked a little nicer. They just feel too long and billowy. They're okay, but I don't love them as much as I hoped.

I really wanted to love this set. I have been on the hunt for a comfy and flattering lounge set that I can wear in public and still look put together. I am 5’7”, size 8/10, and ordered a medium. The fit is great….minus the length. Def not a set for taller girls…as I consider my height pretty average and these are even short on me. I will be returning. The washing instructions seem rigorous and the fabric is likely to pull, so don’t think it’s worth upsizing to hopefully get the right length.
I really wanted to love this, but it’s just not flattering on me at all. The pockets make my hips look massive and it’s to small at the bust. It’s also an odd length on my 5’2 frame.

I really wish these jeans came in petite sizes. I'm 5 foot 3 and 110 pounds. I knew they would be large on me, but they are so stinkin' cute! The size 4 will work, but I'll need to find the right belt to hold them in place without bunching up the waistline. I wanted a slightly baggy fit anyway. They are also a little longer than I'd prefer, but they don't drag on the ground when I wear shoes. I have always been a fan of the button fly, and this one looks really nice. It's high waisted, so it could be long in the crotch if you decide to size up like I did. There is plenty of pocket space in the front and back. The rear end on my pair has a slightly faded look, but it's not dramatic. I know I've said some critical things about these jeans, but all of the problems I have can be attributed to the fact that a smaller size would fit me better. The jeans themselves look fantastic, very fashionable! If they can look this good on someone who doesn't exactly fit them, imagine how good they will look on a person who does!
I received so many compliments! They look small, but they definitely have a good stretch! They run a little long, but nothing heels can’t fix. I got a size med and I’m 5’3

I received the bell bottom jeans with a belt. I love these, they fit as expected. I got the size i normally wear and they look really good. I love that the material is a bit stretchy and allows for movement while wearing. My only complaint is that they are way too long for me even with shoes. I am 5’2” and will need to hem these to not ruin them. I wish there was a petite option, but I love them enough that it doesn’t bother me. I would recommend.

I received the order 3 days early, but not only was there no receipt in the packaging, but there wasn’t a tag on the item, there are 2 holes in the leg and the previous owner obviously washed them before returning. Linen gets a bit rough when improperly washed. This pair is rough and shrunk so short they’re above my ankles and I’m only 5’2. Any company sending used product should clearly not be trusted. Amazon wants me to send the pants back but I fear they’ll get sent to another customer.

I received these jeans today. First of all, they are NOT Classic Fit as described on the Amazon website (see pic). I am 5’6” and 124 lbs, and I have a small waist, wear a size 4, so I don’t like Relaxed Fit. Also, I ordered medium length, so if they shrink, they will be too short. As stated in some of the other reviews, the fabric quality is not consistent with this brand name. I have Lee jeans that I have worn for years and I still wear them. They are just very faded from so much washing. I will return these and see if there are better jeans out there.

I received these pants stained. The proportions are off, too. I’m 5’8 and the legs were the perfect length so I could see how they’d be long on other people. The crotch made it look like I had a diaper on because it was loose and baggy but cinches in at the top of the thighs. I’m 170 lbs and got a size large, they did button, and the legs fit fine, but the waist area is just weird. Don’t know if it’s different for different colors (or skinny ppl)

I received these today and couldn’t wait to try them. I am a curvy 5’ 7”, 190 pounds, wearing a size Large as recommended. The color is just as expected, and the material is comfortable and drapes nicely. I tried these with heels (with white blazer)and felt they are definitely acceptable. However I prefer a longer inseam with heels (personal preference). Now, with flats, sneakers, or sandals this length is perfect in my opinion!I saw a couple of comments of disliking the hook closure rather than a button. I didn’t mind it at all. It does lay a slight bit different, but nothing a belt does not disguise. That’s why you have belt loops. Haha!I believe these are a beautiful addition to your work and office wear. I wear a uniform for my occupation, so will enjoy these mostly in a more relaxed everyday environment. They will get plenty of use.

I recently got a pair of these grey (“pepper”) 100% cotton sweatpants. I decided to size up to an XL in case they shrink. Ordinarily, I wear a M or L depending on the brand, and for reference, I am 6’ tall and weigh 150 lbs. These sweatpants are medium weight, seem well made, and have a relaxed fit that is very comfortable. The waist is slightly large on me, but the rest of the fit is perfect, and there is a drawstring to keep them in place. I usually wear a 32-34” waist, and these are a bit bigger than that, which makes sense because I sized up to an XL. The pockets are medium to small in size, the length is good, and I expect these will get a lot of use.

I recently had barriactric weight loss surgery. I wasn't really sure of my size so I got a size 14. I am 5'7" and weigh 185. I still have a bit of a tummy. These fit so good. They move with you without feeling like you have to keep pulling them up. I am buying more because of the stretching I can lose more weight and still wear them.

I recently lost the vast majority of my clothes in due to Hurricane Florence, and buying pants is always difficult for me, so I wasn't really looking forward to replacing them. I'm 5' 7.5" and I've always had a small waist relative to my hips and butt, and when you add that combo to long legs?! Fuhgeddaboudit when it comes to finding pants and jeans with the right fit...or so I thought until I tried these babies on. To terribly exaggerate, once I easily fastened them, colors were brighter, lights shone down from the heavens while denim-tuxedo clad angels sang Ginuwine's "In those Jeans!" In the real world, I made my husband stare at my hips and butt as we both marveled at how amazingly these jeans fit. I even had jeans left at the ankle, and that is HUGE for me. I got 2 pair to try on through Prime Wardrobe and I'll be getting all available colors now that I'm aware of this magic. Thank you to Levi's and Prime Wardrobe for changing my life! And get yourself a pair of these jeans if any part of my story sounds like your life.

I recently ordered a pair of capris in size 12, and I’m happy with them. They fit perfectly, comfortable and the quality is good. I recently ordered the EVALESS women's casual wide leg work pants high waisted straight long palazzo trousers, and while they look great, they don’t quite compliment my figure as I had hoped. I think I would have been happier if I had ordered the light gray instead.

I recently purchased the Kensie Ella High-Rise Straight Leg jeans in a size 14, which is the largest size offered. For reference, I am 5'3" and weigh 195 lbs. I usually wear a size 16, but these jeans have a lot of stretch, so the size 14 fit me quite well. The fabric is soft and stretchy, which made them comfortable to wear. While I'm getting used to the straight-leg style, I do find the jeans to be flattering overall. However, I noticed some minor issues with the front, where the fit isn't as smooth as I would like. All in all, these jeans are a nice addition to my wardrobe, especially if you're looking for a comfortable and stretchy option. I recently purchased these cargo pants in a size medium. As someone who is 5'5" and weighs 160 pounds, medium is generally my go-to size. If you’re considering buying these, I’d recommend sticking to your usual size. Overall, I’m happy with my purchase and would definitely recommend them to others looking for versatile and comfortable cargo pants!

I swore I ordered a 28inch waist, but received a 27. When I got it - just in a bag, I instantly thought I would have to return it. We all know the drill and pain of trying on pants… but the 27’s slid over my hips and then buttoned up. I could have even gone with a 25, I think. 🤗 I am 5’3” 150lbs - 28inch waist, 41 and 43 inches on the high and low hip, and a 30inch inseam. These jeans hug my thighs but are not a struggle to get on. I also have really big calves, so this is surprising. My hips are easily accommodated with the zipper and button. Once on I have a tiny <2inch back pant gap which is easy to hide or covered by wearing a belt. So that’s the almost perfect part. Had I gotten a size 25 or a high waisted pant I do not believe this would be a problem. On the other hand I have a short torso and my rib-hip length is <4-5inches so I’m always hesitant to get a high waistline pant for this reason- as it shortens my appearance by a foot. My butt looks good, but it does not lift it. My husband thought they wear nice on me… I haven’t washed them yet - I’m just happy to have a pair of pants that fit well enough and flatter my appearance. Should they shrink or unravel in the wash I’ll amend / update this review.

I think I’m giving these a 3.5, I went back and forth with the star rating. These are very comfortable pants. I wasn’t sure whether to go with the 0 or the 2, and the short or the regular. I’m 5’3.5” and my go-to Jean is a size 1 in Celebrity Pink. I ordered the Size 0 Regular. They seemed a tad long for me (I think I like the shorter length on me in tighter jeans) but they were perfect with rolled cuffs. They are definitely more of a relaxed look than I am used to. The legs were looser. I liked the rise and it was high enough to contain everything but I don’t think it gave me a flat butt the way some high-waist jeans do. Great lighter color. I think I would have preferred the shorter length, but with the looseness of the pants I’m not sure that would’ve looked better. So I’m ultimately glad I went with the 0R. If you are on the smaller side of a size 0, you may find these too big, but that’s what free returns are for. The thing is these pants STINK. They smell so bad. It’s like formaldehyde meets moth balls. I thought after a wash the smell would be gone but nope! These still wreaked after a wash. I’m not going to return them and hope that a few more washes will get the smell out, but if you’re sensitive to that, I’d skip these pants.

I think this cargo pocket style has potential, but they look better on the online model than they do on me. For reference, I am 5'7" tall, 130#, and middle-aged! First, the material of these denim shorts is good quality. It is thick and stretchy. They seem like they will be durable shorts for lots of wearing and washing. I would not call them "high-waisted" necessarily. They are not low-rise, but I would have expected the waist to be higher to be considered "high-waisted." My waist is not nearly as toned as the model's, so I have some bulge at the waist in these shorts. If they truly were high-waisted, they could cover my problem area ;). I sized UP to a Medium since they were advertised as stretchy, slim-fitting, and it looked like the models were all wearing them sort of tight. I guess I'm glad I did size up, as that loosened the waist a little, but it made the bottom of the shorts' legs pretty wide/flared like bells. I don't like the "bell" look of the legs, and would rather the legs be straighter. But if you don't want the waist to be tight, I'd recommend you size up as well. Because the waist is small compared to the rest of the shorts. I also wish there were 2 basic, flat, normal jeans pockets on the back, because I feel my butt looks bad with the no-pocket style. The length is a plus, IMO. Many denim shorts are either super short or too long, and I think the length of these (maybe approx 2.5" inseam) is perfect. Thank you!

I think this dress is a great throwback. It has that classic western style that put Wrangler on the map. The snaps have a pearlized look that adds femininity to the design. The front pockets are functional. The sleeve length is generous. The rounded hem hit me several inches above the knee. It was just shy of being too short for me. I got the large expecting it to be a little loose from the waist down since I'm busty. The fit overall was a bit snug. I got the snaps done, but there was major gaping across the hips and bust. I would feel slightly self-conscious wearing the large. I'd definitely need to size up. For fit reference, I'm 5'6", 162lbs and a 34G bra size. The X-Large would have been ideal for me. Thanks so much for taking the time to read this review. I hope the information that I've provided makes your decision a little easier.

I thought because these were leggings they would fit tighter in the thighs, hips, behind & ankles. I also thought they would be longer in length & rise. But they are much too large, in those areas & too short for my body type. Because the rise is too short, they fit below the belly button, unless you want to pull them up & have a camel toe. As a point of reference, I’m more on the tall & thin side at 5’9”, 128lbs, 28” waist, 36” hips with thin thighs & not much behind. The fabric is soft & comfortable, but just not a good fit for me. They were so inexpensive that it doesn’t make sense to send them back. So I will donate them instead.

I usually never buy clothes online because I'll never know if they will fit, but I'm so glad I took a chance with these jeans. Amazon was recommending I go with a size small because I typically wear a size 28 (size 6 or 7 in Old Navy), and I'm so glad I went with a medium based on existing reviews and the minimal difference between size small and medium according to the size chart. I'm a runner, just shy of 5'4'', and my pre-pregnancy weight was around 138-139 pounds. I'm now 18 weeks and my weight has only gone up about 4 pounds, but I was desperate to find some maternity jeans as I was getting increasingly uncomfortable from the pressure resting on my lower abdomen, especially when sitting in a car. I normally wear size 28 (size 6 or 7 in some brands like Old Navy). The inseam length of this size medium is perfect. No area on the jeans feels too tight or too loose. I love how high these sit on the hips because it feels like they aren't going to slip down. I expect there may be some minor shrinkage when I wash them so it may feel a little bit tighter post-wash, but they seem stretchy enough to continue feeling comfortable. I think these will also be awesome postpartum jeans. One thing I was concerned about was that the waistband wouldn't be supportive enough, especially as I get closer to the third trimester, but I simply decided to purchase a separate maternity band for extra support and to wear with workout pants or dresses. Overall, this is a great buy and I recommend for any mamas that are feeling uncomfortable!

I usually wear a 5 or 7 depending on the brand of pants but they always squished my butt and my hips. They felt super tight around those areas and they'd usually tear eventually from there. If I went sizes higher, the waist would be very wide and the bottom leg area very baggy. I needed pants that fit my body with a little extra room on the hip and butt area. I finally found them! I'm 5'7 and 137 lbs. I ordered a size 7. Lengthwise they land right at my heel barefoot ALMOST touching the floor. If I put shoes on it's a little higher but it looks good. They fit perfectly on me, they hug my body but I don't feel like a stuffed sausage, I feel completely comfortable in them. I love them, especially that they're high waist, it helps keep your body snug and in check around the waist so you won't get the ugly muffin top look. I don't know what to say about the butt lift effect because I just wanted room for mine to fit comfortably but if you have the same problems I had I definitely recommend. Oh yeah they ship really fast! They came from Florida to west Texas. I ordered them around mid afternoon Friday and the order was shipped an hour later. The expected delivery date was Wednesday and it arrived today, Monday.

I volunteered to work 4 days at a PGA event at an LA course and although I wear a lot of black pants, the long ones were all starting to get worn. I waited weeks for this to arrive and was super excited b/c these a great fitting jeans and the price was awesome and then.... these royal chakra blue jeans have the worst dye lines across the pelvis.
