I’m 5’4” and not sure of my weight because I don’t weigh myself anymore. But I’m guessing I’m 140-150 range. The material is SOFT! not scratchy in the least. Very flowy and perfect for when it gets hot again. Can’t wait to wear them this summer. Update: I love the white so much, I ordered and just received the red. It’s definitely a brick red which is what I was hoping for. Beautiful color! Soft and flowy just like the white. Love them!!

I was worried. But these came out being my new favorite pants! They are amazing! They stretch so they aren’t tight on your tummy, but have amazing hold to make your butt look good. I’m 5’8 so the length was good even with the booties I have on now.
I washed before the first wear and the deep color didn’t fade. This is a solid denim purchase. Stitching and seams are good. Flattering and comfortable. I ordered an 8 regular. I’m 5’6” and 158lbs

I wasn't aware of the "destroyed" hem style when I ordered it, so I ordered a replacement ... which, of course, was styled the same way. I'm getting used to it because this denim dress is super cute!I'm 5'7", 135lbs, 32DD and the fit is perfect for me, right down to the 3/4 length elastic-ruffled sleeves. Yes, it smells like denim used to smell, but that'll take care of itself in time.I really am happy with the dress. I ordered the gray and am probably going to order one of the blues soon.

I wasn't expecting it fit quite as snug to the body as they do but am pleased just how flattering they are. I'm 5'10 and they fit great. Hug all the right places in a loose-ish form fit. They fall at a great ankle length for my height and look cute when rolled. Women on left and right are wearing the same overalls in different sizes. Left is a small. Right is a large
I wasn’t expecting these to fit as good as they did! I love them and will order more in different colors. I got so many compliments on them. I am 5’4 and 132lbs. I ordered a Med and they fit great.

I wasn't expecting top quality for the price, but these were so awful in so many ways that I don't think they're worth $1. The material feels very cheap and there isn't much stretch to the fabric. There's also these very odd puckers of extra fabric on the outside of the hips and on the inseam in the crotch area, which looks ridiculous. I'm 5'4", 140lbs and got the large size capris because I wanted a looser fit, but these were slightly snug in the waist and loose around the thighs, especially with that extra pucker of fabric. Also, these were not capri length on me. I have short legs and a long torso and, as you can see, they go down almost to my ankles. I'd have to roll them up many times to get the length I want, but the fabric isn't stretchy enough and is too snug around the lower legs to do this. Finally, there are back pockets, but the front pockets are fake. And be mindful of the tag that warns about bleeding dye even after washing. Given all of these faults, I wouldn't recommend these to anyone. There's really nothing positive to say about them. Don't waste your money.

I wore for my best friends birthday and wore it to a concert. Buy it if you love it. I bought a small I'm 5'2 and 130 lbs, its a little baggy but a super cute throwback style.

I wasn’t going to order these because I kept seeing that they ran really long but figured I could return them. They arrived today and I LOVE THEM! They fit me perfectly and are super soft almost satin feeling. Im 5’7” and weigh 140. Ordering more!

I wasn’t sure about the high rise at first, but these Joe's Chloe Utility Wide Leg Pants quickly became a favorite. The deep chocolate brown color is rich, and the comfort stretch twill fabric is both soft and stretchy while holding its shape beautifully. The utility pocket detailing adds a cool, urban edge, making them versatile enough for both work and casual outings. At 5'8", the length is perfect, allowing me to wear both slight heels and flats comfortably. True to size and high quality, these pants are a fantastic addition to any wardrobe.

I wasn't sure about these until I got them. I typically like to buy higher end jeans but these are a great quality and good fit! I am 5'2 and about 140 pounds and I ordered the sized medium which fit well. They are meant to be cropped which is the new trend I guess. I am trying to get on board with these trends. I think where they fall on my right at the ankle line is probably a bit off since I need a medium at 5'2. But that is an issue for me, not for the jeans! Know your size and where you want the jeans to fall. Otherwise, the material is lovely- it is softer and has a little stretch which is nice compared to most other jeans. I would recommend buying these!

I wasn’t sure how fake leather pants from Amazon would go… But I actually love them! They fit perfectly, and are nice and stretchy. I only wish they were high waisted. For reference, I’m 5’4 and weigh 125, and I bought size 3.

I wasn’t sure how the quality or fit would be, as I feel like Amazon can be hit or miss. However, these were totally a winner for me. I got beige and it’s really a pretty color. The material seems really thin at first... like “can you see what color underwear I’m wearing” thin. However, they're not see through and don’t feel too fragile. I’m 5’0” and these were wayyyy too long for me. I cut probably 5-7 inches of length off the bottom and they are still just above the ground. They don’t have much stretch at all, but are still very comfortable. Even with those things considered, I love the fit and the style!!! I get so many compliment. They’re arguably my favorite pants. I just hope they hold up overtime.

I wasn’t sure if this outfit would look good on me but I was pleasantly surprised. It’s loose and comfortable. Fabric is breathable and light. Has pockets which is convenient. The wine red color really complements my light brown skin. It fit me just right…true to size. I’m 5”3’ so the length was just right. It’s low but doesn’t drag. Works well with wedge heels or flats. Ran it through washer and dryer with no issues.

I wasn’t sure what size to get. A lot of people saying it was too big others saying it was too small, I just went with my gut. I’m usually a Medium but I’m so tricky because I’m 5’1 and curvy. Current weight is 150lbs, bra size 36D, jeans between 28-29. I ordered a Large hoping it would be a nice lose fit like the model. The Large is perfect! The dress has a pretty significant gap in between buttons in my opinion so this size helps with the gaps not opening up and revealing my body parts or inside garments lol. It is thin and a bit see through but not horrible. The length was just what I wanted! I can’t wait to wear it. It was a bit smelly but I wash all my clothes before wearing them so that’s the least of my worries. A bit pricey for the quality yes, but the fact that I didn’t have to go shopping in the search of a denim like dress pays off. I know a real denim dress would cost more and weigh more. This is light weight and works for any season! Also, the fit exactly what I was hoping for, it arrived the next day and saved tomorrow’s event. If you wanted to be fitted it will be tricky to find the right size for you unless you have a stick figure body. This is meant to fit like the model in my opinion! I’m happy with my purchase. I also want to mention that I have pretty big arms and this size is comfortable for me. Also, excuse my dirty mirror, I have small kids. Thanks for reading.

I wasn't sure what to expect from these but I have been looking for a nice midweight wide leg pant for work and thought I would give these a try.I really like the fabric, it is a good midweight that I can wear in the winter without freezing to death. The fabric has a sleek feel to it and the texture is somewhere between a jean and a knit pant, it's a very unique fabric. The fabric moves well and feels great on, it also doesn't seem to attract pet hair which I love since I have cats and always struggle with keeping black pants pet hair free.Unfortunately, these do seem to run a bit small. I am 5'7" and 155lbs with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size M and L. I got these in a size L which says it has a 32-33" waist because I really don't like my waistbands tight. As you can see this waistband was pretty tight, not uncomfortably so, but I definitely have a bit of belly bulge going on here. The waistband has enough stretch that when I sit down it's still comfortable but not the most flattering.There are functioning pockets on the front and back. They are slightly smaller than normal pants pockets but still work to fit my wallet or phone (I have a Z4 fold).These pants are comfy and look cute if I have a shirt on that doesn't show the waistband. I really do like them but wish the waistband was an inch wider. I also think they are a bit pricey for what you are getting. However, overall I would recommend...just size up and don't go by their size chart.

I wasn't sure what to expect with this, but took a chance and I will actually be ordering a black one. It is so soft and I don't know how it stays put but it did, all day long. The band itself is a very comfy, very soft material. Reminds me of dry fit type material. It comes with two button extenders too. One short and one long. I am 5'3" and 193 lbs. I am 11 weeks pregnant and this one pair of jeans was a little too tight before my pregnancy and right now are not comfortably button-able. But other than that they still fit. This band allowed me to use the extender and cover the jeans so I didn't feel like anything was showing or falling down. The top tummy band is wide and I had no issues with it falling down at all. The bottom has an elastic under to keep the bottom of the band in place. I was concerned with the top falling down that at one point I flipped it, but it honestly works the best as it was intended! Trust the product! :D

I wear a 10-12 and got a large. I am about 5’4 and length was to the floor.

I wear a 10/12 and ordered a 12 and these fit perfectly! I am 5'3 and with no shoes they are on the ground but not crazy long. I need maybe 2-3" heels for them to sit off the ground. I love the checkered pattern on them! They have a great stretch to them and didn't get baggy when wearing them the first time. They are high waisted and not super thin material. Overall these are adorable jeans!

I wear a 14-16, 220 lb, 5'6 and I ordered an OXL. These pants are incredibly roomy and stretchy. I believe anyone would be able to size down one to two sizes (the waist stretched to twice its width). The fabric is ribbed, almost a corduroy feel except in stretchy polyester. The length runs long; I had these pulled up all the way to my bra line in order to walk in them. They are very comfortable but I believe would snag quite easily. I ultimately returned them, due to the length and the fact that, in spite of the extra fabric, I could still see my lumpy cellulite on my bum.

I wear a 2X if I want a top to fit against my skin (which do not). I wear a 3x if I want a little room. I'm a 38DD. This 3x is a bit tight around arms and chest for a 3x - it kinda clings to upper back and bra roll (ya, I said it)... but length, swing, belly and butt coverage are perfect. I do love it. Material is a light knit... and is not wrinkly nor clingy.

I wear a 2x/18w and I'm 5'7", this xxl fit perfectly. It hits about 2‐3" below the knee and is ideal. The fabric is quality denim and the way it was wrapped allowed me to shake out any wrinkles and wear immediately. The elastic waist band and the tie allows me to continue to lose weight and wear during this process. I look forward to wearing again. This time I paired with a tank top and a sweater with sandals; next wear I will go with a blouse and a scarf tied at the neck with some 2" heels. As with a favorite pair of jeans, this will prove to be versatile.
I wear a 6 petite and these fit right for me. Just know I usually wear petite and am 5’4” 135ish. Nice to have an alternative to white pants in the summer.

I wear an 18/20 and I originally ordered the 3XL and had to returned it for the 2XL. Big back and large bra size? This is the outfit for you! The top portion is wide, so if you are wide at the bottom and wide at the top it will fit. If you have smaller bra size then it will gape at the top. The stretch is just right not to stretchy and it won't lose shape after wearing it all day, but stretchy enough to be comfortable. For reference I am 240 pounds, 40 inch waist, 50 inch hips and 45 inch chest (bra size 38L). I typically wear a 18/20 and the 2XL fit me perfectly.

I wear professional dress for work and have lots of dress clothes. Took a chance on these and I’m so glad I did. They feel and look very high quality. The XL long in dark green is what I got. The color is rich, the fabric is super thick but still drapes nicely because it’s kind of a smooth/slippery/dressy weave. I’ve never had this particular fabric but I like it for dress pants. I’m 5’7. Not sure if the wide leg and pleats would work as well on shorter people, but in size long they drape nicely, especially with a bit of a heel. The color is rich. The length is long without being ridiculous. The pleats camouflage the hips and the elastic in the waist means no gap, which is usually a problem for me. If you are wide in the waist it might be too tight. Love the options for thick or thin material. Overall I’m really impressed and plan to get a couple more pair in other colors. *I think they actually look better in person than in the photo I posted.

I wear S and am 5'7". These pants are super super wide in the thigh. I wish I loved them because the material and pleating is spot on but just too wide. Looks like clown pants. In other reviews the wide leg looked like it was lower. Use caution - it seems like not all pants are made the same way. If you have long legs they also come up too short. Just not the right fit :(

I wear size 6-8 in jeans. I first ordered these in size 8 and loved the fit and the faded whisker-look along the top. After walking around in them for a few minutes, I felt them sliding down a bit. We all know that jeans will continue to loosen throughout the day so I opted to exchange for size 6, which is what I typically wear in KUT from the Kloth corduroys. The fit was great though the whisker fade was much less prominent in the newly exchanged pair (dang it!) The photo I've included is of the size 8 jeans. I like the mid-rise fit. It feels secure and flattering around my mid section but still hip and stylish. I also like the ripped look but not so ripped that I feel like a teenager or homeless person. I have long legs (34 inseam) and these cropped in the perfect place on my calves. Love them!
I wear size M. I’m 5’1”. I got short 6 and it’s definitely true to size. So flowy and comfy. a win for the petite short girls! Yay!!

I wear these almost every day all winter. They're great for nordic skiing and great for under tunics at work. They run two sizes small--I usually wear a medium and have to wear an XL in these. Not too long with heel boots. I loved them all day. Until I spilled something on it and I spot cleaned it. The spot cleaned up fast. I took them off and noticed a circle from where the water had expanded during the cleaning using soap and water ONLY. I threw into the wash and when it was done the circle is still there. Clearly you cannot spot clean these. I’m so disappointed to have wasted my money on a item that is now not available to wear again with a huge circle created from a simple spot clean. Warning to wash before first wear would have been nice. Or you risk the dye going away with spot clean or rain. I’m so upset and nervous to use the two other pairs I bought also.

I went with the size 3/32. I weigh 121 lbs and im 5'1 they fit great and they have some stretch to them. The length is perfect if I wear boots or cork heels. They do drag, but if I would've gotten shorter length they probably wouldn't look right on me. Hope this helps.

I went with the size chart & reviews of people with my height & weight....they just seem so long on me. They looked great on the reviews. I'm 5'3, 140 & I ordered a large. I had to flip my waist band down and they're still really long. I suppose oversized is what I wanted🤷

I WFH so I’m always trying to find comfortable but cute clothing, these are perfect. I bought 3 pairs :) I’m 5’6” 130lbs originally bought a medium they were really cute just slightly loose around the waste so I bought 2 more in a small

I will definitely be ordering more!! Listen I am 5’6 and can never find any work pants that fit my legs all the way down #thestruggleisreal but these.. oh baby! They are FLATTERING! COMFORTABLE AND PRETTY WARM !! Listen I dislike going to work and being uncomfortable and I think these are definitely gonna motivate me just a little more everyday to get my a$$ out of bed !

I will preface this by saying I am 5’4” & short-waisted, but these are not mid rise, but high waisted. While the leg is full & flowy (for denim), the waist is very fitted. I am between a 10-12 depending on fit, I always size up to be safe when ordering online. These fit but are tight through the waist. People slimmer than me won’t have this issue. If you are in between sizes, definitely size up. The denim is nice, but no stretch. I feel like a little spandex is put in a lot of pants these days, these are listed as 48% Cotton, 36% Rayon, 16% Polyester, so no give. The model has a cuff in the listing, but the material isn’t stiff enough to hold a cuff. If you want a cuff, extra steps will be necessary. As I am short, I was looking to cuff too. They are a smidge long on me, but not as much as I thought they would be, so they may be short in taller women. That might be a cute look, as long as you know what you’re getting. Love the patch pockets, but I do wish they didn’t have the buttons on the waist band, as I would like to wear with a belt sometimes. Overall, a cute look that is something a little different.

I will say first that these look nothing like leather in person, however, they are still cute. I'm 5'4 and a size 18 in womens and I got these in a XXL. They fit me just how I'd like. They go past my ankles and rise about 2-3 inches above my belly button. I haven't had any issues with rolling or anything like that. They're not super thick or super thin, there is no see-through of my derriere and above all they are pretty comfortable. Now with all that said I absolutely hate the pockets on these! They're shaped weird and they look even more weird when you have the leggings on. Whenever I wear these, I wear a top that covers the pockets. I would opt to get the version with no pockets. The other unfortunate thing about these is that I don't see these holding up for a long time which is crazy considering the price. I would say that when purchasing these don't expect them to be an everyday type of legging. Think of these as something to be pulled out semi-occassionally that can be dressed up for the impromptu date night or hanging with the girls when you want to be comfortable and cute.

I will say that the sizing of Baleaf leggings is inconsistent. I am 5'7" and 125. I usually wear a small but ordered mediums because I feel like they retain more heat when they aren't skin tight. I got regular fleece lined in the Army Green and water resistant in Cocoa Creme.Regular fleece - the fabric is 75% polyester and 25% spandexWater resistant - the fabric is 77.3 polyester and 22.7% spandex.Water resistant has a tie inside the waist and the regular has no tie. The water resistant also has an additional small pocket inside of the left pocket. The water resistant is larger in general overall but the leg length is about the same on both pair.I held both of them under the tap for a three-count and, much to my surprise, the water resistant pair was, indeed, water resistant. The regular fleece absorbed the water immediately. If left to sit for a minute, the water resistant pair did absorb the water, the same as the regular fleece. The crotch of the water resistant is a single seam. The crotch of the regular fleece is gusseted. Small pocket for a key is in the back on the water resistant; front right of the regular.In my opinion, the regular fleece feels and looks of higher quality but if you need water resistance (but not waterproof, which it's not) I'd get the water resistant pair. Also, I wish these came in different lengths. They are about an inch too short for me.

I will start off by saying I have not naught a pair of jeans in over 5 years. I liked the thought of these being stretchy so I gave them a try. Instantly putting them on they bring out your curves and suck in the unwanted areas. I feel amazing in these pants. They have good stretch to them. I am 5’ 4” around 245 and ordered a xxl.

I will start with the positive - these are a nice weight, have pockets, and the paper bag waist is cute. I was however looking for a long pant and not one that hits several inches above my ankle like I’m expecting a flood. I bought these to take on vacation to the Turks and Caicos in February and was super excited when they arrived, but that’s where my excitement ended. If you are 5’7” or taller and looking for a short pant, these are for you.

I wish I bought these months ago! I’m 5’7” 160 prego and I’m comfortable for the first time in months!! I was worried I’d have to stop working earlier than I wanted because of being so uncomfortable in my other jeans, that’s no longer the case. So happy and very relieved. I got a size L
I wish i got the pants everyone else is talking about. I got paper thin leggings that were baggy me. I'm 5'7 and 150 pounds and ordered a SMALL. You're better off wasting $22 at taco bell.

I wish I had longer legs. Other than the length, these jeans fit me great. I ordered a Medium, which is my normal size (I’m 5’4” and 120 lbs). The length is about two inches too long. I noticed the inseam measurement in the sizing chart shows 31” for all sizes, which is kind of unusual, in my opinion. The cotton/spandex material feels a little stiff. They’re machine washable so hopefully they’ll soften after being tumbled. The 3-1/2” waistband offers some support. The pants have classic denim stitching at the seams, front and back pockets, and hem. They’re pretty comfy and ideal for casual days and nights out with friends. I’m going to find a seamstress who can adjust the length because I can’t wait to wear them with my favorite pair of boots.

I wore for a Christmas party with my boots and snowman scarf. 138lbs 5’6”. Size medium fit perfect. Length good with boots. I wore these all day and I gotta say they are COMFY! I ordered a 4 and I'm a 26-27" waist and they fit really well, I probably could have sized down for a tighter fit on the waist without issue. Obviously the cut of these pants allows for a lot of movement but the fabric stretches and bounces back with ease. I could squat and goblin walk in these and be totally comfy while doing it (we all know that's the true test). I wasn't sure about the aesthetic of these but I got multiple compliments from coworkers and they really grew on me. I did notice that the white threads started to fuzz up by the time I got home. It seems like they may have used cotton thread for these accents which would account for that. My only hesitation is that I wish they were an inch or two longer, but I'm 5'9 and they're definitely long enough. That's why I removed a star, but I think for *most* people the length will be absolutely perfect. These are honestly really great and the quality exceeds the price. Much better than similar pants I've gotten for more $ from popular retailers

I work in a saw mill and have lost over 60 pounds in the last 2 years and it's been so uncomfortable trying to find belts I don't have to fight with or belts I don't break within the first months time. With me being a female suspenders just don't work for me. I started using Bungie cords to try and keep my pants up while I lift logs all day long and after more weight lose they werent working for me either. My boss told me about adjustable belts and options outside normal belts you can buy at Walmart. So...I found these belts here on Amazon and ordered them. I wore one all week last week and love it. Depending on which pants I wear and how high up and low they fit on my waist will depend on if I have to wear the metal piece that comes along with the belts. Most times with me wearing my pants below my belly button I can hook these belts to the front belt loops and won't even have to use the piece of metal to hook the belt at the zipper line of my jeans. I love not having to fight a belt or constantly pulling up my pants or trying to constantly make adjustments just to work and keep my pants where they need to be. I would definitely buy these belts again. I'm a 29 inch waist and I tightened the belt as tight as possible and it keeps my pants up and keeps it comfortable. I don't have to worry about my pants dropping. 🤣 I would definitely buy these belts again.

I would have never bought these because of my body type until I saw an influencer style them. So many compliments, so comfy but can also be worn to work. Forgive the caution sign, lol. I am 5ft, 160lbs

I would say that these pants are comparable to the Time and Tru brand capris walmart has, but maybe a bit thinner than those. They're stretchy, comfortable and fit as expected. I'm 5'3 and with 200 pounds, xl fit great. Due to the material, cellulite can be seen a bit (but that has more to do with me than the pants lol). I got the olive color and I got compliments at work for them. The reviews stating that the inner thighs area can rip, well yeah, if you have thick thighs I'd say a year is really good life span considering the material isn't thick. We need to be realistic. Anyway, they're great! Price is still great for what you get. I'm short and the length is really nice.

I would say the feel of it is polyester. It is stretchy but very wide on the hips. I ordered a Large. I have 33” waist (postpartum), 43” hips. I think the band should be a bit stretchier. I would say these are true to size. The material is very lightweight. At 5’4” these are a little longer on me than they are on the model in the ad who is probably closer to 6 foot tall. They were a little baggy in the upper thighs for me as skinny jeans, but they would’ve been more comfortable for me if they went higher in the back. It seemed like I was pulling them up in the back once in a while. Nice for knocking around the house or running to the store.
I would say to size up then the measurements that it shows comes a little small and fit me good enough but a little small, I ordered the 32-34 waist.

I couldn't give it 5 stars because the waistband is made of very thin elastic so it kept rolling down all day. A wider waistband would be awesone. I am 5'4" and I wear a 14/16 or Large in work pants from department stores.

I’d been on the hunt for a pair of green cargo pants that were short girl friendly and didn’t cost a lot. These fit great and are flattering. I’m 5”2 and you can cinch them up a bit at the bottom and I wear a 12 and got a large.

I'd give it 4 stars since it is indeed warmer than another fleece lined tights I tried. I ordered Small because other reviews state that the extra small is too tight in the waist. However, the small doesn't fit me perfectly (5'2" about 105LBs, 33Hips). A bit loose around legs thighs, knees, and ankles. The length is perfect though. so it's petite friendly. I would have still kept it but there's a hole in the back of the waistband. Brand new and already has a hole?! Can't say that gives me confidence in its quality. Also, the pants really attract lint. Keep that in mind if you have pets. Oh, that fashion logo is such an eye sore. Whose idea was this?!!

I'd give these 10 stars if I could. I'm 5'2", 114 lbs and the Small fits me perfectly, and I mean perfectly, even the length. I will not have to hem these at all, so they may be more cropped on someone that is much taller. That may not be an issue if you plan to wear these tucked into boots.I really like the look of the darker blue wash on these that have a little fading in the right places. The lining is soft and warm. Overall these jeans are extremely comfortable. The jean material is very soft, not stiff with the right amount of stretch which makes these fit so well. The button and zipper both work easily as expected. There is no gap on the back of the waist band that I encounter with some jeans. The stitching looks great and is uniform throughout. The front pockets are deep enough to use and not just for looks. The back pockets are lined with the same fleece as the inside of the jeans which was a nice surprise.I am very happy with every aspect of these and know these will get a lot of use this Fall and Winter.

I'd give these a 3.5 stars. I should have noted that the photos used for the black pair do not show the backside and are strange looking pictures. The pair I got looks like you sat in something like ashes...it's washed out gray and weird looking on the parts you sit on. Can I live with it? Not sure yet. I ordered two pairs and got these first. The second pair I ordered in army green and are two weeks away. I will make a decision then. In the meantime, I will dwell on whether the distressed washed out greyish backside is too strange. I can't stop seeing it...my eyes just go there. Like a lot of black jeans, they have that weird chemical smell. Will it go away? Maybe? Probably? Not sure yet. Next thing: the video shows a black pair with hardly any distressing...which is not what I got. I read tons of reviews and decided to get my usual size 4. Because they are wide legged...not a problem fit wise anywhere. And I have read so many reviews these were long. I am 5'5" with long legs and these hit me a couple inches above my ankle. I would not call these capris. I would call them cropped, which is what I wanted. These were a little snug in the waist, and again...nothing like what was presented in the video which made them appear to be really stretchy. There's some. The other rather unexpected thing is the big wide, bell flare. They don't look that wide in any pictures. So that was the critical portion. Here's the positive: They are well made. Have some stretch to them, are on trend for today and a reasonable dupe of the Billabong Free Fall for about half the price. The front pockets are real functioning pockets and the back pockets are in the right places. These can be dolled up or down depending on your mood. Still on the fence about being ok looking like you sat in something and the super wide bell bottom.

I’d say it really N.A. a little the big, although everything’s a little big when you’re only 5’0”! But loose, nonetheless. I like it. It looks put together, the material is fine, and for shorter people like me, it’s nice that the bottoms of the pants are tapered like a jogger style at the bottom, so you don’t n the d to shorten. I still had to roll them a little at the bottom, but hat was hidden by the excess draping. I may even get another color bc this is perfect to wear to work (as shown in my photos) I recommend

I'd say size up. I'm 5'7" 270 and typically wear an 18w in jeans. I ordered these in a 22w and they fit pretty well. It's a bit looser than I like the calf and ankle part of my skinny jeans but these are comfortable in the thighs, material feels strong, and I can bend and kick in them easily. The button is right above my belly button.
I’d suggest sizing up one size. I did and it was still tight. But good quality and cute jumpsuit. I’m wearing a 2x With heels. I’m 5’3

I'll admit, as an elder millennial, I've been hesitant to stray from skinny jeans. These jeans helped to push me along to the next style level lol. Love that they're high-waisted so I don't have flashbacks to the early 2000's flares lol. I am short- 4'11", somewhere between 120-130lbs. Typically a 4 to 6 in jeans. Ordered a 28 short and they fit great. Inseam is a little long but woth heeled boots, they're perfect.

I’ll be adding photos of these on me soon. For now, I want to say I absolutely do love them, and want to wear them all day, every day. They have nice, deep pockets and are a nice weight Jean. True jeans, not some weird fabric. My only complaint/regret: But, I used the sizing chart, and knowing how wonky sizing can be on Amazon, I ordered 100% according to their size chart since I KNOW my measurements. They are HUGE. 😅 I’m swimming in them but they’re so comfortable that I still recommend them, just size down accordingly unless you’re really looking for that “painter/carpenter in overalls” look (nothing wrong with that!). I wish I had gotten a small or medium, and I got a large. My weight is 130 and I’m 5’2”. Also have to cuff the bottoms and have the buckles pretty high up and tight. I would love to be able to try the right size! The color truly reminded me 100% of painters overalls (brick), they are a burnt orange. If they had fit, the color would’ve been better. However, i decided to overdye them using RIT dye in Wine for about an hour. They took the dye beautifully and turned out a very deep red. I’m going to continue to dye them to keep them from getting too faded! UPDATED REVIEW: After having them for a little while, I wanted to add some notes. They are soo comfortable and I find myself wanting to wear them all the time. However, the pockets have Never laid quite right and this can really affect the way they look on. The front pocket bunches and leaves big gaps between my body and the overalls. The side pockets don’t want to fit correctly in their slots because due to the buttons pulling on them on each side, and the buttons aren’t even functional. I wash these by hand and hang them to dry, and even after steaming them, they still don’t look the way I want them to or as they are in the model photos. Maybe if the size chart had been more accurate I would’ve gotten the correct size and perhaps these issues would resolve themselves.

I'll start by saying I hate buying clothes online but the size chart was dead on. I'm 5'9" so they hit right at my ankles which is fine for wearing with flats. My only complaint is the inseam of the jeans in not straight. I hate pant legs that look like they twist. The right leg is worse but neither are straight. I've experienced this in even expensive jeans and it's disappointing but I'll still wear them. I like that they have actual pockets. The waist is smooth and does not dig in. If the inseam was not twisted they would be 5 star pants.

I'll start by saying I hate denim pants. The texture is rough & usually gives no room for stretch. This is not the case! These jeans are sooooo stretchy & soft like butter against the skin. Not to mention I'm 5'6" & I have a hard time finding pants long enough for me. I selected very long, size 31 & I love them. I just hope they don't shrink too bad after several washings, we'll see.

I am being forced to wear jeans at work (outside construction) I’m used to leggings and sweats, but I love how much these jeans stretch because I have to do a lot of bending and I bought other jeans that start to fall once I start sweating and y’all know there’s no pulling up jeans in the heat so yeah I needed comfort and room to move. These have both and more. I bought the size 28 L, w39, L32 There’s no zippers or buttons and also no real front pockets but I still love them, sounds weird to say that because before this, i don’t know when was the last I wore jeans at all. Im 5’9 and these go all the way past my ankle and it isn’t too short if I sit, ankles are still covered but I’m not tripping either. I’m going to buy more colors and y’all won’t be let down with these. - unpaid testimony from V xD

I'll start by saying that I am a TTS 6/28 in bottoms and 5'3 in height. I went with the L because I wanted a looser fit. I definitely could've gotten a M but I wanted to size up. Would recommend, they are soft and comfy, and great for lounging around or running errands.

I’ll start by saying that the fit and comfort of these jeans is outstanding. Plenty of stretch and they fit true to size. I love where the length hits me at 5’4”. I’m bigger on the bottom so usually have a waist gap but it was all good here. The color is Reed Blue and I am in a small. Usually wear a 2/4. However, I feel that the particular color and wash was not flattering. We love a distressed look but not when it is highlighting your bum. I feel like other colors may look better for my particular body shape. I like them enough to buy a different color that is not so distressed in that spot but will be returning this color.

I'll start of with the fact that I'm 5'4" with a 29" inseam and about 185 lbs. I carry my weight in my belly and thighs. I purchased these to wear while walking my dogs in the winter. I bought the L\XL size. They're a bit long on me, but that's fine. The fit is great. Not too loose, not too tight. No muffin top. In the photos, I pulled one leg down to the middle of my foot so you get an idea of the length. They are high waisted, roughly 3" above my belly button (I'm touching my belly button in the one photo). The waist is a wide one and I haven't had any problems with it rolling down at all. The material is a little shiny but more knit than one of those slick athletic fabrics. It's a thin fleece too. I think I was expecting more of an athletic legging with thicker fleece. I just walked the dogs. It's 42F outside with a 10mph wind. My legs stayed warm. Hoping these hold up in colder temps. If not, I'll just throw on some silky long johns underneath too. Product was received on time as ordered.

I’ll start off by saying I’m 5’3” 142 lbs and 25 weeks pregnant. I got these in a size medium 8/10 I believe. They are EXTREMELY comfy and flattering. They are also just the right length not too short not too baggy (I have longer legs smaller torso). They are very stretchy and I could have definitely gone down a size, but I’m not sure if that would effect the length. Overall I’m very satisfied and highly recommend these pants to get you through your pregnancy and postpartum. Like I said LOTS of stretch especially in the waste, so I’m confident that these will carry me through the rest of this baby making process.

I'll start with I'm 5'4 170 with curves. I first purchased size 32 as recommended by Amazon and the sizing chart. They were large enough I could pull them off without unbuttoning them. Sized down to the 31 which is the size in the pics. The fabric and the top part of the jeans is the only reason these jeans get 2 stars. The waist sits nice and when I sit it doesn't do the stupid gap in the back. I think the cut and sizing all works well until you get to the calves and the inseam. These are long. So much that I had on 4in heels and they still were on the floor if I dared wear them with the heels. I was nervous my heels would get caught in all the excess fabric of the calf area. I get straight leg but at 9in wide (when the thighs are 10 there's some taper) I felt like I was in the oh so popular JNCO jeans. So I tried with tennis shoes. Again my shoe could get lost in the leg causing a trip hazard. So I arolled them up twice which only caused more bulk. If you're not statuesque or planning on cutting these into shorts I wouldn't recommend. But if you're going for that emo hot topic 90's baggy look go for these, though Levi's does have a 94 throwback that may be better for that.

I’ll start with saying I love the way these jeans fit, now that I exchanged them! I’m 5’3” and weigh 139lbs. I will add, I’ve recently lost a lot weight so Im not sure what size jeans I would normally wear now, as these are the first new jeans I’ve ordered since. I had read the reviews stating these ran large so I ordered a 6 and ended up exchanging them for a 4. Now they fit perfect (aside from being a tad long) and feel great. They do look different than the picture (color is lighter and tears are different) but they’re still cute! Overall, I still love them!

I'll start with the positives and those are that these pajama pants run true to size and I ordered my normal size of large. They are also quite high waisted and long. I'm 5'8 and these are nearly too long. However, the quality is pretty bad. If I saw these in a store, I would not buy them. They are thin/transparent and within 1 wear the stitching in the crotch area came undone and created a hole. I would expect a lot better quality for $17 so these are a miss.

I'll write a review because I know it can be for other ladies on a quest for good jeans. I've probably ordered 10+ pairs on Amazon and these are the winners. My measurements: 28" waist. 37" hip. 30" inseam. About 5'3". 133lbs. Athletic build. 30 yr old mom of a toddler. These jeans stay up without a belt. They are mid rise just below my belly button. I gave up on low rise cut jeans awhile ago because I hate having to yank them up every hour. The Levis website says these are relaxed through the hip and thigh. But if you are an athletic build and occasionally do outdoor chores in these pants, I found that they fit great and still flatter. I dislike skinny jeans because I prefer to wear my pants over my winter/fall boots without looking awkward. These bootcut jeans are a rare find in the skinny jean saturated market. Material is medium weight cotton blend, not paper thin. Decent cold weather jeans. I may wear leggings underneath if the temps drop lower in NH. Hope this helps another lady who needs "do everything" jeans.

I’m 11 weeks post partum and struggling to find clothes I feel comfortable in. Pre-pregnancy I was 115 lbs and a pretty standard size 4 in bottoms and small in tops. I’m right at 135 now and unable to fit in most of my clothes, but have been hesitant to spend too much knowing ultimately I’d like to lose some weight. I’m probably between sizes 6 and 8 now depending on the brand and material. I am 5’4” for reference. Best guess on my current bra size is a D. Sharing all this just to make it clear, I don’t really have a “normal” to use as a size comparison right now. Also offering up having a newborn as an excuse as to why my mirror and room are such a mess lol. After reading reviews I ordered a medium, and I’m glad I did. I think I would have struggled to squeeze into a small, and a large would have been too big. I know not to expect top of the line quality from most Amazon clothes, especially in this price range. The quality was about what I expected, not terrible but obviously not top of the line.

I'm 120 lbs and 5'7" and typically wear a slim size 8 for a small waist - these jeans fit me well. The leg length is good as I prefer a pant that covers my ankles - these do. The fabric is a medium weight with a lot of give. These jeans seem to recover their shape ok without getting too stretched-out and droopy. Looking at the inside, the sewing is good but not what I would want for tough work. The pockets are sewn to stretch along with the outer fabric, as they should be. The zipper works ok, not real smooth initially but will likely ease-up after some wear. The button/rivet at the top of the zipper is fine but the buttonhole is a bit too tight. These are decent light-duty to mid-duty denim pants that look nice and are comfortable to wear.

I'm 125 pounds 5'6, and I'm usually a 6 in pants, M for panties. These would have been perfect, if they actually had a lining. To me, if something is lined, that means there are 2 pieces of fabric: the outer layer, and the LINING. These leggings are one piece of stretchy fabric that is satin on one side and fuzzy on the other. These might be good for Texas, but not Michigan winters. I wear leggings to stay warm underneath my full length skirts (yes, even in winter). I chose nude ones because I don't like it when people can see my Halloween leggings when I cross my legs lol. Not sheer at all. I tried these on with black panties underneath and there was no show through. You can see panty lines tho, of course. 5 stars for opacity. The stretch is great. The leggings don't lose shape when worn. Of course, I only tried them on before returning, and didn't test the stretch for a whole day, but it seemed like they would stay good. They were thick enough to be flattering, as I have some butt cellulite and couldn't see it through the leggings. My big hangup is that they aren't lined. It's one piece of fabric that is satiny on one side, and fuzzy on the other. Not true lining. Returning.

I'm 5' 5" with a smaller bust/waist, but LARGE hips and a** (dictionary pear-shape), ON TOP of being plus sized. Even with belts, it's a struggle. These pants do not have a gap. None. Flush against my body all the way around, even in the back. I was FLOORED. I am a US18W (while my tops run closer to a smaller US16/larger US14) just to scale just how much of a size difference there is when it comes to waist vs hips), if you're built like a pear you understand how much of a pain the gap fight is. There's just enough stretch that even at my widest points, they're comfortably snug. Bit of a problem area in my belly after losing weight, front of pants fit comfortably without pressing into/floating over my lower torso area. If you're unsure about buying pants online because you have curvy-girl problems, buy the pants. Just put them in your cart. I bought two pairs of this brand, I'll be replacing all my old jeans with additional pairs soon. The struggle is real, ladies, let these pants handle your curves like a boss.
I’m 5’ 7” 140 pounds athletic build. I got a medium. Super comfortable very stretchy, good quality fabric. I don’t look like a boy in them. Lol.

I'm 5' 7" 164 lbs. Never find jeans to fit me - anywhere. I have a large waist with slimmer hips. These are buttery soft, with so much stretch that they mould to the body, but also "give," if that makes sense. Having no rigid waistband really helps. Still think they'd be great for smaller girls who could just downsize. They feel great and at last I have some white jeans. At the risk of sounding like a cheesy influencer, I'm definitely buying more.

I’m 5’ 9” and 250lbs. I just had a baby so I’m trying to look cute but not frumpy with my pooch belly. I love this jacket way more than I thought I would! I got a 3x and it it’s a little bigger on me, but I don’t want to button it, so so cute with a romper!

I'm 5' and 127 lbs, waist 33" and hips 38", and I ordered both medium and small. The small was way too small, but the medium fits very well. The waist of the jeggings comes up to my belly button, with no muffin top. Even when I lose a few pounds, they'll still be nice and snug. I just got them, so I don't know how they'll wash, but I'm not worried about it, even if they end up shrinking a little. I'm planning to dry them on medium heat, because I don't want to weaken the spandex. Unless they end up being flimsy, or have other unexpected issues, I recommend these jeggings. To answer some questions that other shoppers asked: - The blue jeggings look like jeans and have the orange stitching in the seams to look like regular jeans. The black jeggings have black stitching. - They both have rear pockets, with false front pockets. - Yes, they are long on me by several inches but, so what? It won't matter if I'm wearing boots, and I'll just tuck the bottom under with other shoes. - Both colors have decent fabric thickness (not real thick, but good enough), and it feels soft and comfortable. They are not see-through (don't know about the white jeggings). - I tried them on with different types of underwear, and I think that if you wear properly fitted panties (not too tight), there shouldn't be a panty line.

I’m 5’-8”, weigh 150 pounds and wear size 10 in AE brand jeans. I ordered size 12 in these since my waist is closer to 30 inches and that’s the size which the manufacturer’s chart recommends. The fit is absolutely perfect, and the length is also spot on. I’m beyond thrilled with this pair of jeans, because they fit and look great and I know they’re going to help me get through the cold winter months. They feel a little bit bulky when wearing them, but that’s to be expected since they’re fleece lined and I’m used to wearing thinner jeans. The front pockets are deep and useful (unlike some pairs of jeans that I’ve owned). The waist is high, there’s a zipper and one riveted button at the top and there are also belt loops. The back pockets are plain and completely free of any fancy stitching. The color is great and they’re very comfortable. The amount of flair on the bottoms is just right. These jeans are very well made and are very good quality.

I’m 5’10 and unrolled come to my ankles. Ordered xl and they are really baggy on me, I need a belt. But super comfy.

I’m 5’10 and was concerned over the length of these pants, but they hit just above the ankle and look good with different types of shoes. I’m 5’10 and weigh 145lbs, ordered size M and they fit perfectly! Made my b00ty look bomb and wasn’t too tight on my tum, even covering my bellybutton ring didn’t hurt. Tight in the right places and belled out, at the bottom which I loved. Will wear these in the future for sure!

I'm 5'10 just about 150 lbs. The best part about ordering these Wranglers is the ability to choose your waist & length. Long length is not an option in Most by Amazon sellers. I'm 5'10, 160lbs, size 8/10 long, straight hips, a lil extra butt & thighs. Ordered size 6 Regular. They fit great in length, had room for my thighs & didn't flatten my butt too much. They are thinner than expected, but felt comfortable with some stretch. PROBLEM: the crotch! If I pull them up where they're supposed to be & wear a belt-- they give obvious camel toe. I'm 5'10, I ordered a size 6-long. The length is great! It's just below my ankle, the inseam is probably around 32. Only issue I have is that I had to order a size 6 because they didn't have size 4-long so they're a little big. I'll deal with it, it's worth it to have pants long enough!

I’m 5’10” 185lb and ordered a size lx. The fit around the waist was comfortable and they definitely have plenty of room left to stretch, but they were too short. Came right to the top of my ankles which for me is not the look I was hoping for for this cut of pants. As a taller girl that’s the info I’m always scanning for in pants reviews lol. (Sorry for the terrible photo i tried to show the length!) Also I feel like the seams were sewn a bit off, both the fly and the back seam wanted to sit too far to the left and it seemed like the right side of the waistband hit slightly higher up on my hip than the left. It wasn’t crazy noticeable, if they were longer I could have probably made it work, but too many little things were off imo.

I'm 5'10" 210 lbs. This fits like a true size 18! The fabric is soft and stretchy making the fit even better. I love the lenght. Similar to a boyfriend cut jean or Capri. High waisted and wide legged! Very flattering. I'm going to add some lace trim around the hem for some added style! The stitching is is well down and the color is true to the photo.

I'm 5'10" 245 lbs I got a 1x which fit but didn't drape like I wanted so I got the 2x. Looks like photo and I have plenty of length for heels. I'm stepping on them in the photo. I’m 5’10” and 140lbs. Size small fit perfectly but they’re way too short. I’m so sad! I wanted these for a Dolly Parton party.

I'm 5'10" and 146 lbs. I got size Large Long and I'm super happy with the length. They even bunch just a little around my ankles which I love and can never seem to find, being tall. I'm 5'2" (157.28cm) and 158~ lbs (71.5kg) Ordered the black pair in the size 8, tag on jean says 8/29. Purchased these to wear to work, 10hr shift in fab & assembly. First Impressions: Stretch is good, thicker fabric kinda scratchy Legs actually look longer with the bootcut, does the curves justice. Solid shade of black. No back gap, might need belt. Fit : 4/5 Fit my legs and especially my thighs and butt (the biggest part of my body) comfortably but is a little looser one my waist/stomach. I could go down a full 2 inches on the waistline but nothing a belt won't help with. The waistline sits right at my bellybutton (35" (89cm) to the floor) and doesn't ride too far down when I bend or squat, I have full coverage. I don't know if going down a size would be the right move for me just because I want them to be comfortable while I'm working and if they're too snug will hamper my range of movement. Length : 4/5 They are a little long, would go with a petite size if possible, but they will be worn with boots so it's not an issue. If you don't mind the hems dragging a bit or some hemming work like me then you'll be fine. Work pants are ment to get dirty. Fabric : 3/5 Seems like a sturdy denim. A bit scratchy but that'll be fixed with an initial wash. They do have a little stretch so if you are in between sizes then you're safe to go on the lower end. Seams are solid and the fabric is thicker than regular fashion jeans. Haven't put it through a workday yet but they seem like they'll hold up. Pockets : 2/5 Better than most jeans but could be bigger. When it comes to pockets size matters. At 16cm deep, just barely over 6", there's room for improvement where there isn't for my phone, keys, wallet, chapstick, and work fob. Can't carry a purse around the warehouse, ya feel? Price : 1.5/5 I personally don't like spending more than $20-$25 on a pair of pants but I need something sturdy and that fits over my boots. I bought these on a 20% off sale for $40 instead of $50 which is the only reason I considered these. Honestly just feels like I'm paying for the brand and not the pants at this point. I did order a pair of Wranglers that were on sale for $30 (29% off $42) that'll be here in a day or two. Depending on how well that pair fits I may return the Dickies and get another pair of W's (or 2, for that price). All that being said, I have not actually worn these to work so if I decide to keep them and they hold up for a long while then maybe they're worth it. For a $50 pair of jeans they at least better last me 3-5 years.

I'm 5'2", 127 lbs. athletic build and got size 8. The 6 would have been good too but I like a looser fit. These jeans are GREAT, and are the first high waisted jeans I've liked (other high waists I've tried are stretchy through the leg, but the waist is too tight). The moment I slipped them on, you can feel that they are mostly cotton with enough viscose/spandex stretch. They got the right combination of materials here. Because of my height, they are longer but I wear them with 3 inch and up heel sandals and they make my legs look nice and long. I was able to kick off my heels and play with my daughter at two separate picnics and the stretch and fit makes them super comfortable. I'm thinking of what other color to buy in size 6, for those days I'm walking around with just my partner.

I'm 5'2", 138 pounds and a 31" waist. I ordered both the Small and the Medium, and while the small fit, it JUST made it through my first belt loop, so I kept the medium to allow for more give. Cute with jeans or jean shorts and dresses up a French tuck a bit. I may order this in black, as well.

I'm 5'2", 140 pounds. I ordered my usual size, Medium. They fit fine around the waist because of the stretchy elastic, but they are not capris on me. The legs come all the way down to the top of my foot. The color is definitely not maroon. It's terra cotta. They come out of the package with a faded, pre-washed look. Some people will like that, but it's not my cup of tea. To me they just look old and over-washed. They're also way too wide in the hips and legs. I understand that they're meant to be "wide-legged," and the width might be ok if you're over 5'8" and they're really capris on you. But on me there's way too much fabric. They end up looking baggy and ill-fitting, make me look dumpy. I'm going to have to take about 2" off the outside of the legs and maybe 6-8" off the hem. I washed and line-dried them before trying on, and they came out very wrinkled, which is a bummer because I'm not about to haul out the ironing board. Next time I'll try the dryer on low and see if that works better. On the plus side, they do appear to be well made and are a nice, light weight for summer wear. Final note: I am extremely sensitive to tags in clothes. I always have to remove them. This pair of pants, like so many others, has the tags sewn directly into the seam, so when I removed them it left a hole behind and a sewing chore for me to do. Annoying to have to mend a brand new garment!

I’m 5’2”, 175lbs, typically wear a size 12-14 pant and got these in XXL based on reviews. In the photos I’m wearing 2 inch sling backs. These fit perfectly & are nice quality. The wrinkles come out easily in the dryer, and they’re not the type of material that easily wrinkles. Lightweight, slinky feel. The only downside is the elastic waist on the back looks a little funny when you have a shirt tucked in. When wearing 2 inch heels, the pants are a good half inch above the ground. 3 inch heels would be perfect for someone my height.

I'm 5'2", fluctuate between XS and Small. These pants after one wash hit right at the bottom of my heel without shoes, so no drag. Not too tight at the waist which I really like. Comfortable to wear even with any bloat. The fabric is not too thick or thin. Pant legs are wide enough that leggings could be layered underneath during the winter. The fabric is an athletic material, and stretchy. Pockets are decent size. You can see the shape of anything placed in the pockets, so not too much structure. Stitching is fine but not great. I expect these to fall apart within a year or two. As you can see in the 3rd pic, there's already some pilling and I've only washed these twice so far. Overall, these are petite friendly and ok value for the money. This is the first time I've bought from this brand. I'm not converted into a brand advocate and am not overly impressed. Won't go out of my way to look at their other offerings. But if I want another pair of these or come across something from this brand, would also be fine with buying. Content with this purchase.

I’m 5’3, 120 lbs, normal build, ordered size small. Okay, so... my first two pictures should explain why these get only 3 stars. My husband and I both busted out laughing when I pulled these out of the package. They could easily be mistaken for a child’s pants by their size, until you turn them around and they look like a deflated pin cushion. But then I put them on, and was quite impressed actually at the material - I personally love this material for leggings, it’s the only kind I’ll buy. So kudos are due there. I immediately realized I should have ordered a size up to be safe (although the description/reviews say true to size). Granted they’re a size to small, I like how they fit (assuming they’ll be more impressive when I order a medium). The butt shaper detail is “okay” at best, it definitely gives some definition but the way the butt seam is sewn in makes it shiny so it looks like you’ve got a string up your crack. I’m not sure if that part will go away when I size up, but it’s not so bad that I’m not willing to risk it. I just recommend doing a “bend over” test before leaving the house in sunlight. Overall they’re alright, crossing my fingers a medium makes them 5 star. Key takeaway: SIZE UP!

I'm 5'3" tall, weight 150 pounds. This is a size medium. Plusses: They have the tailoring of the 1940s, with snug fit over hips and waist and flaring to the legs. The elastic on the waistband is wide and quite stretchable to get over hips to put on. Pockets are well made and have the placket to carry tons of stuff. The buttons are 5/8" across giving a nice decorative look. The pants are long for taller people, yet you can tie the legs to lift the hem above your shoes. Minuses: Wish the pockets were velcro closed as the buttons are hard to get through the buttonholes. And when unbuttoned, you can't see them at all. Strings on pant legs are long and could trip up the wearer unless double tied. Nice pair of cargo pants and will be my go-to for casual wear.

I’m 5’3”, 118 lbs and ordered 28 reg based on another pair of Skinny Levi’s I have. Based on the confusing chart for ordering, I wasn’t sure what I would end up with. I got a 28/32 that also says 6 Medium ( guess that translates to the “reg”). I would say the fit in this Vintage pair is comfortable, if not a bit roomy, but at least not tight or restrictive. Length is perfect. I ordered color StayPut, and the actual version is not as faded looking as the pic online, but I like what I got just as much if not better. I really like the flare (ready for a change from the skinny). I also ended up with the actual “vintage” version, which has really cute 3 row stitching on back and front pocket. I noticed at least one review pic had the traditional 2 row Levi stitching, so odd that you don’t know what you’re getting from this particular listing. Anyway, I am very satisfied with my purchase, with a very fair price for new Levi’s.

I’m 5’3”, 135lbs., normally wear an 27/28 or 6/7. I carry my weight in my bum/thigh/lower belly. I ordered a size small. Wasn’t expecting to like these as much as I do! They run a little on the big side, but in a good way. They are a loose fit and have a nice stretch, but they aren’t too loose and baggy. I would say order the size you normally wear; no need to size up. They’re totally comfy due to the stretch. They are def high-waisted. I don’t always look that flattering in high-waisted pants, but I feel good in these! My legs are short, so these look more like high waters on me rather than a capris, but I like the look with boots. But if you have short legs and want them shorter, keep in mind you may need to hem them. Planning to hang dry since they are quite stretchy and I don’t want to damage the elastic. They aren’t the best/thickest quality, which means they likely won’t last too long, but I bet they’ll be comfy when the weather starts to warm up. I was quite happy that I ordered these. We will see how they hold up!

I'm 5'3", 152 lbs, 32" waist, 39" hips, and wearing a size 31. The waistband is about an inch below my belly button; to me, this is on the lower end of “mid rise”. The widest part of my calf is 15", and I found it a bit snug in that area for a bootleg. These were my least favorite of the six I ordered under Prime Try Before You Buy. All jeans I selected were (supposed to be) 98%-100% cotton, and these were described as 100%. However, they are actually 93% cotton, 6% polyester, and 1% elastane, which makes them an automatic fail on that front alone. After losing 125 lbs, I’ve found stretchy jeans highlight, rather than conceal, the loose skin on my thighs. While they aren’t as bad as those with a higher poly content, they can’t compare to the 98%-100% cotton jeans, and thus, are going back. The jeans I tried were: Levi’s Women’s Premium 501 Original Fit Levi’s Women’s 501 Original Fit Gap Women’s High Rise Straight Fit Wrangler Women’s Cowboy Cut High Rise Slim Fit Tapered Leg Wrangler Women’s Real Wrangler Classic Fit Mid Rise Boot Cut Silver Jeans Co. Women’s Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Slim Bootcut (Amazon won't allow me to post with hyperlinks to each item)

I'm 5'3", 152 lbs, 32" waist, 39" hips. Based on reviews and the size chart’s waist measurement, I ordered size 13; otherwise, I would have ordered an 11. The waist was comfortable with a little room to spare. However, the hips and thighs were too roomy for my taste. I would say these would fit a true hourglass shape (with ample hips and thighs) very nicely. If I were to do it again, I would've gone with the 11. Of the six I ordered under Prime Try Before You Buy, these are made of the heaviest weight denim (98% cotton, 2% spandex) and are the most reminiscent of jeans you could buy in the 80s. I suspect they would hold up the best as work jeans. I do like them, and might try again in a smaller size in the future, but due to the fit and having six others to choose from, I’m sending these back. user_comment: I'm 5'4" and 38"x33"x39". According to the size chart the Size 12 would fit best at my waist, but would have been baggy at the hips. I decided to purchase a 10 and a 12 to make sure of the fit. The 12 was obviously tight looking while still in the package, so I tried them on first—never even opened the size 10. The 12 fit very tight across the hips and thighs, but are not tight around my waist. Waistband rolls when I sit, but I don't tuck my shirts in as I did for the photo. Thick fabric for a stretch denim. Regular inseam length hits right above the back of my foot. Front pockets are very shallow, 2" deep. Followup note: These pants are difficult to pull all the way up. I have to grab the waistband and hold it while I bend over to pull them upward up to 3 times. Once up they stay pretty well, but I wish the waistband was a little tighter. Basically, every slim-cut stretchy denim pant I have tried has this same tight hips and loose waist fit problem.

I'm 5'4" and approx 130 lbs and was unsure between S or M because I usually wear size 4-6 and wanted these pants to be loose fitting. After reading reviews, I decided to go with S and am happy to say it was a perfect fit. Verdict: comfortable & soft, not scratchy or stiff, not see-through, and color is off-white/linen color and thin enough to wear during hot season or warm temperature destination. The elastic band is stretchy so if you're in-between S or M, I'd recommend going with S. They hit right above the ankles and I like the breezy look! It arrived overnight by PRIME. I'm pleased with the purchase.

I'm 5'4" and currently 155 lbs., and the Medium size fits me almost perfectly (a tiny bit snug at the waist, but I'm losing weight so that won't be an issue for long). The elastic waist pulls in enough that I probably could also have worn a Large for a more relaxed fit through the seat and leg. The length is ideal for me, hitting just at the heel with a break at the ankle. That means if you're above average height, these pants may be a bit short for you. There's not much hem to let out. I usually have low expectations for knit and/or stretch pants, but this fabric is thicker than most. No panty lines visible, and the fabric has a pretty low stretch ratio -- it has enough give that you can move comfortably, but it's not clingy. The ribbing is visible, but neutral enough that from a few feet away the pants just look like dress slacks unless you're in super bright light (my photos were taken in direct sun to show detail, but in average room lighting they look darker). Obviously this applies to the black pants I'm reviewing; if you select a different color, texture visibility may vary. The pockets are large enough to fit my entire hand, but because they're set at an angle, I'm not sure I'd trust them with a large device like a smartphone. The only thing about these pants that I don't care for is the drawstring, which is 1) enormous and can't be tucked out of sight inside the waistband because it's so bulky, and 2) superfluous, because the waist is so heavily elasticized. I ended up pulling the drawstring out of mine (one end is untied, so it's easy) so the pants look less like sweatpants. I usually wear shirts untucked, so the holes in the waistband aren't likely to show. Overall, I'm pleased with the quality, and will put these into rotation with my fall casualwear!

I'm 5'4", 128 lbs, 38/29/38. Ordered these in size: 4 and the color: blue.It's so nice to find clothes that are flattering!! Really impressed with these Sidefeel Women's Flare Bell Bottom Jeans. These jeans feel really good on, the material is comfortable. The pockets are deep. The color blue matches the stock photos. No camel toe. The button fly on these are easy enough to work with.Now I didn't order according to their size chart. According to their chart I should have ordered a 6 or 8 for my waist and hips but for my height/shorter legs I ordered my usual size 4. These jeans actually fit perfectly due to their stretchiness. I'm 5'4", 147 lbs, 29/39 and chose a size 12 in these jeans. Overall the fit is great, these are very comfortable to wear, and I like the Bluing color of these jeans, but I feel that the leg type is misrepresented. The legs of these jeans are more of a stovepipe design, not nearly as fitted and shaped as the images suggest, and I don't like how huge and stumpy they make my already thick legs look. I didn't expect a close fitting pair of jeans, but neither was I expecting something so loose and baggy on me. These remind me somewhat of a certain brand of oversized leg jeans from the 90s, and I only wore those when I was a size 4-5 to avoid looking like a wall of denim. These make me look stumpy and stubby, and as much as I like how they move and feel I just don't like the look of them on. The legs of these jeans look short in the images, but mine do line up as described in the size chart, just shy of 30". These are about an inch too long for me, but I'm used to that. The material is quite stretchy, and very comfortable to move around in. I can do squats, take long strides, and stretch my legs out as much as I can in these jeans without discomfort. The waist is very comfortable and flattens my problem abdomen without digging in, and these jeans look great under close fitting tops. The backseam doesn't ride up my generously sized posterior, and the seat of these is somewhat flattering on my large backside. The front pockets are wide and deep enough for an earbuds case, but won't fit any of my phones. There are no side pockets, just front and back ones. I did have an issue with the stitching, however. There are many areas where the machine spat out knots of thread into rats nests, there's spots where the stitching was attempted to be backstitched but veered off, gaps in the stitch lines, and loose sections of threads that hang in a gap while being stitched down on either side. The four attached images are a sampling of the problems that I found in the stitching. I am not fully satisfied with the construction quality of these jeans. Overall I like the jeans enough to wear them camping or to work outdoors in cooler weather, but I'm not happy with appearance of them on and how rough the stitching is on these pants. Sidefeel Women's Wide Leg Jeans High Waist Flare Bootcut Jeans Bell Bottom Pants

I'm 5'4", 150 lbs, 34" waist, with all my squishy weight in my stomach. I have very slim hips and legs, and wear a short in pants. I always struggle to find skinny jeans that fit my legs AND my waist. These are absolutely perfect in almost every way. The sizing is a little off. I normally wear a size 8 but a size 6 fits way better. They are very stretchy but hold their shape incredibly well. The legs fit like skinny jeans are supposed to. My only complaints are that they only have two colors available in my size (6 short), and the second pair of the Sofia color that I got fit slightly different than the first pair... the legs aren't quite as fitted, and the color is darker. Overall these are my absolute favorite jeans, so I still gave them a 5 star rating. I'm waiting and hoping that they eventually start stocking the other colors in this size.

I’m 5’4”, 165 lbs. I have them in stormy sky color. The fabric feels like great quality jeans material. I wasn’t sure what size/length to purchase, so I ordered 12S, 12M, 14S, and 14 M. When I received them, I only opened and tried on the 12M and 14M. The 12M fit great, but fake front pockets, it’s tag was off and placed into the back pocket. Most likely returned by someone. The 14M fit nicely too, although not as tight/form fitting, and they had functional front pockets and all its tags were attached. Love the color and quality. It’s a dark wash, which I like for the versatility. I will wash in cold and hang dry before wearing them.

I'm 5'4", 188 lbs. I originally ordered an XL and it was really tight, and very unflattering. I tried an 2XL (picture attached). It was better, but the fabric is so thin, and it shows every lump and bump. The fabric seems soft and stretchy, and I love that it have 4 pockets. But if you're a bigger gal like myself, maybe skip these. I'm gunna keep the 2XL in hopes they'll look better when I lose some weight.

I'm 5'4", 29w and 39h and purchased a size small and these fit well - I've got leggings on underneath so they probably are a bit baggier than it looks in this photo. The denim is thin, so not too heavy. There are some pleats in the front that I'd rather not have. Hardly anyone wants the front of their jeans to puff out like this. These come vacuum packed so I had to wash them before I tried them on and they held up well through one machine wash. They are not low rise however, definitely medium rise.

I’m 5’4”, but my height is all my torso.... very short legs that look like crap in just about everything. But these are very flattering for me and they’re super comfortable! Honestly unbelievable quality for the price. I’m so glad I got these instead of going to a department store to find more uncomfortable slacks for work. These are comfy, work appropriate, and the sizing is very accurate.

I’m 5’4/125lbs and I found the small to be true-to-size. I love the elastic waist and pockets, and the material is lightweight but doesn’t feel cheap. I did need to steam them (to be expected) when they arrived and that helped go ensure they were the right length. I love that you can dress these up or down!

I'm 5'5 (on the left) and about 170 lbs. I orders a large and it was a little baggy. I ended up getting a medium and giving the large to my sister. She really enjoys the pants as well! I usually wear mediums in leggings and such but Amazon had recommend a large. They do have quite a bit of stretch to them!
I’m 5’5 &180 pounds so the material is thin and shows every thigh dimple imaginable. The color did match the description though. Probably better suited for someone with a slimmer figure

I'm 5'5 115 pounds with a 23 inch waist and an xs actually hugged my waist! The legs are long which I love because I always wear at least 4 inch heels with these kinds of pants and it was very flattering. In this picture I was wearing 5.5 inch heels.

I’m 5’5 129 pounds and I ordered the medium despite being a small but that was the size that was recommended. These have a lot of stretch to them. I washed in washer and dryer and there was definitely some shrinkage. When putting on after washing they fit okay except the waist was a little big. The jeans have two pockets in the front and I should have known better than to order pants with pockets in the front where the lower stomach is. Most women, especially of a particular age 😜, do not need anything like pockets to accentuate a part of our body that we don’t actually “want” to be accentuated. Not only that, there are red lines on the pockets drawing the eye further to that area that does not need to be accentuated 😜. I don’t usually order clothes off of Amazon but I have had some luck recently with a few orders of pants I really liked. Furthermore, the pants have the strangest texture. It’s very difficult to describe. I know that I don’t care for the texture and haven’t run into anything like it in any other clothing I have owned. I also think after one wash, they looked like they were about 10 years old already. In short, these jeans fell way short in comfort (weird texture), style, and quality.

I'm 5'5 140lbs. Another brand I find a 2 to be alittle too big. Dickies I got a size 8 and they're a bit tight at the waist. Very long, meant to be cuffed or hemmed. That's why I love dickies! Old school, the length is the easiest adjustment for the consumer to customize. I just have to make sure my man doesn't throw them in the dryer! Yeah, as if he'll do laundry! Ha!

I’m 5’5 145 lbs. I ordered the size 8 long. I probably could have ordered the regular 8. But i like the length. They are high waisted (hits at my belly button). I love how deep the pockets are! I'm 5'5 150lbs 44 butt and 33waist. Muffin top approved. I chose size large. Reviews all said "true to size." Fits surprisingly well. I wanted baggy and long. They are baggy and long. 80% linin, 20% cotton VERY nice. Sturdy but breathable fabric, strong seams. Wide elastic waist that would be perfect fit if I lost 2lbs. Lots of elastic waist pleats. Nice deep side pockets. Strong seams down front and back, and sides of each leg. Lantern cut is cool. My 1st experience with this style. I love the length without having to walk on my cuffs. Beautiful x hatch weave. I'm super impressed. It's over 100° right now and I'm bummed I cant wear them. Maybe wet and then wrung out... They may be very cooling like that. My only negative comment is the color. The grey in the pictures is dark slate/blue steel grey. Mine are normal med grey. I'll keep them but they don't " pop " like I expected. Even in my own pictures they Iook darker than they are. I chose these finally because of a review from an older women who said she will wear these for the rest of her life. I agree. They are pro. My images are from right out of the bag so will smooth out nicely soon.

I'm 5'5, curvy fit, 135ish. Not long legged. I normally wear a 27/6 in jeans ... I sized up to an 8 in this. Nothing was tight but I didn't live the look. Each of those are an obvious oversize. These jeans, on the other hand, just were not baggy looking as the model shows. I read many reviews and hoped they'd work but they literally look like my mom wore in the mid 80s and hers fot about like this. Just not a cute look, imo, if they are not baggy enough. Go big, or go home. I still wear skinny jeans. I have wode keg jeans. I have cropped flares. I cannot wear straight leg or boyfriend style bc I'm a curvy fot (the waist always sticks out too badly). Providing this info, as I think it is all relevant. Last, I'm almost 50. This look might seem cuter on a gal my size but in her 20s. Or with no curve and less boots. Material was fine; blue color a tad off, seemed almost frosted-ish.
