I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs and my measurements are 44(38/40C)bust, 34/35 waist & 44 hips. Per the size guide provided I ordered the XL. After trying, I could’ve gone down a size, but I really like the loose fit of these - they fit how they appear on the model, similar to a boyfriend fit. In my image I’m wearing 2.5” clogs. I love the look. These are super comfortable and the elastic waistband is wide and not constricting at all. The legs are wide but not exaggerated. After washing, these did shrink a bit, but mostly in length, but they still cover the tops of my feet. Fortunately, these pants have a generous hem, approx 3.” So if I ever wanted to wear these with even higher platforms, I could easily let the hem down. These are perfect weight for Fl. I can easily wear these in warm weather or with a sweater when it becomes cooler. I’m super happy with these and I definitely recommend. I also like that the brand, GRAPENT, has an Amazon store. Once I find a brand that suits me, I love to see what else they offer.

I’m 5’5,” 165 lbs, my waist measures 36” & my hips are 44.” Per the size guide provided, a 14 would be ideal, but the largest size available is 12. I hoped with a fiber content of 75% cotton & 25% polyester there’d be some stretch - so I ordered the sz 12. Well, these fit, barely (waist-wise). There is zero stretch to these jeans. Although I love the look of these, the waist is definitely snug. Fortunately I sew well, so I have a few tricks up my sleeve. (Or I could lose those last pesky 10lbs). I received these through Amazon Vine & returns/exchanges or not an option. However, even If those options were available to me, I’m not sure I’d return these - the hips & everywhere else (like my bum) fit super nice. I really like the thick cuffs which are super on trend right now, although I’ll most likely sew them securely all around. The cuffs are currently only stitched in place in a few spots (pls see image). These do run long - I’m actually wearing 2.5” wedges in my image. Those deep cuffs could come in handy for anyone who wanted these shortened (or lengthened). I love the style of the front pockets & especially the placement of the back ones. The overall quality of these are excellent. I feel the price of these are fair, especially when compared to one of my fav brands for jeans, Free People. There’s a pair of jeans very similar to these by FP I’ve had my eye on, but they’re $128. I believe with a few tweaks I’ll be very happy with these. I do recommend these jeans but highly suggest following the size guide provided.

I’m 5’5,” 165lbs but carry excess weight in my tummy. My waist is currently 36/37” and per the brand’s size guide provided I went with the sz 18. I’ve never been a sz 18 - I’m typically sz 14 or 32/33 waist in Free People jeans, but I love these jeans and wanted them to work. Turns out they fit! But due to how incredibly stretchy these are I definitely could’ve sized down to a 16. I still love the way these look and they’re a great dupe for the FP barrel jeans I’ve purchased in the past (and a lot less expensive). They did shrink some in the washer/dryer, but after a few hours they tend to stretch out again. I received these through Amazon Vine, but I love them so much I’m purchasing on my own a pair in a smaller size. Plus, at the time of writing this review (8/23/24) they’re on sale. Will update my review when I receive the smaller size. I do recommend these jeans, but keep in mind the stretch factor. The quality is excellent and I especially love how lightweight these are. It’s hot and humid here in South Florida, but these are jeans I could sport year round.
I’m 5’5. 183 lbs. I ordered the size 10 and they fit perfectly. Exactly what i was looking for

I’m 5’5” 145lbs and do property preservation and plumbing. This past December/January 2022 I shoveled up to 3” of snow and these kept me so warm… I had the snow blower literally shoot straight on me and didn’t get wet or cold. These are perfect!!

I’m 5’5” 147 lbs 38D and got these denim overalls in a small. The fit is spot on (I prefer something a bit more fitted), the length is just right for someone my height. The fabric is about medium weight, a bit thinner than Levi’s overalls. But I actually prefer something a bit thinner like these overalls because it’s more comfortable. Large side pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. Looks great with a t-shirt or cami. I have to take 1 button off in order to take these on/off because my hips are pretty large. The fabric is easy care, I just wash it like my denim jeans. I prefer to air dry but do like to put it in the dryer for a few minutes after it’s fully dried to soften it up. At the current listed price of $40, I think it’s a pretty reasonable price. Good quality.

I’m 5’5” 150 lbs 38D and got a size small because I know most Levi’s run a bit large. My normal size is a medium. The small fits great and comfortable to wear all day. Easy to take off if I need to use the restroom. Love the large pockets, fits my iPhone 13 Pro Max. The material is nice and heavy (not too heavy though) like normal Levi’s jeans. Typical Levi’s quality that won’t disappointment. Just wash like normal jeans. I prefer to let it air dry but do like to throw it in the dryer for a few minutes to soften it up after it’s full dried.

I'm 5'5" and 140lb. I usually get a M, and this M fit perfectly. I love these pants! The fit and color are so cute. I was looking for something to wear to a Barbie party, and I've found it. I'm 5'5" and 150lb. Short torso and prefer high waist jeans. Usually wear around 10 to 12. Sizing chart for these was so confusing that I ordered an 8, 10 and 12. Went with the 10. Here's why: 8 - I knew by looking that they would not fit 10 - tight around belly, too tight at first. But butt and thighs fit good. 12 - Waist was much more comfortable, but butt and thighs had too much extra fabric. Wore the 10 all day and even went skating in them. Never sagged. Never got the "MC Hammer" crotch. The butt is still looser than I'd like, but it's not saggy. The waist was fine, even with sitting most of the day for work. Did not feel like I I had to pull them up after squatting down and standing up, although I still did this. Probably all jeans will do this unless they're jeggings. Have washed and dried (on low) twice and so far, size has not changed. Gave 4 stars instead of 5 because they just do not hug my little to no butt.

I'm 5'5" and 150lbs and the size 10(regular) fit but they are a little loose on me. This isn't a sizing issue I've simply ordered my old size even though I lost weight. I was thinking I hadn't gone down a full size yet but I should have trusted the size chart which indicated I should be in a size 8. That said these are the same comfortable styling and quality I've come to expect from Lee. I’m 5’6, 125lb, 34DD. I usually wear a 26 and sometimes 27 in designer jeans and ordered size 26. I love these jeans! It’s like I’m reliving 1993! They are super stretchy and fit great. I took photos from three angles so you can see. They’re a nice fit all the way around but almost too tight in the waist for me. The material stretches so even though it’s tight, it’s not uncomfortable. I will, however, be careful to wash them in cold and hang them to dry. These are the highest waisted jeans I’ve ever owned but the buttons on the front really make them work. This is actually perfect for the crop tops that I sometimes hesitate to wear because now my midsection will be adequately covered. The length is perfect- I can wear tennies or boots or even a low heel and these jeans will look good with any of them. I highly recommend these jeans. If you want a looser fit, I would size up one size.

I know these aren't supposed to be the 7 for All Mankind early '00s Dojo style that literally drags on the ground, but these don't even hit the top of my foot. In the photo, I don't have them hiked up all the way. Overall, I'm bummed because the material is really nice and the style is actually really cute—it's just too baggy up top to be flattering and too short to let me feel comfortable wearing them. Hope it works better for others!

I’m 5’6”, 129 lbs., and wearing the medium (8-10) in my photo. I’m normally a consistent size 4 in US pants/jeans but I’ve learned to order up from Amazon. I’m happy with the fit of the medium. The fabric is lightweight and feels like a thick chiffon. The pants have a button/zipper fly and deep hip pockets but the back pockets are fake. There are belt loops on the waistband but the back is elastic so the belt is optional. The color is a deep navy. The legs are long enough that I have to wear at least a short heel to keep them from dragging the ground. What I like: 1. The super high waist and wide leg style. 2. Lightweight and flowy, good for summer work pants. 3. Real (and deep) pockets. What I don’t like: 1. The material has a nylon pantyhose feel, just scratchy enough to be noticeable. 2. Profile of the pants is kind of shapeless. The fabric just hangs and does very little to flatter the figure.

I posted a picture with my leg next to a silver bowl so you can get some sense but the picture doesn’t do it justice. These pants are more comfortable than jammies, I prefer wearing them over jeans. This same company puts out another non tummy control version of these that are exactly the same except they are actually boot cut as opposed to straight leg. After ordering about 4 pairs of each, I realized this was the only difference between them. But they don’t tell you in the description or give you an option to buy straight leg vs boot cut. The boot cut is a wider leg, but the straight leg makes me legs look more slender all the way down and fit over the top of my heels too. I just wish they had an actual very light grey in size 10. They need an option to wait list until those colors come in again. The pretty light blue is also not available in my size. But otherwise I lovse these pants!

I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

I'm 5'8 with longer legs and I have an impossible time finding jeans in a 10 Long which fit me, since my waist is a full 13" smaller than my hips (30"/43"). And I cannot stand it when skinny jeans fall inches above my ankle, it drives me nuts. These gave me that relieving feeling of when a skinny jean just perfectly hits the top of my foot (without being super tight around the ankle) - perfection. Normally I avoid mid-rise jeans but the fact there was a 10L in stock, as well as the price, convinced me to take a chance. I'm so glad I did - these are incredibly flattering! I've never owned pull-on jeans before but that's changing fast now. Also the wash "Immaculate-Waterless" is the absolute perfect color and wash - not too dark, not too light, with just enough whiskering around the areas you need to create a sort of contoured shapely look. I do wish the rise was just one inch higher, but that's probably because my lower waist is quite drastically bigger than my natural waist so they're a tad tight. But I'm sure they'll relax a bit after a few wears.

They are very high waisted, they sit above the belly button. I love that all 5 pockets are real. They do feel pretty cheap. The wash is meh, and they feel like jeggings. Definitely not high quality, but they’ll do for what I need.

I'm 5'8" and 145lbs, I usually wear a size 2-4. I bought the size "small extra tall" thinking that I'd be able to wear them with heels. I was weary since there wasn't any information on how long "extra tall" actually was, but I figured that it would be the same fit as the pants the model is wearing in the product image. On closer inspection, it appears that they photoshopped out the white sneakers that the model was actually wearing and added in the black heels to make the pants look like they come down farther. According to the size chart, a small is 38.5" long (length of inseam isn't listed). My pair is exactly 40" long with a 32" inseam. The design is pretty and the pants are comfortable, but they are just WAY TOO SHORT!

I did the try before you buy option which is a great benefit for sure. The 12 fit me about right, perhaps a smidge loose in the waist. These jeggings had a nice comfort band and the material was comfortable and a little thicker than I expected. It definitely is not a cheap looking or feeling pant. I sent it back because I didn't like how shirt it was on me. If it had been longer then it would have made a winter staple for sure due to the thickness of the material, which is a little heavier than I'm used to.

I'm 5'8" around 165lbs Was looking for a good comfy pair of jeggings as I've gained around 15lbs. I didn't want to spend too much because I can't wear these to work, so only once a week would I want to wear these casually on my days off. Love the color. The rest of the reviews are correct, they run long, even for me, so I cuffed them at the ends which is fine. They have the appearance of a real jean, strechy, high-waisted and functioning back pockets. Definitely going to buy these in another color. I'm 5'8" mostly legs. 120 pounds fit. I wear 8 Long in all my other pants. These pants have a gap in the front and the back despite the "contoured waistband to prevent gapping." I don't see anything contoured about it. They are a little on the baggy side too. So I should've sized down to 6 Long. I hoped they would shrink in the washer/dryer, but they did not shrink at all. If you are thin everywhere but have a fuller belly, maybe get your regular size? Otherwise I suggest going down a size. The material is not as thick as men's pants, but it's a little thicker than yoga pants anyway. I was hoping for heavier winter pants, but no one makes that for women. At least there are decent pockets, and I appreciate the long length.
I'm 5'8", 130 lb ordered a size 6. Perfect fit! Material is light enough (not heavy like denim).

I do like how soft and breathable the material is, but the pair I got has red bleeding through it on the bottom. They seem to look really nice on other girls, but if you have a similar shape to mine or want pants that don’t come with stains on it, I don’t recommend these pants.

I'm 5'8", about 120 - 125 lbs, but have a really short torso and the inseam of someone closer to 5'10". I first ordered these pants in size medium in white (pictured) and I loved them so much that I also ordered them in green. For reference, I'm wearing the pants pulled up to my natural waist and wearing 3" heels. I thought the medium fit great and looked ok on my butt, until I tried on the size small in green which looked way better on my butt than the medium, and better all-around. The problem with the green is that they are about 1.5" shorter than the white, despite the product page saying the inseam on small and medium are the same. The picture speaks for itself - clearly they are not the same length. Someone else made this same comment with a photo about the white vs. blue, only she ordered the exact same size and the white were a lot longer. I did not find them to be see-through at all as others have mentioned, but perhaps that's because I didn't buy a skin tight fit. I received many compliments on the white pair and will keep them, but unfortunately any other color seems to be too short, so the green pair is going back. Update: I ordered the blue in medium and they are as long as the white medium so it seems as if the color doesn’t matter, but the small is a lot shorter than the medium even though the product page says the inseam is the same.

I’m 5’9 1/2 and I ordered a large, perfect fit and the length was long enough! I paired them with a pair of ankle boots and the jeans still touched the floor! Very pleased with my purchase!! The waist is very slimming and stretchy! I wore them all day and there was no discomfort!

I’m 60, 5’5” and 165lbs. I carry excess weight in my tummy which is 36 and my hips are 44.” The size guide did not make much sense. It stated an XL waist measured 23.6 which to me is more like an XXS. Fortunately I’ve purchased jeans and pants from this brand, LifeShe, so I ordered my “usual” size XL in Blue. The fit is slightly loose, which is the style/fit I prefer. These do not fit me low rise, but I’m very short waisted. The waistband is approx 36” relaxed. It’s elasticized but only on the sides and I’m especially happy there are belt loops. The care tag states cotton and polyester, no percentages but it looks like all cotton and it definitely feels like all cotton. It’s lightweight and breathable but not thin or flimsy; a nice quality denim constructed very well. I can’t think of anything I dislike about these jeans - from the deep pockets (the front right has a hidden change pocket) to the contrasting stitching. I love barrel jeans/pants and have quite a few, but I’d consider these more of a reg baggy style, although they do flare out in the hips (these measure 26” laying flat side to side). The length/inseam may not be “super tall friendly.” In my image I’m wearing platform Converses that are about 1.5.” I wish these were just an inch longer in case they shrink. If they do will update my review. I received these through Amazon Vine at a discounted price in exchange for my review. However, I feel at just under $40 the price is very fair. They’re actually 10% off at the time of this review (plus free Prime shipping). I love these so much I purchased the black & white ones.

I’m 61, 5’3”, 179 lbs - Age is important, because it changes body shape. I’m excited to find these jeans, so I bought 4 different pairs, hoping something would work. Three of 4 are keepers. I particularly like the stretch, because they’re as comfortable as yoga pants. I don’t mind the waist being a little loose, and it has a slight gap. I’d rather it didn’t cut in and make a roll. It’s not to the point I’m pulling them up. I have thick thighs and don’t want pull lines you get when you cross from fitted to too small jeans. I’m at that age that comfort is important, but so is style. 😁 Over 10 years ago a blogger, Grasping For Objectivity, did a big series on jeans, showing how important pocket placement is along with pictures of the same women in different jeans. It was the only article like that, and it’s still the best source as it showed comparisons of real women and told the brands. (Never buy ON) Why is this relevant? THESE JEANS have the RIGHT pocket placement and aren’t remotely close to $100. What. A. Score! These will be my go-to from now on. I found the inseam on regular is longer than many other brands. At 5’3” I should always need petite length but have a short waist and long legs, so I usually require regular length, especially in cheap brands like Lee. You can see that regular length is very long on me. I could’ve ordered petite, but I don’t mind. If you wear heals, this would be perfect. I will buy these again. I’ve found my go-to jeans!

I’m a 5’5” 165lbs woman. I’m also a 40B and per the brand’s size guide provided, I could’ve easily worn the smallest size. However at age 60, I don’t like my tops too short or anywhere near a cropped look, so I ordered the XL and I love the fit. I actually prefer my clothing to fit loose (think Free People) and this top does so very comfortably without looking sloppy. Actually, this is a pretty good dupe for Free People’s Jude Denim Pullover, except the price, thankfully. The FP one retails for $128. This initially caught my eye because it’s a great dupe, but I ordered it mainly for the fiber content. True to product description and the attached care tag, this is 100% cotton. It’s a lighter weight cotton, but not thin - it’s very soft and breathable. We live in coastal Fl and this will be perfect for cooler evening walks on the beach. I love the unfinished bottom hem and the block stitching throughout. I feel the quality and construction of this top is excellent. Although I received this through Amazon Vine at a reduced cost, I would totally pay full price for this. I’d love to see this in a turquoise or even white. I washed this on cold and dried on low. It came out nicely, but some may want to give it a quick steam. I like that it looks a little rough hewn after washing; lends to the whole beachcomber vibe. My husband also mentioned that since it runs large it’ll be great for layering when it’s cold. This brand, PLNOTME, also has a store here on Amazon with some really super cute items.

I don't have to worry about these falling down when I run. They are fuzzy but not so much that it makes you feel thick. They move great! I would only wear these outside and in the cold. They make running in dreary Alabama winters bearable (it gets down into the single digits here at times). The quality is amazing; you will not regret this buy. Also the fact that they are water resistant is just added awesomeness. No more excuses on yucky days!

I'm a little in love with this pantsuit. I didn't want anything that looked like nurse's scrubs and I think I achieved my goal. I got lots of compliments. It fits really well. For reference, I am 5'8 and 170 lbs. I would recommend washing it before wearing it. I hand washed it in cold and a lot of black dye and chemicals came off. Then I line dried it. There were no wrinkles, but if you do get wrinkles i would say not to iron it because it is a rather sheer, thin fabric. It's comfy, and you can dress it up or down. I balked a little at the price, but it's really worth it.

I'm a little matronly, so they didn't transform me into a 40s pinup girl as I secretly expected ;) oh well! The fit is great. The denim fabric has a little stretch but is also medium-sturdy. Not thin as in one comment I read. They are super comfortable. I probably could have gone a size smaller. 150lb, 5'5, waist 38, hip 41, bought size XL

I'm a mom of two who went from a size 2 to a 6/8 I am 5'7 at 155 so yes I have hips and a butt. No shame. I have tried so hard to find skinny jeans and leggings that were appropriate to wear around my kids and even at work. Ladies this is it! My husband has thought for a week that these were real jeans. Everyone has! They are so comfortable too. I live in Georgia and I was worried the fleece lining would be too hot, it's not! They are breathable and very cute.

I'm a person that HATES tight jeans because the material is so uncomfortable and itchy that when I wear MOST skinny jeans I can't wait to go home and take them off. I was expecting these jeans to be the same since they were from amazon but when I put them on I was so suprised that the material was soft quality and didn't make me dread wearing them. I wore them out and my BF when he saw the jeans on me had his eyes practically pop out of his head. These jeans hug every inch of my curves and they are so comfortable to wear for long hours. I could sleep in them and coming from ME that says alot. I will be purchasing more of these colors and others from here. I don't know how these will old up and if the quality of the fabric will remain the same after wash so I plan to wash on cold and hang dry these jeans to avoide any issues. I love these jeans so much. On my way to buy more pairs. BTW: The top I am wearing is a black turtle neck bodysuit jumpsuit (long sleeves) that I also purchased here on amazon. Both pieces plus a pair of black flats really put me together like I said when I took my jacket off my boyfriend's eyes popped out of his sockets because the jeans and the turtle neck bodysuit hug my whole body. MEASUREMENTS: I'm 5'3 148 pounds and I bought a size 11 and it fits perfectly. A size 13 would have been to large and not as flattering. size 11 is not to big still plenty of stretch to material to not hug your body tight. I normally wear a sieze 10 in jeans so I went up one size. Best decision ever. GET THESE JEANS!!!

I'm a plus size gal. 5'8 240lbs Size 2x-3x or a 20W-22W D cup. Soooo I'm a big gal. These overalls look great. I was surprised how much I liked them. They're real jeans so you can use them to work on the garden or head out to town in them. You gotta love cute and functional. I appreciate the real metal rivets and clips. I’m a shorty so they are long and high waisted on me. 5’0 usually 16 in jeans.

I'm a size 12/14 or XL usually in regular clothes. I ordered a size XXXL based on their chart and reviews. I like them and wear them plenty. I got 2 colors and will be buying more.

I'm a size 14 these are a little snug at the waist for me but overall, I love how they look, and the length is perfect for my 5’1 height. The one thing I love about these jeans there Skinny Jeans but there not tapper to your legs and I like that there high-rise that’s a plus.

I'm a size 4 and I'm 5'3 Length wise they were perfect. I got a size medium but should've went with a small. A medium seems more like a size 6. I love the look of these jeans, they're a great dupe for the ones taylor swift wore.

Still perfect for tying at the bottom. I replaced my AE cargo pants with these, and I prefer these. Only knocked down a star for the length, otherwise, I would have made it 5.
I'm a size 8/10 I'm glad I picked a size 8. They have a lot of give, work great for curves. I'm 160 lbs and 5'6" but they're a bit too long. Will be fine with heels but not with flats

I'm a size L, 30 in waist, 32 in inseam. Sometimes I wear a 12, sometimes a 14. I purchased the Marilyn in size 12. It is too big. I should have purchased the 10. It stretches over 2 in at the waist. That is brand new. It has stretch so that wearing it over the course of the day, this pant will definitely fall off of me. Inseam is fine, a bit long (probably 33 in) but not a big deal because when I wear shoes it won't look long. The instructions on Amazon say to purchase a size smaller. Definitely do that. Weight of the fabric is heavy, winter weight. I don't think it will be comfortable outside in the summer. Honestly I would not purchase these jeans again - I like Madewell High Rise Alley jeans and Jcrew matchstick jeans better, they are more flattering. These are totally mom jeans. Rise is just below the belly button. I like the high waist, but with this much stretch, it kind of defeats the purpose. These have so much stretch that no matter what size you get, they will be baggy and ill fitting by the end of the day. You could wear a belt, but at that point you might as well wear a real pant. At least these are not the leggings that are made to look like jeans. If you buy these jeans, you will be tugging them up all the live long day. A pair of twenty dollar levis from target fit better than this. I give this product two stars. One star for good quality sturdy fabric and stitching, and one star for comfort. Attached are photos of how the jeans fit me. I couldn't photograph the tug at the waist with my phone - the jeans stretch out four inches with minimal effort. Too much!

I’m a solid 8/10 (size 30), 5’6” and 165lbs. I ordered a 31 and they are perfect!

I'm a straight-leg girl, but I liked the look of these online so I thought I'd give them a try. I actually like the way they look and feel overall. The flares balance my broad shoulders nicely...they definitely do away with that top-heavy look that skinny jeans and a blousy top can cause (I'm an inverted triangle shape, so leggings and skinnies can make me look like a chicken if I'm not careful lol). They're incredibly stretchy and soft, and I was shocked to find them long enough for my 33.5" inseam (I NEVER find jeans long enough unless I order tall, and even then they're sometimes only a 32"). The length alone made me celebrate, and I can actually wear them with heels! My only issue with them is that I am short-waisted, even though I'm tall, and these are not made for a short torso. They button at my ribs, which I kind of hate. It feels suffocating. I typically buy mid-rise jeans (I like them under my belly button an inch or so), so getting used to these literally poking the bottom of my breasts when I sit down will be a whole thing. They're ok when standing (no muffin top!), but sitting feels super...awkward. I don't think these will replace my usual Joe's jeans, but I enjoyed wearing them for a night out when we were in Vegas, and they were quite comfy. They'll be nice for occasional wear, and are a great compromise between slacks and jeans when it comes to dressing for those occasions in which you're unsure of the dress code. I ordered a 14 (I usually wear a 31 or 32 jeans) because I have a bigger booty, but these have so much stretch that sizing up was unnecessary. They're quite roomy in the waist...I think a 10 or 12 would probably have been better (if I reordered, I would go with the 10). They work, though, because they're so soft and stretchy. I would recommend these as long as the uber high waist doesn't bother you. If you're used to skinnies, these still have that soft feel, so they're a good transitional option before you dive into a pair of stiff, high waisted Levi's. I washed in cold and dried on medium. They did shrink a bit (which is what I was going for when I put them in the dryer). Now I would probably just hang them to dry.

I'm a sucker for silver bling! These are true to size. I usually wear an XL (5'8", 205lb) but I sized down to a Large since I'm losing weight, and they fit as expected. They fit now thanks to the very stretchy material, but will be perfect and flowy once I get down to around 190. The material is a finely pleated metallic polyester. The face is very shiny, and the back is black. They have a wide elastic waistband that's quite comfortable. They're a tiny bit short on me right now but should relax down a couple of inches as I get closer to a true large size. These are flashy and fun, and great for either a night out or just around the house!

I’m actually surprised at the quality of this skirt. It’s a thick denim, not the lightweight stuff – do remember that because this skirt doesn’t flow softly and it might be a little stiff for some. I purchased both the grey and dark blue denim in this style and love them both. The buttons are fixed on well, the seams are good, and they seem like any other pair of well made jeans (other than it's a skirt!). You will most likely want to size up, unless showing you ‘assets’ is something that appeals to you. I’m in my 60’s, and I’m 5’5”, 107 lbs and quite fit. I ordered a size Small. Not Extra Small. I’ve a tiny waist, and this skirt still fits well. But it clearly hugs my butt. Not something I’m keen on in this part of my life. I’m showing it with a cashmere sweater tucked in (it’s rather thin), and also with a sweater worn on the outside. The skirt does look far better when it’s ironed – I washed and ironed both (no, neither skirt shrunk in a warm wash and regular dryer) and they do look far nicer than the photos I’m showing. The bottom of the skirts tend to get stuck in the tops of my taller ankle boots, but not so much in my shorter boots. Again, it’s a stiffer skirt, semi-full, so walking more slowly is required. Well, at least for me! Still, I love my skirts! I think they'll be so nice this winter with a long, warm sweater. I've a pair of fleece lined tights that will look great with either skirt, and keep me warm this winter.

I’m adding this info for future buyers because I myself like to read a lot of reviews before buying clothes lol.I’m 5’7” and fluctuate between 145lbs - 150lbs. I bought a size 8 (regular length). I’ve recently gained weight so I don’t know my true size right now, I just ordered one size up from what I used to be. I still fit in Free People size 28 jeans, if that’s of any help to anyone.These jeans fit comfortably enough BUT they’re also pretty snug so if they shrink in the dryer they probably won’t fit me anymore. I honestly think I could’ve gone with a size 10 in case they do end up shrinking.. I’m still considering exchanging them. I haven’t washed or worn them out yet, so I don’t know how well they’ll hold up. I will say that the material seems to be thicker and better quality than I expected. They have some stretch but not enough to be considered that weak jegging material (I don’t like), they actually feel like jeans. I also like the length, they’re not short and not too long, as you can see in the picture (I’m not wearing shoes). As for the rise of the waist, I'd say it's mid-rise because it sits about an inch below my belly button. They're definitely not low-rise on me, which I'm glad about because I hate low-rise.All in all, they seem like a pretty good pair of jeans, and if my first impression is correct, they’ll be sturdy and last a decent while.I hope my review helped someone out!

I'm always a little wary of ordering pants online because the sizing can be way off. However, these fit exactly as I expected based on the product description and sizing chart. I'm 5'4" and weigh 125lb so I typically wear an XS or size 2 in pants. However, the sizing chart on Amazon indicated a waist and hip measurements in an XS that made me think they would be too small. So, I ordered the small and they fit me perfectly. I have short legs so I knew that a 29" inseam would be too long and that I would need to hem them. However, because of the excellent product description, I was not disappointed when the product arrived and it was exactly how I expected it to be. The actual pants themselves are beautifully soft and comfortable. The material is great quality and they are extremely well made. They have quite a bit of stretch to them and so are not uncomfortable when you sit down. They have faux pockets in the back which are cute and there are real pockets in the front. I don't actually like that you can see the outline of the pocket at the front so I'm going to cut the pockets off and stitch up the pocket opening to turn them into faux pockets like at the back. These are really lovely pants and I highly recommend them!

I’m always cold and was looking for jeans with lining. These are comfortable, lots of stretch and are warm! I bought XS, normally I wear a size 2. I’m 5”4’ and weigh 125 pounds. A size S would have also worked, but the stretch makes up for it with the XS. I may need more colors for this winter.

I'm always cold, so I finally started buying more fleece-lined clothes. These keep my legs warm and I am happy that I bought them. I would prefer a longer inseam though! -Sizing- I’m 5’3” and I bought "Elasticwaist Blue88" in medium when I was wearing about an eight in pants, but I couldn't get these on at all! The next winter I was down to a four or six - and these finally fit. Several reviewers said these ran large, but my experience is that the medium runs small. -Washing- These machine wash and dry easily, without any noticeable shrinking. Colorfast on the outside, fluffy warm on the inside. I wash these with my dark clothes. -Wearing- Thick fleece = bulky. The bulk isn’t unreasonable, but it is visible without a jacket. These pants are fairly stretchy, but my issue is the length. Walking is fine, but sitting gets uncomfortable because the pants are really tight on my kneecaps. So I pull these up to get more slack at the knees -which makes driving more comfortable- but then my ankles get cold because they've lost their cover. A longer inseam would be great. Until then I just need to be mindful about socks or boots to compensate for cold ankles at the expense of knee mobility. -Overall- Great buy! I can finally go outdoors in the winter and not freeze immediately. A longer inseam would be nice, but these are still worth buying if you’re always cold like me.

I'm always looking for denim leggings, and appreciate new pairs that I can wear out of the house before they get ripped, banged around, paint splattered, etc. I'm not sure these jeans from Dokotoo are that flattering for me, though. I wear an XL (5'4", 170 pounds) and the legs are way tooo long, and the wide flares are also maybe not that slenderizing. The brand-new stitching looks a bit bright and cheap to me. With that said, I may just hem the legs and wear these at home and about town. They are very comfortable and fit well aside from the leg length. They might be perfect to sew kneepads in, for floor work on my various building projects. For someone who is a taller XL, these might be just perfect. Of note is that the slash pockets at the top are real, and the stitching in the area of where cargo pockets would be, is faux. A big plus is that these are elastic cotton -- 98 percent cotton, 2 percent elastane, which may be why they feel comfortable is a way different to the soft comfort of polyester. Just more natural feeling. Review of Dokotoo Women's Casual Mid Waist Cargo Jeans Stretch Wide Leg Denim Pants with Pockets

I'm always looking for denim leggings, and appreciate new pairs that I can wear out of the house before they get ripped, banged around, paint splattered, etc. I'm not sure these jeans from Dokotoo are that flattering for me, though. I wear an XL (5'4", 170 pounds) and the legs are way tooo long, and the wide flares are also maybe not that slenderizing. The brand-new stitching looks a bit bright and cheap to me. With that said, I may just hem the legs and wear these at home and about town. They are very comfortable and fit well aside from the leg length. They might be perfect to sew kneepads in, for floor work on my various building projects. For someone who is a taller XL, these might be just perfect. Of note is that the slash pockets at the top are real, and the stitching in the area of where cargo pockets would be, is faux. A big plus is that these are elastic cotton -- 98 percent cotton, 2 percent elastane, which may be why they feel comfortable is a way different to the soft comfort of polyester. Just more natural feeling. Review of Dokotoo Women's Casual Mid Waist Cargo Jeans Stretch Wide Leg Denim Pants with Pockets

I'm currently replacing old belts in my wardrobe accessories, and I really like reversible ones because I like the two color options in one belt. So, getting this AWAYTR Reversible Leather Belt was an easy decision for me. The first thing for me to say is that before I ordered it, I noticed that its design looked more like something I would wear instead of something my wife would, even though it's advertised first as a woman's belt. Once I was able to look at it in person, the style's definitely something I could wear. With that observation out of the way, here is my assessment of the belt. It is well made with good thick leather, a stylish belt buckle, and the stitching is really good. Reversing the belt is easy to do, and it locks solidly in place. The length of the belt can be cut down for more of a proper fit, if necessary. I found this info on the product page (Instructions for doing this did not come with the belt). I cut about 2 1/2" from the length to give me the correct fit for my 34" waist. Also, the two colors, black/coffee, are exactly the same as belts that I'm getting rid of, so I am really satisfied with this new belt.

I’m currently: 30 weeks - 5’5” - 152lbs. Have mainly gained weight in belly/boobs not so much hips/legs/butt. My impressions: opening the package, yes they have a smell. I guarantee this goes away once washed. Look: I received what is modeled regarding the color and distressing. Size: maybe a little bit long, but I like that I can wear them as a full pant or cuffed (pictured both). With how and where I carry weight a Medium felt true to size for me. Fit: I didn’t look at the size chart but what I can say is that the belly band sits a little high for me and my liking. I prefer it either completely under or over my belly. I am able to fold just the front elastic down and that sits perfectly under my belly. The legs are just a little loose - but that’s how I like my jeans to fit. Stretch: the right amount of elastic. I’m 95% positive that I’ll be able to wear these for the remainder of my pregnancy. Overall: I’m not disappointed in purchasing these jeans. I feel that the price matches the quality/value of the pants.

I'm fond of these jeans. I love a straight leg and I love a patch pocket, so I was already primed to like them. I was a little apprehensive about them being only 85% cotton, but they feel like true denim to me (unlike some more polyester-y feeling jeans.) The "classic blue" color really is a classic. I love that they're a solid wash without any whiskering or fading (which I tend to despise.) I only wish they had a little more slackness in the thighs, because they do cling to the tops of my thighs a little bit, which is typically not something that happens with a straight leg. But it's not too bad; I'm probably my own worst critic where my upper thighs are concerned. They hold my tummy pooch in rather than emphasizing it, which is nice. I am a size 10 with wide hips, and ordered the large. The waistband is snug, so these definitely run on the small side. I'm 5'5" and the hem falls just above my heel in the back. I usually prefer an ankle length, but these jeans are the perfect full length, which is better for wintertime anyway. I think they will be a smidge more comfortable after I lose just a couple pounds, but I am pleased with these jeans and anticipate wearing them a lot in the months to come.

They are flattering and lay flat in the front, despite being button fly. They are a tad long for a short person if wearing with flats. Perfect with heels.

I'm hard to fit - I have the dreaded "apple" shape instead of the more common "pear" body shape. These Lee Plus-Size Relaxed Fit Straight Leg jeans fit. Being almost 6ft I get the tall variation. The fabric is not as heavy as the Lands End relaxed jeans I loved but are now discontinued (darn!). They feel a little skimpy by comparison and did not keep me as comfortable/warm in this exceptional New England winter as those heavier ones. These have elastic built into to fabric itself. The Lands End jeans had a sliding elastic panel. Different solutions and each worked well for me. One thing I like about these Lee Jeans is the pockets are deep enough to use quite useful. My doctor told me to stop carrying a shoulder bag (and my shoulder problems stopped!) so now I have come to rely on my pockets so getting pants with useful and deep pockets is a big plus for me. Besides fitting my waist they seem to fit everywhere else too. Maybe I should get the legs shortened just a little. If I get the regular length they are too short and as my mother used to kid me - I can't cut it longer! I've been wearing these jeans for about a year and I'm very happy. They seem to be wearing quite well with many laundry cycles still look quite good. They keep getting softer and more comfortable with each wash. They have been color-fast so I can just throw them into any load. No worries. I'm really pleased with these Lee Plus-Size Relaxed fit jeans. I don't have a down side for this review so I think they are a solid 5 star item.

I’m just not feeling the bell bottoms coming back in style. For one I’m to short and thick so they tend to make me look bigger. They are a good fit thru the waist and legs. For reference I’m 5’1 148lbs and I ordered these in a medium. If you like the bell bottoms then I would recommend these as they are a nice pair of jeans.

I'm just shy of 5'6 and around 150lbs. I measured my self to get the right size according to the size chart. According to that I was a 12 but I ordered a 10, which turned out to be way too big. Thankfully my daughter fit them. So I reordered a 6, still a bit loose but comfortably loose. Leaves room just in case they shrink after washing and drying. Overall I really like them. They have tons of stretch and the length is perfect. In my opinion maybe size down if you use the size chart. Or order what you normally would wear in pants.
I’m just under 5’2” being 115 lbs and unfortunately these were 1/2-1” too long. Aside from that, the quality seemed nice and the style was cute!

I'm just under 5'8" and down to 195lbs at the time of my order. I lost over 60 lbs last year, from a size 22-24 to I'm not sure what size exactly. This might be important for others to know. I'm still losing slowly, but based on the measurements I took of myself before ordering (Waist 39", Hips 45", Inseam 32-34"), I optimistically ordered size 14 -- and miraculously, they fit, just a bit snug but not overly tight. I think the slight stretch to the material helped with that. The tag was so amusing that I had to share. I'm too old to deal with hand washing anything, so if it doesn't survive my machine, it's not worth having. You can bet that not only did I wash it in my machine (cold water, normal setting, with the rest of my clothes), I also tumbled it dry -- to be fair, I did follow the instructions to not luncle dry it! They came out just fine, with no noticeable shrinkage. If machine washing & drying makes them wear out faster, that's just too bad. The most I'll go out of my way for something special I rarely wear is to put it in a net bag to wash and hang to dry -- but that's only if I have the time and inclination. :) The only problem I have with them is the same as with many pants: there is a gap at my lower back since my hips & butt are bigger than my waist. I have to wear a belt with these pants to correct that. They are not really made for women with my type of curves. I'm also a notorious penny-pincher, and these pants are nearly $40, which is almost twice as much as I prefer to pay for pants. These are comfy & soft, but the material is also thin, so I don't know how long they'll last before they tear. I've only worn them a few times since I received & washed them. For this price, I hope they last a long time. If they do tear or have any problems prematurely, I will come back to update.

I’m leaving this review for other skeptical amazon buyers lol. I’m not big on leaving reviews but other reviews help me so I want to help back .... I ordered the black pants size 15 .. if it helps I’m 5’5 and weight 198lbs. The pants came fast but I did spend $3-$4 extra for faster shipping I needed them quick. I wore them 2x and washed them 2x in all of a week honestly. I did not experience any fading as of yet. The first pic is the first time I wore them a week later I wore them in the 2nd pic. They fit great. Hopes this helps someone.

I'm looking for a soft pair of jeans that don't constrict (I haven't yet been able to embrace the leggings-as-pants trend). My waist is smaller than my hips and WAY smaller than my thighs, so I have a tremendous amount of difficulty finding pants that fit. These are so highly rated that I was hopeful. Alas...these are not at all a "curvy" fit, but rather seem to be intended for women whose waist and hip measurements are nearly the same. I'm 5'7" and 138, measurements matching what the size chart listed as a size 9. Though my actual waist is a 28, and the waistline of these pants doesn't hit at my waist, nor is it a 28. I didn't measure it, but you can see how huge it is. The jeans do fit in the butt and thighs, but I could fit my cat in the gap in the waist (I didn't try that, don't worry). I'm giving three stars because they're cute and soft, but the description is inaccurate. They're going back. Also, ladies, if you're shaped like me and can make a recommendation for a brand that will fit, please reply!

The waist has elastic in the back so a medium would have fit. I knew these were wide leg but the saggy butt area on me doesn’t work. I thought since these were cropped they would be full length on 5'1" me & with taller shoes they wouldn't drag on the floor. They drag. I’m not sure about these pants. I like the bright whiteness of them and I like that there’s pockets. They’re definitely a yoga style pant however, I feel like you could wear them as leggings if you want to. My issue with them is that they’re not as thick as I would of hoped to have had them, now they are not see-through however, because they’re not that thick they don’t do everything you want, leggings or yoga pants to do. Hide “things”! It’s hard to tell in these pictures with great lighting(I am using a ring light), but they’re not hiding everything you would want to hide. Ss a matter of fact, in some bad lighting, or maybe even a little bit of shadowing, it kind of makes your legs look even worse than what they probably are. So if you have dimples or any kind of cottage cheese going on in the thigh/hamstring/glutes area, you’re not gonna wanna wear these by themselves, ladies you know what I mean. But I feel they would be a good layering pant. Or perfect for a young lady that has nice smooth legs or super fit no jiggle or sagging in the skin yet hahah! They are definitely stretchy, so that’s a plus. I am 5‘9“ 145 to 150 pounds and these are a size medium.

I’m not sure what it is with the brand Hybrid & Company, but you could order five pairs of pants the same size and they’d all come out different. I always give it a 30% chance when I order…I got this red color of skinny jeans in a size small at first, and they were a bit tight and I couldn’t even button them, so I went up to a size medium which are the pants in the picture (smudge on mirror, not on pants). These must’ve been mislabeled or something because they’re HUGE. I’m 5’4” and 108 pounds but…come on. Then the smalls should’ve fit? And the cut is long & slouchy. Such a joke.

I’m not the HUGEST fan of skinny jeans, so my 4 stars are cuz of that. But these were a great price in my size 6 long in white and I needed some white. They fit true to size. I am always a 6 (28) in stretchy jeans. Then I found the khaki was on sale but size 6 regular… you can see in the pics that the khaki are like ankle jeans on me. I’m 5’9, 165 lbs apple shaped. No waist and skinny butt and legs. The waist goes right up to or slightly below my belly button. I do not find them see through but with the white I obviously wear nude or white underpants. I wouldn’t pay full price for these, but am happy with my purchase. If they shrink or do anything weird in the wash I will update. I try to wear jeans a bunch before washing. They are so stretchy that they are extremely comfortable but do not sag during the day!

I'm not unhappy with the fit of the pants I got, but it's not anything close to the casual high-waisted wide-legged look in the product photos. For reference, I'm 5'3" with a 29" waist and 40" hips, and I got these in Medium per the size chart. They're the PERFECT length, which is always a pleasant surprise for us shorter humans. And they fit, just not how I expected them to. So if you want that really oversized fit they're showing in all the photos, you will have to size up. They're really comfortable, especially with the elasticized inset at the back of the waistband. The fabric has a bit of give, I wouldn't go so far as to call it stretch but it's not as constricting as a typical corduroy. It's a lighter weight fabric, better suited for cool weather than cold, and if you get one of the lighter colors you will want to be aware of what color undies you put on that day and try to stick to something as close to your skin tone as possible.

I’m not usually a fan of linen pants. I would like to be but usually they wrinkle so bad that it’s not even worth the trouble. These pants are a little bit heavier thicker than your typical linen so I can throw them in the washer dryer, and they do not look crazy wrinkled. They are so comfortable. I bought a second pair. I am 5’4” and this is a size large so for me they come down right to the top of my ankles so if you’re taller they’re going to be cropped on you.

I’m not very tall, but I have long legs. It’s hard for me to find pants long enough. I have a 33 inch inseam. I ordered these pants for the long inseam, and they do fit nicely. However, even their long inseam is not that long. They’re long enough for flats, but not to wear with any type of heel. I do like that the waist actually fits at waist level. The size chart for these jeans were perfectly. And the pants fit comfortably. I would consider these a lightweight denim, nothing that’s going to stand up to very much stress, but that are comfortable to wear around. I have completed this review after using the product thoroughly myself. Even though I participate in the Vine program, my reviews are 100% authentic…I my experiences, both good and bad.

I'm often hesitant to try jeans online but the sizing on these was thankfully alright. For reference, I am 5'5" and about 155 lbs right now. I got these in a size 8 which is what I typically try, but these days I might be a 6 to a 10 depending on the company selling the jeans. It's hard to trust jean companies anymore even when the give waist/length sizes, because pretty much nobody fits each parameter perfectly. Anyway these are very comfy and all pockets are open and functional. Gotta say I like the two forward pockets especially as most front jeans pockets you'd be hard pressed to actually put anything in. These actually fit my small phone in a pinch and do fit a wallet or other small object. The blue is nice and the pre wear on the pocket doesn't look like it will rip out too fast. It's tolerable unlike most pre wear/shredded types that you know are going to give you issues in cold weather or become functionally an issue. I'm a bit concerned the main button could eventually come out as its placement seems a bit tight on the waist. Otherwise though, I really like the feel of these.

I'm one of the last of the millennials to switch up from my beloved skinny jeans into something more "trendy" and I honestly spent hours and went from store to store to store in malls and shopping centers to find things, only to be disappointed by the fit, color or the sizing. Amazon was my last effort so I went on a hunt and found these. They are trendy, cozy, and I feel extremely fashionable and relaxed in them all at the same time. I am 5'5" and weigh 121, I'm a Levi's 6/28 and with more of a booty than anything else, so finding pants that fit me well is always hard. Not these! I got a size 06. They're perfect for anything! I can wear a belt or go without, I can do heels or sandals, dress them up or down for any occasion. I'm definitely a fan! The only downside is that they REEK of leather. I washed them with an extra rise on heavy cycle and it still smells heavily of leather. My legs even smell like leather after wearing them. I'm about to throw them into the washer for the second time since Saturday (today is Wednesday) in an attempt to at least dial down the aroma. We'll see how it goes. Regardless of the smell, they're a fabulous pair of pants and definitely worth it. If you're like me and have been looking for something to switch up your style to fit in a bit more without compromising comfort, give them a try!

I’m really happy with this belt. For the price point, it’s a great quality. I’m really impressed - and did not expect to be. Jeans are jeans. But these are maybe one of three pair of jeans in my lifetime that are not too long! I am 5’1” - so this is a rarity. I bought a size 14. Also, I had not noticed in the description that spandex is one of the materials - this doesn’t mean at all that they are tight, but rather they have very nice give in the tighter areas (i.e. waist). They are indeed frayed - so this is a worry as they might continue to fray. I have some Stop Fray that might protect the hem. The description also states they are straight-legged. True, but very wide, as wide as some bell bottoms. One more thing - unfortunately the jeans have a strong odor, almost like gasoline. Most of the smell came out in the first wash; I’m certainly hoping it will dissipate altogether in the next wash. See picture. Nice jeans. I really like them.

I’m really tired of the high waisted fashion trend. Low rise is a cut, and fits certain body types better. I don’t like pants covering my belly button, it’s uncomfortable and HOT. I’m 5’4” and ~117lbs. I ordered a small. For reference, another pair of size small workout pants I bought in 2016 (when “low rise” was in fashion) *facepalm* Why not make pants for alllll bodies?? I’ve ordered six pairs of new pants all claiming to be low rise, not a one of them even comes close to pants made 5-6 years ago.

I'm really torn on these jeans. While they look cute on my butt, I found them to be pretty uncomfortable and don't love the price. The button fly paired with the stiffer denim, plus the fact that these were probably a size too small on me, made them kind of miserable to wear. For reference, I'm currently anywhere from an 8 to a 12 in jeans, depending on the brand/fit/material, and my measurements are currently 44"-32"-44". I'm 5'3" with a larger butt and thighs, with a short torso and longer legs. I usually buy regular length pants because short tends to be just a tad too short for my taste, but still too long to be that perfect ankle length... Hopefully that makes sense. I got these in a SIZE 30 in the color TANGO LIGHT. As you can see in the photos, the color is lovely but they're a bit tight on me. I suspected I may need a 31 but hoped they would relax a little after a few hours of wear and become the perfect snug fit in the 30. While that may be the case, I honestly don't know if I like them enough to be miserable for the hours it'll take to make that happen. I will say that the Premium material is a bit softer than the other stiffer Levi's jeans, like the standard Wedgie Skinnies. They still aren't soft though, so if that's what you're looking for, I would say these aren't for you. I'd recommend these for someone looking for a sturdier denim with little to no give. I'm sure they will last a long time. I ended up returning these and am unsure if I'll ever bother to try a 31. I've decided I'm not really a lover of the button fly. Oh well.

I'm sharing because I find detailed reviews extremely if they contain details beyond "I love this." I hope this is to someone else shopping. _ I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs (sometimes 158 lbs.). My shape is hourglass, meaning I have hips (40") and a smaller waist (33") I am over 50, so I have done some shopping in my life. My US size is 10-12 at Macy's and 12 at Target. _I like clothes that do not bind, loose fit, but do not look like I am wearing an old canvas bag. _I like good fabric, and I am a longtime sewer, so craftsmanship of clothes is important to me. I look at the construction of a garment. So, these pants: _Linen fabric is quality and a mid-weight, no shine and has a great but subtle linen texture - the fabric is soft to the skin and does not scratch or itch. Good for three seasons in the north of the USA. The drape is excellent right out of the bag without ironing or putting them in the dryer for a touch-up. The elastic at the waist is about 1-1/4 inch wide and not stiff - the elastic waist could easily accommodate a waist 8 inches bigger than mine or more - it depends on the fit you like - I actually might make the elastic a little tighter, an easy 10 minute fix if you sew or know someone who does sew. The pants have two good sized pockets. The construction is excellent. The fabric is cut correctly so it drapes correctly. _Depending on your lifestyle and personal style these pants can be worn almost anywhere besides a formal event. How you style with a top, shoes and accessories is the key. Definitely outside pants, not sleeping pants. _When shopping online make sure you get a tape measure and know your measurements. I ordered US L and the fit was as expected for me - I could have sized down one size to a US Medium, but I like a loose fit. Also, make sure you look at the manufacturer's size chart, not the generic Amazon size chart to make your size selection. _My photos are of the pants right out of the bag, and in front of a dusty (sorry) mirror, but hopefully they are to you. _I have been wearing them for a few hours now and these pants are so comfortable, so cute. If this is your style, your silhouette, I can completely recommend these pants. IXIMO Women's Casual Cotton Linen Baggy Pants with Elastic Waist

I’m so bummed I have to return these jeans because I LOVE them. For reference, I am 5’7, 140 pounds with a long torso. I'm sorry, my photos are not great. I'm just posting what I have in my personal gallery, not intended for review. I loved these pants so much though that I ordered both other colors! I’m still trying to decide how I feel about these pants. The 12s were too big, so I purchased the 10s this time. For the most part, they fit well, though a bit snug in the waist (I do carry most weight in my stomach). They have enough stretch to be comfortable though. The back pockets are big, which would be fine (since I keep my phone in my butt pocket), except they’re big enough that it goes below the bottom of my butt and creates an odd look. I’m certainly not bootylicious, but these pants definitely make my booty look flatter than it is. If you have any chub in your stomach, these pants don’t look great with a tight/fitted shirt - you’ll want to pair with something looser. I have examples of two different shirts of reference. I love doing a French tuck with my tops (tucking the front into my pants) - it looks fine from the front, but from the side, the pants definitely accentuate my stomach pooch. Since measurements are when deciding to buy clothes online, mine are 40-33.5-39, 168 lbs. As you can see from the pictures, they’re definitely a longer pant - I’m just shy of 5’6” and they go just below my ankle bone. Super comfy, but I’m not sure yet if I’ll keep them, since I want to feel confident in whatever I’m wearing for work and these definitely accentuate some flaws and are a bit hard to match tops with.

I’m surprised by the quality of these belts. They are well made for the price point, and the simplicity of the buckle elevates any outfit. The belts were packaged with care. Each belt had a sleeve and the buckles had protective tape on them. They also came in a nice box. I got the large size and I am 170 lbs and carry weight around my belly. When I use the belt on my pants, I could button it on the 3rd to last notch. When I use it for an accent piece with my dresses, I can get it to the last notch because it sits higher on my waist. I could go for a smaller size, but I don’t like the look of the extra holes showing on the belt. With the larger size, most of the notches are hidden and makes the belt look more tailored and streamlined. The extra length of the belt that goes around the waist underneath doesn’t give much bulk and is still comfortable. So it’s up to personal preference whether you want to go with size or look. Both the brown and black are great colors to work with any outfit. Whether to pull the look together or to break up sections. I’d purchase it again in a different color.

I'm terrible at taking pics. But I got some of the pants, the length, and with an entire outfit. I hope they help. For reference, I am 5'0" and a out 130lbs (changes between 128-133 often, because well... food) anyway..... If you have any type of belly weight, I would recommend sizing up. My belly weight is just skin from losing a lot of weight (I was 165 last year, but being this short created a lot of extra skin in my fupa area). If you have extreme belly weight, these can still work for you. Just size up and wear a longer shirt. The pants are lovely. They have a great stretch and they help tuck in and hide a few problem areas for me. The length is perfect. I got a size small with 27" inseam. I have these in black and khaki now. I will absolutely be buying a few more in different colors and may go up a size on a few for a more relaxed fit. (Ya know.. some days bloating takes over and I just want it to hang a little more loose). If you're on the fence, I would recommend taking a leap of faith. You can dress them up and down. I mean, I wouldn't exactly wear them to a yoga class, but they could certainly handle the job if you wanted to try. I'm wearing them at work right now. Hence the bathroom pics b3cause I don't have full length mirrors at home.

I'm typically an 8 or a 10 because my thighs are somewhat curvy, and read all the reviews and decided on a 30 and two 31s. I'm so sick of stretch in jeans and cropped lengths so was hopeful. Unlike others, I didn't find these to run that crazy small. They are made for a semi-curvy pear shape. My waist is about 30" and my thighs 41". Black 31's were not bad in the waist but big in the thighs, the dark wash 31s were too big, the dark wash 30's I'm keeping. If you have a similar proportion and are sick of waist gaps these are worth considering if the price is right.The denim material itself seems fine but the stitching is not that well done and the thread quality looks poor. I can update when I wear them but I'd thrift actual 90s jeans vs paying over $40 for these. They are not quality wise as good as ones from the 90s but where these differ is they are slimmer in the leg than most 90s jeans and are higher waisted than a number of them as well. I'm tired of companies today calling an 11" rise high. Not for a long torso. These are 13" on the 30's and 31's and actually hit the smallest part of my waist which is the whole point of a high rise!The bummer is they are so long. I wish they looked on me like some of the other reviewers. I'm 5'8" so I'm not short but I did have to roll them twice like the stock photo and it was bulky. Perhaps smaller sizes are shorter or they shrink in the wash? All the ones I bought had an inseam around 33.5". The amount of bulk is going to look bad if you're under 5'6". I might consider hemming them as my preference is not cuffing at all to keep them looking sleek.The black ones in the photo are size 31 in case it helps you on sizing. I feel like they measure exactly what their stated size is when pulled to their max - I.E. the 31's are 15.5" flat and closer to 15" relaxed.

I live in New England and it gets cold, my legs get cold so easily and I usually wear tights under my jean but I won't need them with these. These are not cheap flimsy jeans these are nice and thick made really well. I ordered the Light Blue and will be buying the Dark Blue so I'll have a couple of really warm pairs of jeans. I’m usually a size 12, but ordered these in a 16 hoping they would be baggy (and long enough as I’m 5’11) and they are crazy comfortable. I’m very disappointed in the color. I ordered the purple and instead I got a pinky/lavender color. I’ve provided a side by side comparison of the product image and the actual color. I'm very disappointed in these pants, as they are a cute design and looked like they'd be comfortable. But I can't really wear them after washing them. For reference, I am 5'7" and 133#. I ordered size Small. My photos were taken BEFORE I washed them... 1) There are many loose threads hanging off these pants, due to low quality control. 2) The elastic waist is very tight. Too tight for a supposedly comfy pajama/lounge pant, especially one worn mid-rise. I think buyers should size-UP. 3) These pants are 100% Cotton, and definitely shrank after the first wash in cold water and tumble dry on Low heat. Now they feel even smaller and are too short for me. In general, I recommend that buyers SIZE-UP by 1-2 sizes, and either hang dry or dry on low heat for very short time. Too bad the quality is low, because the navy/white gingham design and "boxer" look is cute.

I've been looking for a pair of bright white pull-on, straight leg pants for a while now. I thought I'd found them with these Slim-Sation pants. Pros: nice bright white color, perfect length, perfect fit in the legs. However... the back of the waistband gaps terribly!!! Please see photos taken from back and side. (Ignore the butt, please.) I have to continuously keep pulling them up in the back. I am 5'6", 120 lbs. and purchased size 4. Everything fits well, except for the way the back portion of the waistband is designed. There is a separate section below the waistband that just gaps out very weirdly. Unfortunately, I've worn these pants, so cannot return them. But, I am so disappointed that after paying $67 (with tax) for a pair of pants, this is the sad look that I'm stuck with. Oh well... keep looking. I plan to send this same message to the seller and hopefully I'll get an explanation as to the waistband design. Hope this review helps others.

I’ve been looking for a pair of white jeans for awhile, so went for these - my 1st pair of trendy wide leg pants. I did wash them before wearing because there were deep fold lines + I wanted to see if they shrank because of the 75% cotton (they did a little bit in the length). WHAT I LIKE: == very comfortable! The stretch of the fabric softens them. == the button fly is flattering & flattening. == the length ends at my ankle bones, so your shoes are featured. WHAT I DON’T LIKE: == the wide legs need ironing in the opposite direction, if that makes sense. They flare out oddly at the very bottom. MY SIZE/THESE PANTS: I’m 5’8”, 108 lbs, & wear size 2/4. I ordered these in size 4 & might have needed a 2 instead. They’re plenty roomy & the 23% polyester/2% spandex makes them stretch out as you wear them, so they get a bit looser throughout the day/evening. Bottom line? They’re a really well made pair of denim pants in a trendy style. Pay attention to the size chart & order carefully. **Thank you for reading this review & I hope that it’s !** 😊

I've been looking for pants like these for quite awhile. I wanted lightweight pull-on pants with pockets big enough for a cell phone that I can wear during the summer months. For me, the hardest part was determining the right size. The online size chart indicated that I should wear a size 18P, which is larger than anything I own. Nevertheless, I bought a pair and they turned out to be too big. I knew as soon as I tried them on, though, that I wanted one in every color. So, I ordered two more pairs in both a 16P and a 14P. The 16P turned out to be the best fit, even though it is still a little loose; but I definitely didn't want pants that were too tight. These are good every-day pants for a retired woman in her 70's. I am 5'2" and weigh about 160. I had no problems with making multiple returns.

I’ve been looking for something other than leggings to wear around the house, so I decided to give these a try. I’m 5’6”, 190 pounds, size 12/14 in pants. According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart, I fit in their size large which is what I ordered. I’ve worn them around for a few days, here are my thoughts.What I liked.• The fabric is soft and drapes nicely.• The fabric doesn’t get stretched out and baggy.• They are machine wash cold, tumble dry low. I laundered mine that way and they came out great.• The pants are solidly constructed and designed well. The manufacturer went the extra mile to add a yoke on the back of the pants which creates a more flattering fit.• They have 2 on-seam pockets that are big and deep. I could securely carry my iPhone 15 Pro Max in them.• They are comfortable, mostly.• They fit, mostly. I liked the 29” inseam, which hit me below the ankle. For hanging around the house, the length was good. I wasn’t stepping all over the hem, especially going up and down the stairs.What I didn’t like.• The fabric is a little odd. Yes, it’s soft, but it is more of a heavy knit.• The fabric is hot. Because of the loose, wide leg I thought they’d be nice and cool during our heatwave. They were not.• The pants were not a great fit through the hips on me. They were just a tiny bit snug. As a result, I preferred the look of a long, oversized t-shirt over them. They also seemed a little short in the rise (crotch).Overall, I think these sweatpants fall squarely in the “they’re OK” category. I wasn’t crazy about the feel and weight of the fabric, or the fit. However, they are well made and do launder great. I’m giving them 4 stars.

I’ve been looking for something other than leggings to wear around the house, so I decided to give these a try. I’m 5’6”, 190 pounds, size 12/14 in pants. According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart, I fit in their size large which is what I ordered. I’ve worn them around for a few days, here are my thoughts.What I liked.• The fabric is soft and drapes nicely.• The fabric doesn’t get stretched out and baggy.• They are machine wash cold, tumble dry low. I laundered mine that way and they came out great.• The pants are solidly constructed and designed well. The manufacturer went the extra mile to add a yoke on the back of the pants which creates a more flattering fit.• They have 2 on-seam pockets that are big and deep. I could securely carry my iPhone 15 Pro Max in them.• They are comfortable, mostly.• They fit, mostly. I liked the 29” inseam, which hit me below the ankle. For hanging around the house, the length was good. I wasn’t stepping all over the hem, especially going up and down the stairs.What I didn’t like.• The fabric is a little odd. Yes, it’s soft, but it is more of a heavy knit.• The fabric is hot. Because of the loose, wide leg I thought they’d be nice and cool during our heatwave. They were not.• The pants were not a great fit through the hips on me. They were just a tiny bit snug. As a result, I preferred the look of a long, oversized t-shirt over them. They also seemed a little short in the rise (crotch).Overall, I think these sweatpants fall squarely in the “they’re OK” category. I wasn’t crazy about the feel and weight of the fabric, or the fit. However, they are well made and do launder great. I’m giving them 4 stars.

I’ve been looking for the perfect work pants. I’ve even searched the same material & percentages to match my favorite ransoms I found at Ross. Came across these & ordered 2 sizes. The 12 Short were perfect!!! For reference I am 5’4 and 170lbs. My waist is a bit smaller but these thighs lol. These fit perfectly and don’t leave that weird look in the front or drag on the bottoms.

I’ve been looking for wide leg jeans and I liked how these looked on some of the ladies in the reviews and decided to take a chance but once I got them and tried them on they were an immediate no for me. I wanted to make it work because they were very different than all of my jeans and I wanted to get out of my comfort zone but the two things that I always look for is: how my butt looks and the length. Both a big NO on me. I’m 5’4” 130lbs with wide hips. The hips were fitted and had no stretch. My butt looked so flat and the length was a little too short for my liking. As far as thickness and quality, the material was a thicker jean with no give on the hold but the legs were comfortable (obviously with the width lol) and it appeared to be good quality.

I’ve been looking forever for a high-quality denim jacket with just the right amount of authentic fade but at an affordable price. I should have known to check out Levi’s first! This jacket is soft yet structured. The denim has a little personality and fade but still goes with everything. The fit is right on point. Seriously it doesn’t get much better than this! I opted for a M in the Jeanie wash and LOVE it. I’m 5’3”, 130lbs with muscular shoulders which usually prevent me from wearing a small. The medium is just the right fit without looking bulky. Jacket runs true to size.
I’ve been loving the flare jeans on my body lately and Levi’s never disappoints! I’m about 5’2” and 144lbs for reference.

I've been on the quest to find the perfect sweatpants, and these are as close as I've found so far. I'm 5'10", and the 34" pants were long enough! These are soft, comfy, I love that they're high waisted, and they make my small booty pop. However, these pants are super snaggy. My cat got on my lap for just a minute and snagged my pants in multiple places. So cat owners beware.
